urn:contentItem:6JGF-YBS3-S2WN-K0S6-00000-00PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP - INDUSTRY UPDATES AND PEER TRENDS2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:02ZLexisNexisSTOCKPILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP - INDUSTRY UPDATES AND PEER TRENDS

DASHBOARD: PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP

PermID4296383614Websitewww.pillsburylaw.com/en/IndustryFull-Service BigLawAddress31 West 52nd Street, New York, NY 10019 USA

ACTIVITIES:

Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP, founded in 1868, is a global law firm headquartered in New York City. With over 700 lawyers across 20 offices, it serves clients in energy, technology, and finance, specializing in M&A, regulatory, and litigation. Generating over $700 million annually, Pillsbury advised on 2024 renewable energy transactions, recognized by Columbia Law School. Its culture emphasizes innovation, diversity, and pro bono work, with strong associate training programs. Competing with Baker McKenzie, Pillsbury stands out for its energy and tech regulatory expertise. In 2024, it expanded its AI and ESG practices, focusing on sustainable finance. Recent initiatives include cloud-based legal tech and partnerships with nonprofits like Lawyers Alliance for New York, reinforcing its community impact.

+ PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP [3 Press Releases]

+ PILLSBURY ADVISES BLUEPRINT EQUITY ON K1 INVESTMENT MANAGEMENTS GROWTH INVESTMENT IN SPIN.AI

Pillsbury advised Blueprint Equity, a tech-focused growth equity firm focused on investing in and building market-leading enterprise businesses, in connection with small-cap AI-powered software investor K1 Investment Management, LLC's investment in Spin.AI.

Source: Company Website

+ PILLSBURY ADVISES SCHEMATIC ON $6.5 MILLION SEED FUNDING ROUND

Pillsbury advised Schematic, a Boulder, Colorado-based provider of a runtime monetization platform for SaaS and AI companies, in connection with its $6.5 million seed funding round.

Source: Company Website

+ PILLSBURY EXPANDS ITS MARKET-LEADING EMERGING COMPANIES & VENTURE CAPITAL PRACTICE WITH ADDITION OF PARTNERS MIKE FLYNN AND SAARAH WOODBY IN NEW YORK

Pillsbury's premier Emerging Companies & Venture Capital (ECVC) practice-ranked by LSEG among the Top 4 most active for VC financings and investor representation globally-has welcomed partners Mike Flynn and Saarah Woodby to its New York-based team. Flynn, who has previously served as a Pillsbury partner, and Woodby both join from McDermott Will & Schulte.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ PERKINS COIE [3 Press Releases]

+ PERKINS COIE REPRESENTS SEDRON IN INVESTMENT FROM ARA PARTNERS OF UP TO $500 MILLION

PORTLAND, OR (April 15, 2026)-Perkins Coie announced today that it represented Sedron Technologies, a leader in advanced upcycling systems that transform waste streams into reusable water and fertilizer products, in an investment of up to $500 million from Ara Partners, a global private equity and infrastructure firm focused on industrial decarbonization.

Source: Company Website

+ PERKINS COIE STRENGTHENS PACIFIC NORTHWEST TRIAL BENCH WITH COMMERCIAL LITIGATOR CHAD COLTON

PORTLAND, OR and SEATTLE, WA (April 6, 2026)-Perkins Coie is pleased to announce that Chad Colton has joined its Commercial Litigation practice as a partner in Portland and Seattle, further strengthening the firm's trial capabilities in the Pacific Northwest.

Bringing two decades of experience, Chad represents clients in high-stakes commercial matters such as partnership, shareholder, breach of contract, IP, and licensing disputes. He also frequently serves as lead trial counsel for bet-the-company cases, often joining to advise and advocate for clients in the middle of litigation or immediately before trial.

Source: Company Website

+ PERKINS COIE RANKS 44TH ON FORTUNE'S "100 BEST COMPANIES TO WORK FOR®" LIST FOR 2026

SEATTLE (April 1, 2026)-Perkins Coie is proud to announce that it has again been named to Fortune's "100 Best Companies to Work For®" list for 2026, marking the 24th consecutive year that the firm has been featured on Fortune's prestigious list.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ COOLEY LLP [4 Press Releases]

+ COOLEY LLP VETHOS ANNOUNCES $200 MILLION IPO

Cooley advised Ethos Technologies, a top life insurance technology company on a mission to democratize access to life insurance, on its $200 million initial public offering (IPO). Ethos offered 5,127,696 shares of Class A common stock, and selling stockholders offered an additional 5,398,619 shares of Class A common stock, at a price of $19 per share. Ethos began trading on the Nasdaq Global Select Market on January 29, 2026, under the ticker symbol LIFE.

Partner Matthew Bartus led the Cooley team advising Ethos since its formation in 2016, including on its $100 million Series D-1 in July 2021 and $200 million Series D in May 2021.

Lawyers Jon Avina, Jean Park, Milson Yu, Samara Zaifman, Katie Lapidus and Charlotte Yin led the Cooley team advising Ethos on its IPO.

Source: Company Website

+ COOLEY LLP INFERACT ANNOUNCES $150 MILLION SEED FINANCING

Cooley advised Inferact, a startup founded by creators and core maintainers of the open-source project vLLM, on its $150 million seed financing at an $800 million valuation. The financing was co-led by Andreessen Horowitz and Lightspeed Venture Partners, with investments from Sequoia Capital, Altimeter Capital, Redpoint Ventures, ZhenFund, The House Fund, Striker Venture Partners, Laude Ventures, Databricks Ventures and the UC Berkeley Chancellor's Fund.

Lawyers Peter Werner, Tara Capsuto, Won Lee, Carl Min and Isabella Ysebrands led the Cooley team advising Inferact.

Source: Company Website

+ COOLEY LLP NEUROPHOS SECURES $110 MILLION SERIES A

Cooley advised Neurophos, a leader in photonic artificial intelligence chip technology, on its oversubscribed $110 million Series A round, bringing total funding to $118 million. The new funding will accelerate the delivery of Neurophos' first integrated photonic compute system, including datacenter-ready optical processing unit modules, a full software stack and early access developer hardware.

The round was led by Gates Frontier, with participation from M12 (Microsoft's venture fund), Carbon Direct Capital, Aramco Ventures, Bosch Ventures, Tectonic Ventures, Space Capital and others.

Lawyers James Allen, Nick Hobson, James Reizman and Nathan Alamillo led the Cooley team advising Neurophos.

Source: Company Website

+ COOLEY LLP OPENEVIDENCE RAISES $250 MILLION SERIES D

Cooley advised OpenEvidence, the most widely used artificial intelligence (AI) platform by doctors in America, on its $250 million Series D financing, valuing the company at $12 billion and making it the most valuable healthcare AI company in the world. The new funding will be used to invest heavily in the research and development and compute costs associated with the multi-AI agentic architecture of OpenEvidence, which provides the highest quality and most accurate medical answers of any system in the world.

The round was co-led by Thrive Capital and DST Global, and brings the total funding raised by OpenEvidence to nearly $700 million over the past year.

Lawyers Derek Colla, David Safren and Sela Dougherty led the Cooley team advising OpenEvidence.

Source: Company Website

+ K&L GATES [3 Press Releases]

+ TWO K&L GATES PARTNERS AGAIN NAMED TO INDIA BUSINESS LAW JOURNALS INTERNATIONAL A-LIST 2026

K&L Gates is pleased to announce that Singapore Office Managing Partner Raja Bose and Singapore Corporate partner Meraj Noor have once again been named to India Business Law Journal's (IBLJ) International A-List. This marks the eighth consecutive year both partners have received this recognition.

IBLJ's International A-List identifies the top lawyers based outside of India who are recognized for their work on India-related matters. The A-List was compiled by IBLJ's seasoned editorial team following extensive market research, drawing on feedback from hundreds of lawyers at Indian law firms and thousands of India-focused in-house counsel within India and abroad.

The full International A-List 2026 is featured on IBLJ's website.

**All Singapore lawyers are members of K&L Gates Straits Law LLC, the Singapore office of global law firm K&L Gates. Source: Company Website

+ MORE THAN 80 K&L GATES LAWYERS ACROSS ASIA-PACIFIC RECOGNISED IN 2027 BEST LAWYERS, ONES TO WATCH EDITIONS

Best Lawyers®, a peer-review publication in the legal profession, has named more than 80 K&L Gates lawyers across the Asia-Pacific region in the 2027 editions of its guides, covering Australia, Japan, and Singapore. In addition, nearly 20 of the firm's lawyers were named as Best Lawyers: Ones to Watch® in Australia and Japan, lists that recognise lawyers early in their careers.

Alongside these recognitions, four Australian partners were recognised with "Lawyer of the Year" awards, an accolade that is particularly noteworthy, as it is only bestowed on one standout lawyer for each practice area in each jurisdiction.

The Best Lawyers® guides highlight top legal talent, selected by peers within the same geographical region and legal practice area, through millions of votes each year. Congratulations to the Lawyer of the Year award recipients below, as well as all lawyers recognised in the 2027 editions - resulting in nearly 180 total recognitions for the firm. Source: Company Website

+ K&L GATES ADVISES DOLPHIN ENTERTAINMENT, INC. ON US$15 MILLION PIPE INVESTMENT

Global law firm K&L Gates LLP advised Dolphin Entertainment, Inc., an entertainment marketing and premium content production company, in connection with a US$15 million private investment in public equity (PIPE) financing with Lincoln Park Capital, an investment group and asset management firm.

The Corporate team advising on the transaction was led by Miami partner Clayton Parker with support from Miami partner Erin Fogarty.

"This transaction underscores Dolphin Entertainment's commitment to accelerating strategic growth across its marketing and content-driven businesses. We were pleased to support them in securing investment from a partner aligned with its long-term vision," said Parker.

K&L Gates' Corporate practice is one of the most substantial in the legal industry, with hundreds of lawyers in offices across the globe providing clients with practical legal solutions in the structuring, financing, and completion of domestic, international, and cross-border transactions. Source: Company Website

+ REED SMITH LLP [4 Press Releases]

+ REED SMITH ADVISES NATURPAK ON SALE BY TRIVEST TO PPC

CHICAGO - Global law firm Reed Smith represented Trivest Partners and IPMF, LLC, doing business as NaturPak, a manufacturer of bone broths, soups, sauces, wet pet food, and food ingredients for national retailers and brands, on its sale by Trivest Growth Investment Fund II to Pritzker Private Capital ("PPC"). Trivest Growth Investment Fund II, managed by Trivest Partners LP, a private equity firm focused exclusively on investments in founder- and family owned businesses, partnered with NaturPak through a minority investment in 2020. During Trivest's partnership, NaturPak transformed from a small specialty co-manufacturer into a scaled, multi-segment platform, increasing its R&D and manufacturing capabilities and production capacity.

The cross-office Reed Smith deal team included attorneys in Chicago, New York, Denver, Philadelphia, Dallas, Orange County (California), Pittsburgh, Washington, Houston, Tysons (Virginia), and Miami. The team was led by Nick Gibson, Kristen Capriotti, Crockett Stevenson, and Sudeep Kalkunte, with support from Constantine Karides, Brad Schmarak, David Hayes, Simon Kliegman, Ian Sherwin, Paulo McKeeby, Daniel Hagedorn, Michelle Mantine, Cori Goldberg, A.J. Wissinger, Jillian Burstein, Tyler Thompson, Mike Summers, Michael Jacobs, Brendan Leanos, Benjamin Brimeyer, Michael Galibois, Michael Lowell, Joseph Mammone Jr., Michelle DiPuma, Batool Banker, Leah Hungerman, Taber Rueter, Clirae Bourke, Michaela Mapes, Peter Loy, Talia Boiangin Behar, Elliott Race, George Avdellas, Katherine Litaker, Taylor Kollmansberger, and Nikita Bazaj.

Source: Company Website

+ REED SMITH PARTNERS JOSH LOVE, ERIC MARDER, STEPHEN SESSA, AND EDWARD SHAPIRO NAMED TO VARIETY'S "DEALMAKERS 2025" REPORT

LOS ANGELES - Global law firm Reed Smith announced today that Josh Love, Eric Marder, Stephen Sessa, and Edward Shapiro, partners in the firm's global Entertainment & Media Industry Group, have been named to Variety's "Dealmakers 2025" report. Love, Marder, Sessa, and Shapiro are among an elite group of entertainment attorneys who have been chosen based on their more than $3 billion in music transactions in 2025, high‑profile client roster, and leadership across the music industry. The publication highlights their work on "sports and music transactions, start-ups and brands," "recording, music publishing, and live performance agreements (including Bad Bunny's upcoming Super Bowl halftime show)," and "catalog acquisitions."

This recognition reflects Reed Smith's continued leadership in high-stakes entertainment and music transactions, talent representation, and emerging legal issues at the intersection of entertainment, music, technology, and finance. The four partners were selected for their high-profile talent negotiations and representation business.

Source: Company Website

+ REED SMITH ADVISES LEON CAPITAL ON STRATEGIC INVESTMENT IN AVANTIS GROUP

LONDON - Global law firm Reed Smith has advised funds advised by Leon Capital on the strategic investment in Avantis Group, a UK-based provider of specialist engineering services to the maritime and energy industries. The transaction, which represents the latest new investment by Leon Capital, completed on 23 December 2025. The terms of the investment are confidential.

The transaction involved the Development Bank of Wales and Avantis Group's founders, Chris David and Tom David. Avantis Group's existing management team will retain operational control of the business. Blake Morgan acted as legal counsel to the Development Bank of Wales, while Acuity Law advised the founders. AMA Capital Partners acted as corporate finance adviser to Avantis Group.

Reed Smith advised Leon Capital on all aspects of the transaction, providing comprehensive M&A support in connection with the strategic investment. The matter involved a multi-disciplinary team spanning M&A, tax, and regulatory considerations.

The investment supports Avantis Group's long-term growth strategy across maritime and energy markets, including expansion into digital infrastructure and defence, and reflects Leon Capital's confidence in the company's differentiated engineering platform and experienced management team.

For Reed Smith, the transaction represents a further example of the firm's work advising private capital sponsors on complex M&A transactions and continues to strengthen its relationship with Leon Capital as it expands its platform.

The Reed Smith team advising on the matter was led by Andrew Houghton, with support from Ciaran Sweeney, Georgia Moorhouse and Selina Cook on M&A matters; Caspar Fox and Tom Baxter on tax; and Emma Weeden on FDI and antitrust.

Source: Company Website

+ REED SMITH STRENGTHENS ASIA-PACIFIC FINANCE AND TRANSPORTATION CAPABILITY WITH HIRE IN SINGAPORE

SINGAPORE - Global law firm Reed Smith announced the appointment of banking and finance partner Dr. Gregory Xu to its Singapore office. Xu, who joins from Stephenson Harwood, where he led the general banking and project finance offering in Southeast Asia and served on the firm's Supervisory Council, its executive committee, will join Reed Smith's Transportation Industry Group.

With over 15 years of experience, Xu advises on high-value, cross-border transactions across a wide range of sectors, including transportation - shipping, aviation, and logistics - trade, life sciences, energy, and technology. He regularly acts for financial institutions, funds, export credit agencies, leasing houses, asset owners, and Fortune 500 companies on complex financing, restructuring, and enforcement matters.

Xu has a strong track record in green and sustainability-linked lending and advises on multi-jurisdictional insolvency-related enforcement and restructuring matters. He also brings extensive experience in general corporate and commercial matters.

Richard Hakes, chair of Reed Smith's Transportation Industry Group, said: "Gregory is an outstanding addition to our team, bringing deep knowledge of ship finance, aviation finance, and banking throughout Southeast Asia. His arrival further strengthens our capabilities in Singapore and reinforces our commitment to the maritime, aviation, and broader transportation sectors across the Asia-Pacific region and beyond. Working closely with our shipping teams in Hong Kong and Shanghai and our aviation team in Singapore as part of our global Transportation Industry Group, Gregory will enhance our ability to provide seamless, cross-border support to clients operating in one of the world's most dynamic industries."

Xu commented: "Reed Smith's global platform, sector focus, and collaborative culture are hugely impressive. The firm's market-leading finance practice and its long-standing reputation in general banking, asset finance, and project finance were major draws for me. I'm delighted to be joining such a highly collegiate team with a clear strategy for growth in the region and look forward to contributing to the continued expansion of the firm's finance offering across Asia-Pacific."

Praj Samant, Reed Smith's Asia-Pacific managing partner, said: "We are delighted to welcome Gregory to the Singapore team. His arrival further strengthens our offering in one of the region's most strategically important sectors. We look forward to the valuable contributions he will bring to the firm locally and globally."

Xu is recognized as a leading practitioner by multiple legal directories. Chambers Asia-Pacific ranks him as a "Leading Individual" for Banking & Finance (India), while The Legal 500 identifies him as a "Leading Partner," "Next Generation Partner," and "Recommended" lawyer across Banking & Finance, Asset Finance, Projects, and Shipping in Singapore and in the foreign juris

+ GREENBERG TRAURIG, LLP [3 Press Releases]

+ GREENBERG TRAURIG ADDS ALEXANDRA CAMPBELL-DIEHL AS LABOR & EMPLOYMENT ASSOCIATE IN BOSTON

BOSTON - April 22, 2026 - Global law firm Greenberg Traurig, LLP has added Alexandra Campbell-Diehl to its Boston office as an associate in the firm's Labor & Employment Practice.

Campbell-Diehl advises employers on matters including single-plaintiff and class action employment litigation related to wrongful termination, harassment, discrimination, retaliation, and more, as well as workplace investigations and corporate transactions. Her work focuses on traditional labor law issues, including employer rights and obligations related to unions, and advising on union organizing, collective bargaining, and labor law developments. She has defended employers in attorney general investigations, state and federal litigation, and proceedings before the National Labor Relations Board, Department of Labor, Equal Employment Opportunity Commission, and the Massachusetts Commission Against Discrimination.

Campbell-Diehl earned her J.D. from Boston College Law School and a B.S. from Cornell University.

"We are pleased to welcome Alex to the Boston Labor & Employment team," Terence P. McCourt and David J. Dykeman, co-managing shareholders of Greenberg Traurig's Boston office, said in a joint statement. "She brings a strong foundation in both employment law and traditional labor law, with experience advising employers on various employment matters in the workplace. Alex's addition enhances our capacity to support clients facing an increasingly dynamic and regulated employment landscape."

Source: Company Website

+ GOODWIN PROCTER LLP FLOW NEUROSCIENCE ANNOUNCES FDA APPROVAL OF WORLDS FIRST AT-HOME BRAIN STIMULATION TREATMENT FOR DEPRESSION

Goodwin congratulates Flow Neuroscience on receiving FDA approval of their breakthrough at-home brain-stimulation device for the treatment of major depressive disorder (MDD). FDA approval marks the first time clinicians in the US are able to prescribe an at-home non-drug therapy as both a stand-alone and an adjunctive treatment for adults with moderate to severe depression. Goodwin's Technology and Life Sciences teams were thrilled to assist Flow Neuroscience in bringing this breakthrough device to patients in the US

urn:contentItem:6JG9-SPG3-RSND-R481-00000-00MILBANK LLP - RECENT NEWS OF DEBEVOISE & PLIMPTON LLP AND PEERS2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:02ZLexisNexisSTOCKMILBANK LLP - RECENT NEWS OF DEBEVOISE & PLIMPTON LLP AND PEERS

DASHBOARD: MILBANK LLP

PermID4297973195Websitehttps://www.milbank.com/IndustryFull-Service BigLawAddress55 Hudson Yards, New York, NY 10001-2163

ACTIVITIES:

Milbank LLP, founded in 1866, is a global law firm headquartered in New York City. With over 800 lawyers across 12 offices, it serves clients in finance, energy, and infrastructure, specializing in project finance, M&A, and litigation. Generating over $1 billion annually, Milbank advised on major 2024 energy deals, per its recognition by Columbia Law School. Its culture fosters collaboration, diversity, and pro bono work, with 247 attorneys registered in New York. Competing with Sullivan & Cromwell, Milbank stands out for its finance expertise, with attorneys like John D. Franchini noted for excellence. In 2024, it expanded its renewable energy and AI practices. Recent initiatives include digital transaction platforms and ESG-focused financing, aligning with market demands.

+ MILBANK LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ MILBANK'S STRATEGIC PLAN TO COMBAT INVASIVE BEETLE THREATENS ASH TREES

MILBANK — In a proactive response to an invasive species threat, city leaders in Milbank are implementing a comprehensive strategy to protect their ash trees from the emerald ash borer. The infestation, first detected last summer, poses a significant risk to the northeastern South Dakota city’s tree canopy, which is composed of over 50% ash trees, including those found in local parks and the cemetery. On 21st April 2026, city administrator Steve Pendergrass outlined the financial implications of the crisis, indicating that the cost of removing the trees could reach up to $4 million.Conversely, Milbank's strategy to develop what it describes as 'herd immunity' against the beetle will require an estimated $2.4 million over the next 12 years. This approach aims to enhance the resilience of the ash trees through targeted treatment and monitoring, allowing the city to preserve its green spaces while combating the invasive pest. The strategy is seen as a viable model that could be adapted by other cities in South Dakota facing similar threats to their tree populations.The emerald ash borer, a woodboring beetle, has wreaked havoc across various regions of the United States, leading to widespread tree loss. Milbank's initiative to promote herd immunity reflects an understanding of the ecological importance of maintaining a robust tree canopy, which provides numerous environmental benefits including improved air quality, water retention, and habitat for wildlife. By investing in the health of these trees, Milbank hopes to create a sustainable urban forest that can withstand the challenges posed by invasive species.As Milbank embarks on this ambitious project, the collaboration between local officials, environmental experts, and community members will be crucial. The city plans to implement a series of treatments on a phased basis, focusing on the most vulnerable areas first. This careful approach not only aims to save the existing trees but also hopes to foster a greater awareness of the importance of tree health and biodiversity in urban environments.The successful execution of Milbank's strategy could serve as a template for other communities grappling with similar challenges. As the city takes steps to protect its ash trees, it stands as a testament to the importance of proactive environmental stewardship, offering a beacon of hope for cities that wish to maintain their natural heritage in the face of invasive threats.

+ MILBANK ANALYZES CFTC'S NEW GUIDANCE ON PREDICTION MARKETS

In a recent webinar, Milbank attorneys examined the Commodity Futures Trading Commission’s (CFTC) latest guidance on prediction markets, which was released last week. The event, titled 'The CFTC’s New Guidance on Prediction Markets: What You Need to Know,' took place on Tuesday. This follows the CFTC's announcement on 17th March 2026, introducing a regulatory expectations advisory and an Advanced Notice of Proposed Rulemaking, initiating a 45-day public comment period that concludes on 30th April.Milbank Partner Josh Sterling opened the discussion by highlighting the remarkable growth of prediction markets, which surged from $3 billion in trading volume in January 2025 to $27 billion in January 2026. This growth has attracted attention not only from those aiming to rejuvenate financial markets but also from the courts and state regulators. The session featured Milbank litigation partners Grant Mainland, Matt Laroche, and John Williams, who provided insights on the current state of the prediction market industry and the implications of the CFTC's recent announcements for market operators.Laroche emphasized that the CFTC's advisory indicates the commission's belief that contracts related to sports events fall under its jurisdiction, which he described as a significant clarification. He stated, 'The purpose of the advisory was to provide right up from that the CFTC is recognizing it is a unique financial asset class that is rapidly increasing and provides a reliable source of information for a variety of uses.' This recognition could influence ongoing litigation regarding the classification of such contracts.Williams noted that the notice of rulemaking represents a strong stance by the CFTC to assert jurisdiction over these contracts, though it does not clearly outline future regulatory directions. He remarked on the extensive list of questions posed for public comment, which invites input on how event contracts might contribute to hedging, price risk, and information dissemination. 'It’s a good sign the commission is thinking about those things,' he added, indicating a focus on public interest.To navigate the changing regulatory landscape, Milbank attorneys outlined practical steps for prediction market operators. Recommendations included evaluating existing contract listings for compliance, establishing relationships with sports leagues, assessing manipulation risks, participating in the rulemaking process, and staying informed about blockchain developments. Mainland also provided an overview of over 20 ongoing court cases related to prediction markets, indicating that the legal landscape remains complex and uncertain, especially regarding the question of whether the Commodity Exchange Act preempts state law.

+ MILBANK TO FACILITATE DISCUSSION FOR KENNEDY-WILSON BONDHOLDERS

Milbank LLP will host a call for holders of Kennedy-Wilson, Inc.'s 4.75% senior notes due 2029, 4.75% senior notes due 2030, and 5.00% senior notes due 2031. The call is set to take place today, 5th March 2026, at 3:30 p.m. (EST), addressing concerns raised by numerous bondholders regarding the recent Exchange Offer and consent solicitation statement dated 2nd March 2026.This call is particularly significant as Milbank has received indications of interest from holders of a substantial portion of the Notes, suggesting a heightened level of engagement among investors. Bondholders who wish to participate in the call must provide their detailed contact information, along with their holdings information on a series-by-series basis, to ensure their inclusion in the discussion.Milbank LLP, a prominent international law firm founded over 150 years ago in New York, specializes in providing innovative legal services to clients globally. With offices in key financial hubs including London, Hong Kong, and S o Paulo, Milbank collaborates across various practices to assist commercial, financial, and industrial enterprises, as well as institutions and governments, in achieving their strategic objectives.In addition to hosting calls for Kennedy-Wilson bondholders, Milbank has been actively analyzing recent developments affecting unsecured creditors of Braskem S.A. This highlights the firm’s commitment to providing timely and relevant legal advice to its clients in an ever-evolving financial landscape.As Milbank continues to expand its footprint and enhance its services, the firm remains dedicated to fostering transparent communication between issuers and investors, ensuring that concerns are addressed promptly and effectively.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ DEBEVOISE & PLIMPTON LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ DEBEVOISE & PLIMPTON ACHIEVES UNPRECEDENTED REVENUE GROWTH IN LONDON

Debevoise & Plimpton has reported a remarkable revenue surge of 40% in its London office, reaching a record $268 million. This significant increase has drawn attention in the legal industry and among investors seeking insights into the firm's growth trajectory. The results, announced on 7th April 2026, highlight the firm's robust performance in a competitive market.The leap in revenue is attributed to an uptick in demand for legal services across various sectors, including corporate law and dispute resolution. Debevoise & Plimpton's strategic focus on high-value client engagements and its ability to adapt to changing market conditions have played a key role in this growth. The firm has consistently positioned itself as a leader in providing comprehensive legal solutions to its clientele.In light of this impressive performance, analysts are keenly observing how Debevoise & Plimpton plans to sustain this momentum moving forward. The firm has expressed its commitment to investing in talent and expanding its service offerings, which may further bolster its market position. This proactive approach could potentially attract more clients and drive additional revenue growth in the coming years.The legal sector has witnessed a notable transformation in recent years, and firms like Debevoise & Plimpton that embrace innovation and adapt to new challenges may be well-positioned for continued success. As competition intensifies, maintaining a strong financial performance will be crucial for the firm to retain its competitive edge.As Debevoise & Plimpton celebrates this milestone, the legal community is left wondering whether this growth trend will continue. Investors and clients alike will be looking for signs of sustained performance, as the firm navigates the complexities of the ever-evolving legal landscape.

+ DEBEVOISE & PLIMPTON ACHIEVES LANDMARK VICTORY FOR YPF S.A. IN $16 BILLION CASE

In a significant legal victory, Debevoise & Plimpton LLP has successfully secured an affirmance from the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Second Circuit regarding its summary judgment win on behalf of YPF S.A., the largest energy producer in Argentina. This decision, made on 30th March 2026, effectively dismisses claims for over $16 billion in damages and interest, marking a pivotal moment in a case that has lingered in the Southern District of New York since 2015.The Second Circuit upheld the District Court’s finding that YPF did not have any contractual obligations to Petersen Energ a Inversora, S.A.U., Petersen Energ a, S.A.U., or Eton Park Capital Management, L.P., along with its associated funds. This ruling is seen as a major victory for YPF, affirming its non-liability for the claims advanced by the plaintiffs, who also included litigation funder Burford Capital.The case traces its roots back to Argentina's 2012 intervention and subsequent expropriation of 51% of YPF's capital stock. The litigation commenced in 2015, and Debevoise took over YPF's defense in December 2020 after earlier motions to dismiss were denied. Following two years of extensive discovery involving numerous expert testimonies, both the plaintiffs and YPF, alongside the Republic of Argentina, sought summary judgment in 2022.In 2023, the District Court granted YPF’s motion for summary judgment in its entirety, adopting Debevoise's arguments that YPF was not obliged under its bylaws to compel the Republic to conduct a tender offer or restrict the Republic's corporate governance rights over the expropriated shares. Conversely, the District Court largely granted the plaintiffs’ motion for summary judgment against the Republic, resulting in a judgment exceeding $16 billion against it in September 2023.Following appeals from all parties involved, the case was fully briefed and argued before the Second Circuit in October 2025. The affirmation of YPF's victory not only reinforces the firm's position but also highlights the complexities involved in international energy disputes and expropriation cases, setting a notable precedent in the legal landscape.

+ DEBEVOISE ELEVATES JEFFREY SULLIVAN KC AND INA POPOVA TO EUROPEAN DISPUTES LEADERSHIP

Debevoise & Plimpton LLP has announced the appointment of partners Jeffrey Sullivan KC and Ina Popova as Co-Chairs of European Disputes, effective immediately. This strategic move enhances the firm's presence in key markets, particularly in Paris, Frankfurt, and Luxembourg. The announcement comes on the 17th of March, 2026, highlighting the firm's commitment to strengthening its capabilities in international dispute resolution across mainland Europe.Catherine Amirfar, who serves as Co-Chair of Debevoise's global International Dispute Resolution Group, expressed confidence in the leadership of Sullivan KC and Popova. She stated, 'Ina and Jeff’s leadership will build on Debevoise’s long-standing strength in major disputes across mainland Europe, ensuring we continue to deliver the highest level of advocacy and strategic counsel to our clients.' Their appointments align with the firm's strategy of appointing regional leaders to drive its dispute resolution practice worldwide, joining other prominent leaders in Latin America and Asia.Jeffrey Sullivan KC is already recognized as a leading figure in international arbitration and public international law. In his new role, he will continue to focus on high-stakes commercial and investor-state arbitrations, representing a diverse clientele that includes companies and private equity sponsors. His established reputation in the market is complemented by his experience as an arbitrator in significant cases.Ina Popova brings a wealth of experience in international arbitration and public international law, focusing on managing and litigating complex disputes. Her dual qualification in civil and common law, along with her fluency in multiple languages, positions her uniquely to navigate the intricacies of cross-border disputes. Popova's previous role as a member of the ICC International Court of Arbitration further underscores her expertise and credibility in the field.With over four decades of experience, Debevoise’s International Dispute Resolution Group has consistently delivered exceptional outcomes in international arbitration and public international law. The team's extensive expertise spans multiple jurisdictions and languages, making it a formidable player in the global disputes arena. Recent engagements have involved over 125 countries and stakes exceeding $110 billion, showcasing Debevoise's commitment to achieving significant results for its clients.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ SULLIVAN & CROMWELL [2 News That Matters]

+ SULLIVAN & CROMWELL'S RODGE COHEN REFLECTS ON FIVE DECADES IN BANKING

H. Rodgin “Rodge” Cohen, senior chair at Sullivan & Cromwell, has been a transformative force in the banking industry for over five decades. On 19th February 2026, during an event hosted by NYU Law's Institute for Corporate Governance & Finance (ICGF), Cohen shared his experiences and insights on the evolution of the modern financial system. The conversation was enriched by contributions from Jay Clayton, former chairman of the US Securities and Exchange Commission, and Edward Rock, co-director of ICGF.Cohen's legal career has coincided with numerous landmark events that have shaped the financial landscape. Clayton, who has previously worked alongside Cohen at Sullivan & Cromwell, highlighted the profound changes in banking regulation since the 1970s. Cohen emphasized the significance of the Bank Secrecy Act and the Community Reinvestment Act, noting that these measures have drastically changed access to banking services, reducing the percentage of unbanked Americans from one-third to just five percent.A defining moment in Cohen's career came during the 2008 global financial crisis, where he represented several major banks in efforts to stabilize the financial system. Reflecting on the crisis, Cohen recalled the critical hours leading up to the government’s decision to intervene after the collapse of Lehman Brothers. He stated, 'We were on the edge of a cliff,' emphasizing the urgency and gravity of the situation. The collaboration between Jones Day and Wachtell highlights the critical importance of experienced legal guidance in navigating complex mergers, particularly in an era of heightened regulatory scrutiny.

+ WACHTELL LIPTON GUIDES PUBLIC STORAGE IN $10.5BN ACQUISITION

Wachtell, Lipton, Rosen & Katz LLP and Clifford Chance are taking lead roles in a monumental $10.5 billion transaction aimed at establishing a self-storage powerhouse. The deal was officially announced on 24th March 2026, with Wachtell Lipton advising its long-standing client, Public Storage, the largest self-storage company in the United States by market capitalization, on its acquisition of National Storage Affiliates.Clifford Chance is representing National Storage Affiliates in this significant all-stock transaction, which will boost Public Storage's market capitalization to approximately $57 billion. The acquisition will enable the combined entity to oversee an impressive square footage equivalent to that of a small city, expanding its footprint significantly in key U.S. markets.Leading Wachtell Lipton's team is Adam Emmerich, co-chair of the corporate department, alongside M&A partner Kyle Diamond. Emmerich has a history of advising Public Storage on major transactions, including its $6 billion acquisition of Shurgard in 2006 and the $2.2 billion purchase of Simply Self Storage from Blackstone Real Estate Income Trust in 2023. The firm’s expertise is expected to play a crucial role in navigating the complexities of this latest acquisition.Clifford Chance's team is spearheaded by M&A partners Chang-Do Gong and Robert Chung, who have both recently joined the firm from White & Case. They are joined by capital markets partner Andrew Epstein and real estate partner Jeanne Roig-Irwin. Meanwhile, DLA Piper is providing real estate financing counsel to Public Storage, with its team led by partner Amy Carbins and supported by New York-based partner Viktor Sapezhnikov.Under the terms of the agreement, shareholders of National Storage will receive 0.14 of a share of Public Storage common stock for each National Storage share they own, translating to a total consideration of $41.68 per share. The transaction is anticipated to close in the third quarter of 2026, following the formation of a joint venture that will encompass 313 properties with an estimated value of approximately $3.3 billion.

+ ELON MUSK'S X CORP ENDS $90M LEGAL BATTLE WITH WACHTELL LIPTON

In a swift legal pivot, Elon Musk’s X Corp has voluntarily dismissed its high-stakes $90 million lawsuit against powerhouse law firm Wachtell Lipton Rosen & Katz, closing a bitter chapter from the chaotic 2022 Twitter takeover. The lawsuit, filed in San Francisco Superior Court back on 1st October 2023, accused Wachtell of pocketing an 'improper bonus payment' that violated fiduciary duties, claiming Twitter executives inflated fees with 'success' bonuses just before the $44 billion deal closed.Wachtell Lipton, which represented the old Twitter board in the fierce battle to compel Musk to honor his buyout pledge, responded to the accusations with vigor. The firm emphasized its 'complete success' after months of litigation, which ultimately secured billions for shareholders. They asserted that the fees in question were approved by the board itself, effectively countering the claims made by X Corp.The dismissal of the lawsuit, which was revealed in a court filing reported by Reuters, bars refiling and indicates a clean break between the two parties. A spokesperson for Wachtell Lipton expressed satisfaction with the outcome, stating, 'We are pleased that X has dismissed its meritless lawsuit with prejudice. There was no settlement.' This marks a significant turn in the ongoing legal saga surrounding the Twitter acquisition.This legal retreat comes on the heels of another reconciliation for X Corp, which recently acknowledged a $128 million executive payout suit related to the tumultuous period during the Twitter acquisition. The dismissal signals a potential shift in strategy for X Corp, as it seeks to distance itself from the financial and legal entanglements of its predecessor.As the legal landscape continues to evolve for Elon Musk's X Corp, the resolution with Wachtell Lipton may pave the way for a more focused approach to its business operations. Investors and market observers will be keenly watching how these developments influence the company's trajectory moving forward.

+ CRAVATH, SWAINE & MOORE LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ CRAVATH SWAINE & MOORE PARTNER VENTURES INTO CORPORATE INVESTIGATIONS

In a notable shift within the legal landscape, a partner from Cravath Swaine & Moore has announced the formation of a new boutique law firm dedicated to corporate investigations. This move comes as businesses increasingly seek specialized counsel to navigate complex regulatory environments and compliance challenges. The partner, whose experience at Cravath has positioned them as a leader in the field, aims to leverage their expertise to provide tailored services to clients facing intricate corporate scrutiny.On 9th March 2026, the launch of this boutique firm signals a growing trend among established legal practitioners to break away from traditional law firm structures in favor of more agile, focused practices. The new firm is expected to offer services ranging from internal investigations, compliance assessments, to risk management strategies. This initiative not only reflects a shift in client needs but also highlights the evolving nature of legal services in the corporate sector.Cravath Swaine & Moore has long been recognized for its prowess in corporate law, and the establishment of this boutique by one of its partners further emphasizes the firm’s influential role in the legal community. As regulatory environments become increasingly complex, the demand for specialized knowledge in corporate investigations is likely to grow. The new firm is set to cater to corporations facing challenges related to compliance violations and internal misconduct, providing a fresh perspective backed by years of experience in high-stakes legal matters.Industry experts predict that this boutique firm will attract a diverse clientele, including Fortune 500 companies, private equity firms, and startups. The partner’s extensive network and reputation built during their tenure at Cravath Swaine & Moore will be instrumental in establishing the new firm's credibility and market presence. Clients are expected to benefit from a personalized approach that larger firms might struggle to provide, particularly in sensitive investigations where discretion and expertise are paramount.As the legal landscape continues to evolve, the emergence of such specialized firms underscores the importance of adaptability within the legal profession. The launch of this corporate investigations boutique represents not only a strategic career move for the Cravath partner but also a response to the pressing needs of corporate clients. The legal community will be watching closely to see how this venture develops and whether it will set a precedent for similar initiatives in the future.

+ BIOCON'S $1.2 BILLION BIOSIMILARS DEAL INVOLVED MULTIPLE LEGAL ADVISORS

Biocon, an Indian biopharmaceutical company, has finalized a significant $1.2 billion deal aimed at fully consolidating its biosimilars business by acquiring the remaining stakes from minority partners. This strategic move is expected to streamline Biocon's operations and enhance its market position in the growing biosimilars sector. On the 11th of December 2025, the company disclosed this acquisition, marking a pivotal shift in its growth trajectory.The deal sees Biocon acquiring stakes from various minority partners, including a notable transaction with Serum Institute Life Sciences, which was advised by Cyril Amarchand Mangaldas. Viatris, having sold its remaining stake for $815 million, received guidance from Khaitan & Co and Cravath, Swaine & Moore. The acquisition also involved other sellers, including True North and Tata Capital Growth Fund II, which was advised by Veritas Legal, further highlighting the collaborative nature of this transaction.Shardul Amarchand Mangaldas (SAM) led the advisory team for Biocon, with partners Raghubir Menon and Natashaa Shroff at the forefront. The deal team also included principal associate Tarumoy Chaudhuri and senior associate Kriti Saraswat, alongside a comprehensive support team. This consolidation comes as Biocon has been eyeing the completion of its integration with Viatris and the refinancing of its debt, as reported by Reuters.The legal landscape for this transaction was extensive, with multiple law firms providing specialized guidance. Khaitan & Co's transaction team, led by partners Rabindra Jhunjhunwala and Abhishek Dadoo, played a crucial role in advising on various aspects of the deal. Meanwhile, Cravath, Swaine & Moore's team, headed by partners Mark I Greene and Aaron M Gruber, focused on financing matters, showcasing the depth of expertise involved in this high-stakes transaction.As Biocon moves forward with its plans, the successful consolidation of its biosimilars business is anticipated to strengthen its competitive edge in the biopharmaceutical market. The company's strategic maneuvers, supported by a robust legal framework, position it well for future growth in an increasingly competitive sector.

+ CRAVATH SWAINE & MOORE UNVEILS YEAR-END BONUSES, ASSOCIATES TO EARN UP TO $140K

Cravath Swaine & Moore, one of the largest U.S. law firms, has officially launched the year-end bonus season by announcing that its associates will receive payouts of up to $140,000. On 20th November 2025, the New York-based firm communicated to its associates via an internal memo that annual bonuses will range from $15,000 for first-year associates to $115,000 for the most senior associates, according to information reported by Above the Law.In addition to the standard bonuses, Cravath plans to offer special bonuses ranging from $6,000 to $25,000, mirroring the payouts recently made by rival firm Milbank. This strategic move reinforces Cravath’s position as a traditional first mover in the competitive landscape of associate compensation within the legal sector. While the firm declined to provide further comments, the bonus structure aligns with expectations set by previous years.Following Cravath's announcement, other major firms are already responding. Paul Hastings, for instance, quickly disclosed its own year-end and special bonuses, which reflect a similar scale to those set by Cravath. A spokesperson from Paul Hastings also chose not to comment, indicating that the firm is likely following the trend established by Cravath.The bonuses announced by Cravath Swaine & Moore are consistent with those distributed by large U.S. law firms last year, as many associates now earn annual salaries ranging from $225,000 to $435,000 depending on their class year. The competitive nature of the legal profession often leads firms to match salary and bonus figures to retain top talent, and Cravath’s decision is expected to influence other firms in the sector.Looking ahead, U.S. law firms are poised for a profitable 2025, with large and midsized firms experiencing a significant increase in client demand, particularly in transactional practices. This positive outlook was highlighted in a recent analysis of firm financial data, suggesting that the legal industry is gearing up for another successful year, further emphasizing the importance of competitive compensation packages like those offered by Cravath.

+ DAVIS POLK & WARDWELL LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ SIGENERGY'S HK IPO GAINS MOMENTUM WITH DAVIS POLK'S LEGAL EXPERTISE

Sigenergy Technology, a Chinese energy storage systems maker, has successfully navigated its initial public offering (IPO) in Hong Kong, raising HK$4.4 billion, equivalent to approximately $562 million. The transaction was facilitated by prominent law firms Davis Polk & Wardwell and Jia Yuan Law Offices, who provided critical legal advice throughout the process. This significant event was recorded on the 17th of April 2026.Founded in May 2022, Sigenergy specializes in distributed energy storage systems (DESS) and is headquartered in Shanghai. The company focuses on delivering innovative stackable, distributed solar-plus-storage all-in-one solutions, positioning itself strategically within the rapidly growing energy sector. Sigenergy has emerged as a leader in the market, commanding a 28.6 percent share in the global segment of stackable all-in-one distributed energy storage solutions, according to market data.The IPO was underwritten by a consortium of financial institutions, including CITIC Securities and BNP Paribas, with China International Capital Corporation (CICC) also playing a role as a joint sponsor. Legal support for the underwriters was provided by Linklaters and Jingtian & Gongcheng, with Linklaters’ team led by partner Christine Xu, who oversees Greater China ECM, and capital markets partner Lipton Li, alongside counsel Queenie Tong.In a related development, Mumbai-based Mindspace Business Parks REIT has made headlines with its acquisition of the International Tech Park in Chennai, valued at 30 billion rupees, or $321 million. This transaction was advised by Khaitan & Co, marking a significant investment in the Indian real estate sector. Furthermore, Indian asset manager SBI Funds Management is preparing for a substantial 130-billion-rupee IPO, with Cyril Amarchand Mangaldas and Linklaters advising on the endeavor.The successful IPO of Sigenergy Technology not only highlights the company’s robust market position but also reflects the growing investor interest in renewable energy solutions. As the demand for energy storage systems continues to rise, Sigenergy is well-positioned to capitalize on emerging opportunities in the sector.

+ DAVIS POLK'S LIGHTYEAR CAPITAL SECURES $2.5B FOR SIXTH FUND

Lightyear Capital, a private equity firm co-founded by Davis Polk & Wardwell, has successfully raised $2.5 billion for its sixth fund. This significant capital infusion highlights the firm's robust position in the asset management space and reflects investor confidence in its investment strategy and market outlook. The announcement was made public on April 14, 2026.The latest fund is poised to target investments in the financial services and technology sectors, which have shown resilience and growth potential in recent years. With this capital, Lightyear Capital aims to capitalize on emerging opportunities within these industries, leveraging its extensive network and expertise to identify lucrative investment avenues. Davis Polk's longstanding experience in providing legal services to financial institutions further strengthens Lightyear's operational capabilities.The fundraising comes amid a competitive landscape for private equity firms, where securing investor commitments has become increasingly challenging. Lightyear Capital's ability to attract significant backing can be attributed to its track record of strong returns and strategic investment decisions. The firm plans to focus on both established companies and innovative startups, providing them with the necessary support to scale their operations effectively.Davis Polk & Wardwell's involvement in Lightyear Capital is not merely advisory; the firm's attorneys have been integral in structuring the fund and navigating the regulatory landscape. Their expertise ensures that Lightyear operates within legal parameters while maximizing investor returns, a critical factor for stakeholders in today’s complex financial environment. The collaboration between the two entities underscores the synergy between legal prowess and investment acumen.Looking ahead, investors will be keen to see how Lightyear Capital deploys its newly raised capital and whether it can maintain its momentum in generating returns. As the market evolves, the firm’s strategies will be closely scrutinized, particularly in the context of economic fluctuations and sector-specific challenges. With Davis Polk's backing, Lightyear Capital is well-positioned to make a significant impact in the private equity arena.

+ DAVIS POLK EXPANDS WESTWARD WITH SKADDEN PARTNER JASON RUSSELL

Davis Polk & Wardwell is set to expand its footprint by opening a new office in Los Angeles, recruiting Jason Russell, the head of Skadden Arps Slate Meagher & Flom’s LA office. This strategic move is expected to enhance Davis Polk's capabilities in a city known for its vibrant legal landscape, particularly in the sectors of sports and entertainment. The firm plans to officially open its Los Angeles office on 14th April 2026.Russell, a distinguished litigator with years of experience at Skadden, has represented prominent clients such as the National Basketball Association, Christie’s Inc., MGA Entertainment Inc., and Credit Acceptance Corp. His expertise in high-stakes litigation and complex class actions positions Davis Polk favorably in the competitive Los Angeles market. Additionally, Russell has served on Skadden’s policy committee, underscoring his influence within the firm.The opening of the Los Angeles office is a significant milestone for Davis Polk, which has been gaining prominence in the legal sector. The firm had a remarkable year in 2025, achieving a 26% increase in revenue to $3.2 billion, which was the highest growth among major law firms. In comparison, Skadden recently crossed the $4 billion revenue threshold but did not match Davis Polk's growth rate. This success has translated into attractive partner profits, with Davis Polk partners earning an average of $9.8 million.Davis Polk is not alone in recognizing the potential of the Los Angeles market. The city has seen a surge of interest from leading law firms, including Willkie Farr & Gallagher, which established an office in 2021 and has since expanded to over 100 attorneys. Moreover, Paul Weiss Rifkind Wharton & Garrison recently opened an office in LA following a strategic acquisition from Kirkland & Ellis, indicating strong competition for top legal talent and clients.As Davis Polk prepares for its Los Angeles debut, further hiring plans are already underway. With Jason Russell at the helm and an ambitious growth strategy, the firm aims to carve out a significant presence in the region. The legal industry will be closely watching how this expansion unfolds in a city that has long been a hub for major law firms.

DASHBOARD: MILBANK LLP

PermID4297973195Websitehttps://www.milbank.com/IndustryFull-Service BigLawAddress55 Hudson Yards, New York, NY 10001-2163

ACTIVITIES:

Milbank LLP, founded in 1866, is a global law firm headquartered in New York City. With over 800 lawyers across 12 offices, it serves clients in finance, energy, and infrastructure, specializing in project finance, M&A, and litigation. Generating over $1 billion annually, Milbank advised on major 2024 energy deals, per its recognition by Columbia Law School. Its culture fosters collaboration, diversity, and pro bono work, with 247 attorneys registered in New York. Competing with Sullivan & Cromwell, Milbank stands out for its finance expertise, with attorneys like John D. Franchini noted for excellence. In 2024, it expanded its renewable energy and AI practices. Recent initiatives include digital transaction platforms and ESG-focused financing, aligning with market demands.

+ MILBANK LLP [3 Press Releases]

+ 53 MILBANK LAWYERS FEATURED IN 2026 PRO BONO RECOGNITION LIST OF ENGLAND & WALES

Congratulations to the 53 Milbank lawyers featured in the 2026 Pro Bono Recognition List of England & Wales. The list recognizes barristers and solicitors who have given 25 hours of their time to pro bono work in the previous calendar year.

Source: Company Website

+ MILBANK ADVISES SOCIEDAD TRANSMISORA METROPOLITANA ON $630M GREEN RULE 144A PROJECT BOND OFFERING

Milbank LLP advised Sociedad Transmisora Metropolitana S.A. ("STM") in connection with the $630 million issuance of 6.385% green senior secured project bonds due 2055. STM is a leading Chilean power transmission company and a subsidiary of Grupo Saesa (a JV between Ontario Teachers' Pension Plan ("OTTP") and Alberta Investment Management Corporation ("AIMCo")). The proceeds of the bonds were used by STM, together with the proceeds of a bond issuance in the Chilean market, to repay a $1.1 billion syndicated credit facility used to finance Grupo Saesa's acquisition of Enel Transmision Chile, the principal power transmission company in Chile's metropolitan region.

Source: Company Website

+ MILBANK TRUSTS & ESTATES GROUP RANKED BAND 1 IN 2026 LEGAL 500 US PRIVATE CLIENT GUIDE

Milbank LLP's Trusts, Estates and Exempt Organizations Group is top-ranked for Private Wealth in New York in the 2026 Legal 500 US Private Client Guide. In addition to the firm recognition, co-practice group leader Austin Bramwell is featured as a "Leading Partner," and co-practice group leader Jessica Soojian is "Recommended."

Source: Company Website

Debevoise & Plimpton LLP has advised Elliott Investment Management in connection with its investment in Stratolaunch, a hypersonics technology accelerator, to support the expansion of the company's hypersonic production and flight capabilities. For more information, please see the press release.

+ DEBEVOISE ADVISED KKR IN THE FORMATION OF ITS $2.5 BILLION ASIA PRIVATE CREDIT FUNDRAISE

Debevoise & Plimpton LLP has advised KKR in the formation of its $2.5 billion Asia private credit fundraise focused on committing capital to privately originated performing credit investments in Asia Pacific. For more information, please see the company's press release.

The Debevoise team was led by investment management partner Lorna Bowen and included partner Sheena Paul, counsel Christina Chen Marex and Huiyu Yin and associates Charlie Altman, Ali Curry, Chris Duff, Zach Ferguson, Ruth Fruchter, Emma Hauber, Patrick Zuliang Li, Lawson Miralha, Julia Glazer Needle, James Tai, Johanna Waber and Eike Bjorn Weidner, and tax partners Stephen Jordan and Daniel Priest and associates Shanna Adler and Jeannie Le.

Source: Company Website

+ DEBEVOISE & PLIMPTON LLP: MORE THAN $21 BILLION OF CFO TRANSACTIONS CLOSED IN PAST NINE MONTHS

Debevoise & Plimpton LLP has advised its clients across multiple Collateralized Fund Obligation (CFO) transactions with an aggregate value exceeding $21 billion over the past nine months. In addition to these CFO transactions, the Debevoise investment management and finance teams have advised clients in many single fund rated feeder transactions over the same time period.

Source: Company Website

+ SULLIVAN & CROMWELL [3 Press Releases]

+ SULLIVAN & CROMWELL: FDIC RESCINDS SUPERVISORY GUIDANCE ON MULTIPLE RE-PRESENTMENT NON-SUFFICIENT FUNDS FEES

Financial institutions commonly charge NSF fees when checks or Automated Clearinghouse ("ACH") transactions are presented for payment but cannot be covered by the balance in a customer's transaction account. After a check or ACH payment has been declined, it may later be resubmitted for payment (e.g., by a merchant), which may result in the customer's financial institution charging an additional NSF fee for the same transaction if the customer's account does not then reflect the necessary balance. The FDIC refers to these additional NSF fees as re-presentment NSF fees.

In its now-rescinded guidance, the FDIC had stated that failing to "clearly and conspicuously" disclose a practice of charging multiple re-presentment NSF fees could be both "deceptive" and "unfair" and therefore potentially a violation of Section 5 of the Federal Trade Commission Act, which prohibits unfair or deceptive acts or practices. The FDIC had also stated that consumer disclosures may not be sufficient to address potential unfairness to consumers, especially if "multiple NSF fees are assessed for the same transaction in a short period of time without sufficient notice or opportunity for customers to bring their account to a positive balance in order to avoid the assessment of additional NSF fees." The FDIC's guidance also described additional risks related to charging multiple re-presentment NSF fees, as well as risk mitigation practices and the FDIC's supervisory approach to addressing consumer harm. Source: Company Website

+ SULLIVAN & CROMWELL: GENIUS ACT IMPLEMENTATION

On April 7, the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation (the "FDIC") issued a notice of proposed rulemaking (the "FDIC NPR")[1] to implement provisions of the "Guiding and Establishing National Innovation for U.S. Stablecoins Act" or the "GENIUS Act.[2] The FDIC NPR proposes requirements that would apply to FDIC-supervised permitted payment stablecoin issuers ("PPSIs"), which in all cases would be subsidiaries of FDIC-supervised insured depository institutions ("IDIs"). The FDIC also proposes clarifications to its deposit insurance regulations, including that deposits held as reserves for a payment stablecoin would not be insured on a pass-through basis. The FDIC NPR also sets forth updates to the FDIC's regulations to provide that the technology or recordkeeping used by an IDI to record its deposit liabilities does not affect whether those liabilities constitute "deposits." This update would clarify that a tokenized deposit that meets the statutory definition of "deposit" under the Federal Deposit Insurance Act (the "FDI Act") is a deposit and is treated no differently under the FDI Act than other forms of deposits. Source: Company Website

+ SULLIVAN & CROMWELL: FEDERAL RESERVE PROPOSES EXPANSION THAT WOULD PERMIT USE OF FEDNOW FOR CROSS-BORDER PAYMENTS

On April 8, 2026, the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System issued a proposal to amend Subpart C of Regulation J, which governs the FedNow® Service. The proposal would permit FedNow participants to use intermediaries other than Reserve Banks to send funds transfers through FedNow.[1] This change would permit FedNow to be used for cross-border payments, aligning FedNow with the Fedwire® Funds Service, which is widely used for cross-border payments and has "permitted intermediaries for decades."

Currently, banks that are FedNow participants may not use intermediaries (other than Federal Reserve Banks) for funds transfers sent through FedNow.[2] This means that FedNow is not currently available for cross-border payments because both the originator and the beneficiary must be customers of U.S. banks. When the Federal Reserve announced details of FedNow in 2020, it acknowledged this limitation to domestic payments, but noted plans to consider expanding to cross-border payments in the future.[3]

In its current proposal, the Federal Reserve indicated that it expects that allowing participants to use intermediaries other than the Reserve Banks would enable cross-border payments to be processed through FedNow. In particular, either the sending U.S. bank or receiving U.S. bank would be permitted to act as a correspondent bank for a non-U.S. bank. As a result, an international portion of a payment could be processed through that correspondent bank outside of FedNow, with FedNow being used for the domestic portion of the payment.

Comments on the proposal will be due 60 days after publication in the Federal Register. Source: Company Website

+ LATHAM & WATKINS LLP [4 Press Releases]

+ LATHAM & WATKINS ADVISES LEADING SEMICONDUCTOR INFRASTRUCTURE INVESTOR MAVERICK SILICON ON FINANCING FOR MMTRON

mmTron, a semiconductor startup redefining the RF layer powering next‑generation connectivity, has announced US$45 million in Series A financing. Kenneth Safar at Maverick Silicon led the round with participation from Kelvin Khoo at Tipping Point Ventures, J&M, and other investors. This funding milestone endorses mmTron's demonstrated capabilities and its product‑market mission to redefine high‑performance RF connectivity.

Latham & Watkins LLP represented Maverick Silicon in the transaction with a corporate deal team led by partners John Miller and Haim Zaltzman, with associates Mason Taylor, Samantha McGarvey, and William Brown.

Source: Company Website

+ LATHAM ADVISES BLACKSTONE INFRASTRUCTURE IN INVESTMENT OF UP TO 2 BILLION IN EUROWIND ENERGY

Latham & Watkins has advised Blackstone Infrastructure on an agreement to invest up to EUR2 billion in Eurowind Energy, a leading pan-European renewables developer and independent power producer. The transaction is expected to close before the end of the year, subject to customary conditions.

Source: Company Website

+ LATHAM & WATKINS ADVISES CONCORD ON COMBINATION WITH BMG TO CREATE WORLDS LEADING INDEPENDENT MUSIC COMPANY

BMG and Concord have announced a definitive agreement to combine their businesses, creating the leading independent music company in the world. Operating under the BMG name, the combined company will bring together two highly complementary businesses to establish a scaled, global company delivering superior service to artists, songwriters, and partners - firmly grounded in an independent, artist-first model.

Latham & Watkins LLP advised Concord in the transaction with a corporate deal team led by partners Nadia Sager, Javier Stark, and Shanta Chirravuri.

Source: Company Website

+ LATHAM ADVISES ON ASIAN INFRASTRUCTURE INVESTMENT BANKS US$2 BILLION BOND OFFERING

Latham & Watkins has advised the underwriters on Asian Infrastructure Investment Bank's (AIIB) issuance of US$2 billion 3.875% notes due 2031. The firm previously advised on AIIB's US$1 billion offering in January 2026.

The Latham team was led by London corporate partner Ryan Benedict, with associates Valera Bamgbala, Caroline Koller Hill, and Tom Breheny.

Source: Company Website

+ SKADDEN, ARPS, SLATE, MEAGHER & FLOM, L.L.P. [4 Press Releases]

+ SKADDEN, ARPS, SLATE, MEAGHER & FLOM, L.L.P.: LIFE SCIENCES ANTITRUST: RECENT EUROPEAN COMMISSION ENFORCEMENT AND TRENDS

The European Commission appears to be advancing a more expansive antitrust agenda in life sciences - one increasingly focused on innovation, exclusionary conduct and strategic interference with future competition. Counsel Michael Frese and European counsel Georgios Zacharodimos discuss how recent cases highlight the EC's efforts to prevent innovation suppression, unfair practices and anticompetitive conduct across the pharmaceutical sector, where merger reviews, abuse investigations and cartel enforcement are reshaping industry dynamics.

Source: Company Website

+ SKADDEN, ARPS, SLATE, MEAGHER & FLOM, L.L.P.: NINTH CIRCUIT ENJOINS CALIFORNIA CORPORATE CLIMATE RISK DISCLOSURE LAW, BUT THE WAITING GAME CONTINUES

On November 18, 2025, the Ninth Circuit stayed the enforcement of a California law requiring certain companies to publish a climate risk report. Whether companies will need to disclose their greenhouse gas emissions in 2026 remains an open question. Partners Raquel Fox and Virginia Milstead, counsel Caroline Kim and Liz Malone and associate Sydney Smith discuss the legal challenges, ongoing regulatory uncertainties and implications for companies' compliance planning.

Source: Company Website

+ SKADDEN, ARPS, SLATE, MEAGHER & FLOM, L.L.P.: NEW YEAR, NEW POLITICAL LAW TRENDS

Spending on federal lobbying reached an all-time high of approximately $4.5 billion in 2024 and continues to increase. Federal political action committees (PACs) raised $25.3 billion in the 2020 election cycle and $25.4 billion in the 2024 cycle, both more than doubling any previous election cycle, according to Federal Election Commission data. This upward trend continues in the 2026 cycle.

Source: Company Website

+ SKADDEN, ARPS, SLATE, MEAGHER & FLOM, L.L.P.: BANK OF ENGLAND REVISES ITS PROPOSED REGIME FOR REGULATING SYSTEMICSTABLECOIN

Bank of England proposals for regulating sterling-denominated stablecoins have shifted since 2023, but remain significantly more onerous than the FCA's proposals for non-systemic stablecoins in many respects. Partners Simon Toms and Sebastian Barling and associate Joseph Kamyar discuss the bank's latest proposals - including criteria for systemic importance, asset composition, capital and reserve standards, holding limits, legal claims and safeguarding measures - as well as the next steps for consultation and implementation.

Source: Company Website

+ SIMPSON THACHER & BARTLETT [3 Press Releases]

+ SIMPSON THACHER REPRESENTS INITIAL PURCHASERS IN $350 MILLION SUBORDINATED NOTES OFFERING BY TXNM

Simpson Thacher recently represented the initial purchasers, led by MUFG, Citigroup and RBC Capital Markets, in connection with an offering of $350 million in aggregate principal amount of 7.000% Fixed-to-Fixed Reset Rate Junior Subordinated Notes due 2056 by TXNM Energy, Inc. ("TXNM"). TXNM intends to use the net proceeds from the offering to repay short-term debt and for general corporate purposes.

TXNM is an energy holding company based in Albuquerque, New Mexico. Through its regulated utilities, Public Service Company of New Mexico and Texas-New Mexico Power Company, TXNM delivers energy to more than 800,000 homes and businesses across New Mexico and Texas.

The Simpson Thacher team for the transaction included Mark Brod, Barry Lau and Emily Anne Owen (Capital Markets); Jon Cantor and Jemma Li (Tax); and Jennie Getsin (FINRA and Blue Sky).

Source: Company Website

+ SIMPSON THACHER & BARTLETT MARK SKERRY REFERENCED IN MLEX ON CFIUS PRIORITIES AND OUTLOOK FOR 2026

Partner Mark Skerry was referenced in an MLex article examining the priorities and policy direction of the Committee on Foreign Investment in the United States (CFIUS) last year and the trends to look for in 2026. Mark provided insights on emerging regulatory measures regarding foreign investment, noting that while CFIUS will continue to address national security threats, other federal and state-level measures shouldn't be overlooked. These measures, Mark observed, will have a significant impact on investment into and out of the United States in the coming year.

Source: Company Website

+ SIMPSON THACHER & BARTLETT GEOFF BAILHACHE AND JAMES HOWE QUOTED IN LAW.COM ON M&A OUTLOOK IN EUROPE

Geoff Bailhache and James R. Howe, co-heads of the Firm's European M&A practice, were quoted in Law.com on what to expect from dealmaking activity in 2026. James explained how "European deal activity strengthened notably through the second half of 2025, with large cap and mid-market transactions rebounding as assets that had been held back earlier in the year finally came to market." Geoff also highlighted "despite ongoing macroeconomic challenges, an improving IPO market in both the U.S. and Europe has provided an important tailwind, reinforcing confidence and helping to stimulate M&A activity."

+ DAVIS POLK & WARDWELL LLP MICHAEL RISHTY PROFILED IN NEW YORK REAL ESTATE JOURNALS 2026 ONES TO WATCH INDUSTRY LEADERS

Davis Polk partner and Real Estate practice co-head Michael Rishty was profiled by New York Real Estate Journal as part of the publication's "2026 Ones to Watch - Industry Leaders" list. In the Q&A, Michael discusses a recent accomplishment, who has impacted his career the most and advice for emerging professionals in commercial real estate.

Source: Company Website

+ DAVIS POLK & WARDWELL LLP DAMIAN SCHAIBLE DISCUSSES OUT-OF-COURT CREDITOR DEALS WITH BLOOMBERG

Davis Polk partner and Restructuring practice co-head Damian Schaible discussed the market conditions that are driving an increase in out-of-court restructuring deals with Bloomberg.

Source: Company Website

ACTIVITIES:

Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP, founded in 1868, is a global law firm headquartered in New York City. With over 700 lawyers across 20 offices, it serves clients in energy, technology, and finance, specializing in M&A, regulatory, and litigation. Generating over $700 million annually, Pillsbury advised on 2024 renewable energy transactions, recognized by Columbia Law School. Its culture emphasizes innovation, diversity, and pro bono work, with strong associate training programs. Competing with Baker McKenzie, Pillsbury stands out for its energy and tech regulatory expertise. In 2024, it expanded its AI and ESG practices, focusing on sustainable finance. Recent initiatives include cloud-based legal tech and partnerships with nonprofits like Lawyers Alliance for New York, reinforcing its community impact.

+ PERKINS COIE STRENGTHENS PACIFIC NORTHWEST TRIAL BENCH WITH COMMERCIAL LITIGATOR CHAD COLTON

+ COOLEY LLP [4 Press Releases]

+ COOLEY LLP VETHOS ANNOUNCES $200 MILLION IPO

Cooley advised Ethos Technologies, a top life insurance technology company on a mission to democratize access to life insurance, on its $200 million initial public offering (IPO). Ethos offered 5,127,696 shares of Class A common stock, and selling stockholders offered an additional 5,398,619 shares of Class A common stock, at a price of $19 per share. Ethos began trading on the Nasdaq Global Select Market on January 29, 2026, under the ticker symbol LIFE.

Partner Matthew Bartus led the Cooley team advising Ethos since its formation in 2016, including on its $100 million Series D-1 in July 2021 and $200 million Series D in May 2021.

Lawyers Jon Avina, Jean Park, Milson Yu, Samara Zaifman, Katie Lapidus and Charlotte Yin led the Cooley team advising Ethos on its IPO.

Source: Company Website

+ COOLEY LLP INFERACT ANNOUNCES $150 MILLION SEED FINANCING

Cooley advised Inferact, a startup founded by creators and core maintainers of the open-source project vLLM, on its $150 million seed financing at an $800 million valuation. The financing was co-led by Andreessen Horowitz and Lightspeed Venture Partners, with investments from Sequoia Capital, Altimeter Capital, Redpoint Ventures, ZhenFund, The House Fund, Striker Venture Partners, Laude Ventures, Databricks Ventures and the UC Berkeley Chancellor's Fund.

Lawyers Peter Werner, Tara Capsuto, Won Lee, Carl Min and Isabella Ysebrands led the Cooley team advising Inferact.

Source: Company Website

+ COOLEY LLP NEUROPHOS SECURES $110 MILLION SERIES A

Cooley advised Neurophos, a leader in photonic artificial intelligence chip technology, on its oversubscribed $110 million Series A round, bringing total funding to $118 million. The new funding will accelerate the delivery of Neurophos' first integrated photonic compute system, including datacenter-ready optical processing unit modules, a full software stack and early access developer hardware.

The round was led by Gates Frontier, with participation from M12 (Microsoft's venture fund), Carbon Direct Capital, Aramco Ventures, Bosch Ventures, Tectonic Ventures, Space Capital and others.

Lawyers James Allen, Nick Hobson, James Reizman and Nathan Alamillo led the Cooley team advising Neurophos.

Source: Company Website

+ COOLEY LLP OPENEVIDENCE RAISES $250 MILLION SERIES D

Cooley advised OpenEvidence, the most widely used artificial intelligence (AI) platform by doctors in America, on its $250 million Series D financing, valuing the company at $12 billion and making it the most valuable healthcare AI company in the world. The new funding will be used to invest heavily in the research and development and compute costs associated with the multi-AI agentic architecture of OpenEvidence, which provides the highest quality and most accurate medical answers of any system in the world.

The round was co-led by Thrive Capital and DST Global, and brings the total funding raised by OpenEvidence to nearly $700 million over the past year.

Lawyers Derek Colla, David Safren and Sela Dougherty led the Cooley team advising OpenEvidence.

Source: Company Website

K&L Gates is pleased to announce that Singapore Office Managing Partner Raja Bose and Singapore Corporate partner Meraj Noor have once again been named to India Business Law Journal's (IBLJ) International A-List. This marks the eighth consecutive year both partners have received this recognition.

IBLJ's International A-List identifies the top lawyers based outside of India who are recognized for their work on India-related matters.
