value.Title: POLITICO: NY GOVERNOR BACKS OFF AGGRESSIVE CLIMATE TIMELINE.(NEWS)
value.Overview: Fuel Oil News ASAP, Pg. 28; ISSN: 0016-2396; Vol. 91; No. 5", 297words

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JMK-1RP3-RRR0-C4VH-00000-00POLITICO: NY GOVERNOR BACKS OFF AGGRESSIVE CLIMATE TIMELINE.(NEWS)2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:02ZLexisNexisPg. 28POLITICO: NY GOVERNOR BACKS OFF AGGRESSIVE CLIMATE TIMELINE.(NEWS)

Gov. Kathy Hochul said she wants to rewrite New York's climate law, arguing the state needs more time to meet its aggressive goals amid rising energy prices and a legal challenge, reported Marie J. French for Politico. The governor spoke at Politico's New York Agenda: Albany Summit. "We just need some breathing room," Hochul said at the Summit, according to Politico.

"Back when the law was passed in 2019, it was a very different world," Hochul said according to a transcript issued by the governor's office. "It was a world that had not seen a global pandemic, that disrupted supply chains for all the component parts, for the nacelles and the wind turbines and everything you need to be able to build offshore wind... Could not have foreseen that. Followed by some of the highest inflation we had seen in years jacking up the cost once again. And then let's throw on a hostile, very hostile administration in Washington that basically eliminates the tax incentives that businesses count on when they're going to make investments in renewable energy--solar and wind being the top of the list.

"My first conversations with Donald Trump in the White House were all about how he hates offshore wind--worried about the birds and the whales, and it was like, really? All of a sudden, you're a nature lover? This is new to me. So I had some real fights just to get offshore wind turned back on in New York State.

"So, what we didn't have was a pandemic, inflation, lack of support from the federal government, which had been there before when this was all enacted, and tariffs. So, I'm trying to create an environment that'll adhere to those goals. Who does not want to protect our environment and our climate? Absolutely. I just can't undo what has happened since those were put in place."

value.Title: Interstate Waste Foresees Potential Exit in Two to Three Years

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JMB-3723-RS7Y-J01N-00000-00Interstate Waste Foresees Potential Exit in Two to Three Years2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:59:47ZLexisNexisInterstate Waste Foresees Potential Exit in Two to Three Yearsby Sam Weisberg

Interstate Waste Services Inc., backed by Littlejohn & Co. LLC and the private equity unit of Ares Management Corp., foresees a potential exit window of 24 to 36 months, said executive chairman Brian D'Amico.The Teaneck, N.J.-based business, with slightly more than $1 billion in annual revenue, doesn't anticipate a sale happening before most or all of the New York City zones it operates in are fully implemented into the NYC Department of Sanitation's Commercial Waste Zones, or CWZ, program, he explained.Via the program, IWS, which provides solid waste collection, transfer, recycling, and disposal services, has been awarded to be an authorized waste carter within 15 zones citywide, through its subsidiary Action Environmental Services Inc. Four of these zones have "gone live" - or are now complying with the program's mandates to reduce waste truck traffic and emissions and offer more efficient and safer recycling and organic waste treatment, among other functions, D'Amico noted on the sidelines of the ONE Houlihan Lokey Global Conference on Tuesday.Ideally, IWS will be close to realizing its CWZ goals by the time it exits, said D'Amico, though he added that a deal could happen sooner depending on market conditions.For the time being, one of its key goals is to keep executing on "mining the waste streams we collect every day and creating renewable gases," he said.IWS's services include converting landfill methanes and food waste into renewable energy.He acknowledged that many large infrastructure investors could be interested in buying IWS as "there are not a lot of platforms of our size."Ares and Littlejohn declined to be interviewed for this article.Meanwhile, IWS is actively seeking acquisitions, with "a handful of deals" signed, said D'Amico.

It is looking at bolt-on targets with $50 million to $100 million in annual revenue as well as tuck-ins with $20 million or less in revenue, to build density in existing markets. Targets could include hauling businesses with or without transfer stations, he added.Action has made 11 recent acquisitions in NYC, D'Amico said, adding that it will keep buying companies based in the city to maintain its strong standing in the CWZ program. NYC businesses that failed to win contracts in their zones are "looking to sell, and we're taking advantage of that," D'Amico noted.However, one of the waste management groups it bought - Filco Carting Corp., in December - had been awarded three zones in Manhattan.Last month, Action was named a designated hauler for the Midtown South Commercial Waste Zone, ensuring that new as well as existing customers in the zone, including the Ritz-Carlton Hotel, will continue to use Action as a provider.Outside of NYC, IWS operates in Connecticut, Central and Northern New Jersey, and N.Y.'s Westchester and Hudson Valley regions. IWS wants to expand into the Southern New Jersey and Philadelphia markets, as well as Northern and Eastern Connecticut, as it primarily services other regions of that state, said the chairman. It could achieve this goal via acquisitions, he added.IWS doesn't use an outside financial adviser for add-ons. Its law firm is Kirkland & Ellis LLP, said D'Amico.In early 2020, Action, then owned by Summer Street Capital Partners, LLC - where D'Amico is still a managing partner - became part of Summer Street-backed IWS via its merger with Prophet Equity-backed Apex Environmental. Littlejohn led the equity investment in IWS, with Summer Street, Prophet, and IWS management retaining significant stakes. Co-investor O-Corp Investments, LLC, also took a position in IWS.In October 2023, Ares' funds became co-owners of IWS with Littlejohn. IWS also completed a debt refinancing with a JP Morgan-led arranger group. Summer Street no longer holds a position in IWS, although Prophet and O-Corp remain as shareholders, according to D'Amico.Brown Gibbons Lang & Co. and Houlihan Lokey Inc. were financial advisers to IWS on the 2023 recap. The company used Sheppard, Mullin, Richter & Hampton LLP as legal counsel. Robert W. Baird & Co. was exclusive financial adviser and Kirkland & Ellis LLP was legal counsel to Ares.

DEAL SIZE

$ 1-10 Billion

value.Title: US Likely to Drop Fraud Charges Against Gautam Adani, Settle SEC Suit: Report

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK4-77B3-RT89-V146-00000-00US Likely to Drop Fraud Charges Against Gautam Adani, Settle SEC Suit: Report2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:02ZLexisNexisUS NEWSUS Likely to Drop Fraud Charges Against Gautam Adani, Settle SEC Suit: ReportTimes Now Digital

The US Justice Department is preparing to drop criminal fraud and bribery charges against Indian billionaire Gautam Adani, linked to an alleged $265 million corruption scheme tied to India's largest solar power project, according to a Bloomberg report. The development could bring significant relief to the Adani Group chairman more than a year after US prosecutors in New York accused him and other executives of orchestrating a multibillion-dollar corruption network.


Details of the Allegations

The charges were announced in November 2024. Prosecutors alleged that Adani, his nephew Sagar Adani, and several others participated in a bribery scheme involving about $265 million in payments to Indian government officials. The bribes were allegedly made to secure contracts expected to generate nearly $2 billion in profits over two decades for a major solar power project.
US authorities also accused the Adanis and former Adani Green Energy CEO Vneet Jaain of raising more than $3 billion through loans and bond offerings while concealing the alleged corruption from lenders and investors. The three faced charges of securities fraud, securities fraud conspiracy, and wire fraud conspiracy. The Adanis and others were also named in a parallel civil fraud case by the US Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC).At the time, none of the accused were in US custody. Arrest warrants were issued, and authorities planned to work with foreign law enforcement to apprehend them.
Current Status

According to people familiar with the matter cited by Bloomberg, the Justice Department is now preparing to withdraw the criminal charges as early as this week. The SEC is also moving toward settling the parallel civil case. The remaining defendants included former Azure Power Global executives Ranjit Gupta and Rupesh Agarwal, along with executives linked to a Canadian institutional investor. This is a developing story. Neither the US Justice Department nor the Adani Group has issued an official comment on the Bloomberg report so far.

value.Title: PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP - THE RECENT IN THE SECTOR

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK3-D173-RX2K-114G-00000-00PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP - THE RECENT IN THE SECTOR2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:54:14ZLexisNexisSTOCKPILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP - THE RECENT IN THE SECTOR

DASHBOARD: PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP

PermID4296383614Websitewww.pillsburylaw.com/en/IndustryFull-Service BigLawAddress31 West 52nd Street, New York, NY 10019 USA

ACTIVITIES:

Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP, founded in 1868, is a global law firm headquartered in New York City. With over 700 lawyers across 20 offices, it serves clients in energy, technology, and finance, specializing in M&A, regulatory, and litigation. Generating over $700 million annually, Pillsbury advised on 2024 renewable energy transactions, recognized by Columbia Law School. Its culture emphasizes innovation, diversity, and pro bono work, with strong associate training programs. Competing with Baker McKenzie, Pillsbury stands out for its energy and tech regulatory expertise. In 2024, it expanded its AI and ESG practices, focusing on sustainable finance. Recent initiatives include cloud-based legal tech and partnerships with nonprofits like Lawyers Alliance for New York, reinforcing its community impact.

+ PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP [2 News That Matters]

+ PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN EXPANDS TO BOSTON WITH NEW OFFICE LAUNCH

Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman is set to open an office in Boston, expanding its footprint in one of the largest legal and business markets in the United States. On 12th May 2026, the firm announced that it will commence operations with a team of five senior lawyers, including a notable litigator hired from Latham & Watkins.Robert Gilbert, who joined Pillsbury after a seven-year tenure at Latham, specializes in representing policyholders in complex insurance recovery matters. He has previously acted for prominent Boston-area clients such as Harvard University, UniFirst Corporation, and Northland Investment Corporation. Gilbert will be instrumental in launching the Boston office alongside key figures from Pillsbury's leadership, including Michael Zeliger, Andrew Troop, Don Lonczak, and David Simons.David Dekker, chair and CEO of Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman, emphasized the strategic importance of the Boston office, citing the city's dynamic start-up ecosystem, world-class research institutions, and a concentration of financial services companies and private capital investors. He remarked, "Pillsbury has long represented clients headquartered in the Boston area and those connected to the Boston-Cambridge innovation ecosystem. Opening a Boston office is a natural next step given our capabilities and relationships in the tech, life sciences, healthcare, and financial industries in Boston and around the world."This new office marks Pillsbury’s 17th location in the U.S. and its 25th globally. The firm joins a growing list of major law firms that have established a presence in Boston, aiming to leverage the city's thriving technology and life sciences sectors, as well as its expanding financial services market. Recent entrants include Reed Smith, which opened in March, along with a slew of other firms like Freshfields and Simpson Thacher & Bartlett.With its Boston office, Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman is poised to deepen its engagement with local clients and industries, further solidifying its role in the evolving legal landscape of the region.

+ MORGAN LEWIS STRENGTHENS EMPLOYEE BENEFITS TEAM WITH PILLSBURY TALENT

Morgan Lewis & Bockius LLP has announced the addition of a prominent attorney from Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP to its Employee Benefits team. This strategic move is seen as a response to the growing complexities in employee benefits law and the increasing demand for expert legal guidance in this area. The attorney, whose experience spans multiple facets of employee benefits, is expected to enhance Morgan Lewis’ capabilities in providing comprehensive legal services to its clients.The announcement comes as Morgan Lewis looks to bolster its offerings in employee benefits law, particularly in the wake of several recent regulatory changes. The attorney's arrival on 08th April 2026 is intended to strengthen the firm's position in an increasingly competitive legal landscape. Morgan Lewis has consistently focused on expanding its practice areas to meet client needs, and this latest addition is part of that ongoing strategy.Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman, known for its robust practice in employee benefits, has seen a number of its attorneys transition to other firms in recent years. The departing attorney brings a wealth of experience in compliance and regulatory issues, which will be invaluable to Morgan Lewis as it navigates the complexities of employee benefit plans and pension laws. This move not only reflects the attorney's individual career advancement but also highlights the fluid nature of talent within the legal sector.Morgan Lewis has a reputation for its deep expertise in various practice areas, and the inclusion of the Pillsbury attorney is expected to further enhance its service offerings. The firm has been proactive in adapting to changes in legislation affecting employee benefits, ensuring that its clients remain compliant and well-informed. By integrating new talent, Morgan Lewis aims to provide even more specialized services tailored to the evolving needs of its diverse client base.As the legal field continues to evolve, the strategic hiring of top talent from firms like Pillsbury is critical for maintaining a competitive edge. Morgan Lewis’ latest hiring signals its commitment to delivering high-quality legal solutions in employee benefits, reinforcing its reputation as a leading firm in this practice area. The legal community will be watching closely to see how this new addition impacts Morgan Lewis’ capabilities and client service in the months ahead.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ PERKINS COIE [3 News That Matters]

+ ASHURST PERKINS COIE MERGER GAINS STRONG SUPPORT FROM PARTNERS

The merger of global law firms Ashurst and Perkins Coie into the entity Ashurst Perkins Coie has received overwhelming support from the partnerships of both firms. This decision was finalized following a consultation period, with the approval announced on 13th April 2026. The merger is expected to close in the third quarter of the year, contingent upon customary closing conditions.Mark Birnbaum, executive committee chair of Perkins Coie, emphasized the significance of this approval, stating that it reflects the partners' commitment to creating a forward-looking firm. He noted that the merger underlines a shared confidence in their capacity to grow and deliver exceptional legal services in an evolving global market. Paul Jenkins, CEO of Ashurst, also highlighted the alignment between the two firms, asserting that their combined strengths will benefit their clients significantly.The merged firm aims to focus on key sectors including technology, energy, infrastructure, and financial services. Bill Malley, firmwide managing partner at Perkins Coie, expressed optimism that the combination would enhance the firms' abilities to meet clients’ complex cross-border legal needs. The new entity is set to boast approximately 3,000 lawyers across 52 offices in 32 countries, with major hubs located in Seattle, London, Sydney, and New York.The merger, first announced last year, brings together two firms with combined revenues of around USD 2.8 billion. Their lawyers have extensive experience in international markets, having worked in cities such as Brussels, Dubai, Frankfurt, Hong Kong, Paris, Seoul, Shenzhen, Singapore, and Tokyo. The leadership of the new firm will be shared by Malley and Jenkins as global co-CEOs, while Ashurst's global chair, Karen Davies, and Perkins Coie's corporate practice chair, Brian Eiting, will serve as co-chairs.As the legal landscape continues to evolve, the Ashurst Perkins Coie combination is poised to enhance its competitive edge by leveraging the strengths of both firms. This strategic move not only positions the new entity to better serve its clients but also sets the stage for sustained growth and performance in the legal industry.

+ PERKINS COIE AND ASHURST UNITE TO FORM ASHURST PERKINS COIE

NEW YORK — Perkins Coie and Ashurst announced today that the partnerships of both firms have voted overwhelmingly to approve their proposed combination. The approval follows consultation from both firms and reflects strong support for the combination of two law firms with highly complementary geographic reach, sector depth, and practice strength. The date of this announcement is recorded as 2026-04-14 16:18:02.950901.Upon closing, Ashurst Perkins Coie will emerge as a globally integrated firm with combined revenues of approximately US$2.8 billion. The new entity will boast around 3,000 lawyers across more than 50 offices, including flagship hubs in Seattle, London, Sydney, and New York. Ashurst Perkins Coie will be particularly noted for its strengths in the technology, energy and infrastructure, and financial services sectors—industries that are pivotal to the ongoing global economic transformation.Bill Malley, the Firmwide Managing Partner at Perkins Coie, remarked on the significance of this merger by stating, 'Our partnership's approval reflects a shared belief that combining to form Ashurst Perkins Coie will create a truly differentiated global legal platform—one with the scale, sector depth, and technological leadership to meet our clients' increasingly complex, cross-border needs.' This sentiment underscores the strategic vision behind the combination.Paul Jenkins, Global CEO of Ashurst, echoed Malley’s sentiments, emphasizing the strong alignment between the two firms. 'This vote confirms the strong alignment between our firms and our joint ambition for the future,' he stated, highlighting the complementary expertise across sectors and practice areas that will benefit clients. Jenkins added that the merger will create a compelling platform for top legal talent.The combination remains subject to customary closing conditions and is expected to finalize in the third quarter of 2026. As the legal landscape becomes more complex, Ashurst Perkins Coie aims to position itself as a trusted advisor, ready to navigate the intricacies of the global market and deliver exceptional legal services.

+ PERKINS COIE REENTERS CHINA MARKET WITH NEW SHENZHEN OFFICE

Perkins Coie has officially opened a representative office in Shenzhen, marking the firm's significant comeback in the Chinese market. This development comes on the heels of a strategic decision to re-establish an on-the-ground presence in the region, with the office inaugurated on 14th April 2026.The Seattle-based law firm had previously operated offices in Beijing and Shanghai, which were closed in 2024. At that time, Perkins Coie indicated plans to pivot towards Shenzhen, citing the city’s rapid growth as a key tech and manufacturing hub. The firm aims to leverage this new office to enhance its support for Asia-based companies navigating US and cross-border legal needs.Leading the Shenzhen office will be Bing Ai, serving as chief representative and managing partner, along with Wei Yuan, who will act as a representative and litigation partner. Both lawyers are US-trained and bring extensive experience in advising technology-focused clients. Ai specializes in patent procurement and portfolio management in sectors such as semiconductors and medical devices, while Yuan is adept at handling patent infringement disputes.Bill Malley, Perkins Coie’s managing partner, emphasized the firm's commitment to providing US legal expertise across borders. He noted that the opening of the Shenzhen office reflects a dedication to assisting clients in navigating the complexities of global markets, especially in light of the increasing challenges they face.As Perkins Coie re-establishes its presence in mainland China, it stands apart from the trend of office closures and headcount reductions by many leading US firms due to geopolitical tensions and regulatory challenges. However, several international firms have recently opened offices in Shenzhen, attracted by its status within the Greater Bay Area, which has seen substantial economic growth. Perkins Coie’s strategic move comes as the firm prepares for a potential merger with UK-based Ashurst, which would create a top 20 global firm with projected annual revenues of around $2.7 billion.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ COOLEY LLP [2 News That Matters]

+ CROSBY SECURES $60M IN FUNDING, BACKED BY COOLEY LLP

Contract review start-up Crosby has successfully raised $60 million in its latest funding round, marking its third capital raise in less than a year. The funding announcement was made on 3rd April 2026, highlighting the firm’s rapid growth and the interest from prominent investors, including the West Coast-based law firm Cooley LLP.The Series B round was co-led by Lux Capital and Index Ventures, with participation from renowned venture capital firms such as Sequoia, 01 Advisors, and Bain Capital Ventures, as well as angel investor Elad Gil. With this new influx of capital, Crosby plans to enhance its technology capabilities and provide better support to its growing customer base, which includes notable software development firms Clay and Rogo, as well as the financial services operations platform Ramp.Crosby combines artificial intelligence with legal expertise to expedite the contract review process. The firm reported a significant increase in the value of its negotiated contracts, which has soared to just over $1 billion. This is a remarkable rise from the $30 million in brokered contracts when Crosby emerged from stealth mode last year, showcasing the firm’s impressive trajectory in the legal tech space.In a recent blog post, Crosby emphasized its commitment to innovation, stating, 'As we’ve scaled, we’ve also started experimenting with entirely new technology experiences.' The firm has introduced various features, including simulations of counterparty responses to contract redlines, voice agents that negotiate on behalf of clients, and a platform that allows clients to oversee and provide input on Crosby’s work.Founded in 2024 by Ryan Daniels and John Sarihan, Crosby has quickly established itself in the legal tech landscape. Daniels, a former associate at Cooley, has an extensive background in tech entrepreneurship, while Sarihan brings experience as a former Google software engineer and tech lead for Crosby client Ramp. Together, they are steering Crosby towards a future of enhanced contract negotiation and review, supported by substantial investments from Cooley and other leading firms.

+ COOLEY LLP ENHANCES TECH PRACTICE WITH FRESHFIELDS ALUMNI

Cooley LLP has announced a significant expansion of its technology practice, bringing on board several attorneys who previously worked at Freshfields Bruckhaus Deringer. This strategic move, aimed at bolstering Cooley's capabilities in the tech sector, highlights the firm's commitment to remaining competitive in an increasingly dynamic legal landscape.As of 23rd November 2025, Cooley's latest recruitment drive underscores the firm's ongoing efforts to enhance its service offerings in technology, a sector that continues to evolve rapidly. + MORRISON & FOERSTER ANALYZES SENATE'S BAN ON PREDICTION MARKET TRADING

In a significant move for the financial markets, the Senate has unanimously approved a resolution banning Senators and their staff from participating in prediction markets. This decision was made on 29th April 2026, reflecting growing concerns about the integrity of financial and political processes. Morrison & Foerster observes that this initiative is part of a broader effort to regulate prediction markets, which have garnered both interest and skepticism in recent years.Hours after the Senate resolution, Senators Kristen Gillibrand (D-NY) and Dave McCormick (R-PA) introduced legislation aimed at preventing government officials from trading on prediction markets. The proposed bill seeks to establish standards on insider trading, implement anti-money laundering measures, and create an independent Office of the Retail Advocate within the CFTC. Morrison & Foerster notes that these changes are likely to reshape the landscape for prediction markets, emphasizing consumer protection.In a related development, Kalshi, a regulated exchange for prediction markets, announced new features designed to restrict underage access to its platform. The measures include mandatory face ID for users and two-factor authentication for parents. Kalshi has stated that these updates align with the legislative framework proposed by Senators Gillibrand and McCormick, highlighting the proactive approach the company is taking in response to regulatory changes. Morrison & Foerster suggests that such measures could foster greater trust in prediction markets among consumers.The Senate Commerce, Science, and Transportation Committee is set to hold a hearing on 20th May 2026, addressing issues surrounding prediction markets and sports betting. Former Representative Patrick McHenry will testify alongside industry representatives, making this an important event for stakeholders in both sectors. Morrison & Foerster emphasizes that the outcomes of this hearing could influence future regulations affecting prediction markets.Additionally, CFTC Chairman Michael Selig has defended the commission's exclusive jurisdiction over prediction markets, countering claims of lax regulation. In a letter to The Wall Street Journal, Selig described prediction markets as innovative financial products rather than mere gambling sites. Morrison & Foerster highlights that this defense is crucial for maintaining the legitimacy of prediction markets amid increasing scrutiny from lawmakers and the public.

This new consumer-focused fund represents a significant development in the private equity landscape, with Goodwin Procter providing essential legal support throughout the fundraising process.On April 15th, 2026, Topspin announced the conclusion of its latest fund, which has been designed to capitalize on emerging opportunities within the consumer sector. The legal expertise from Goodwin Procter has been instrumental in navigating the complexities of fundraising, ensuring compliance with regulatory standards and streamlining the investment process for potential investors.The consumer-focused fund is expected to target a broad array of investment opportunities, emphasizing sectors that are experiencing robust growth. Topspin Consumer Partners, leveraging the guidance and support of Goodwin Procter, aims to identify and invest in innovative companies that are well-positioned to thrive in a rapidly evolving marketplace. This strategic focus aligns with current trends indicating a shift towards consumer-centric investments.Goodwin Procter’s involvement not only underscores its reputation as a leading law firm in private equity but also highlights its commitment to supporting clients in realizing their investment objectives. The firm’s adept handling of legal frameworks and partnership structures is expected to enhance the operational efficiency of Topspin’s new fund, thus improving investor confidence.As the market for consumer investments continues to expand, the collaboration between Goodwin Procter and Topspin is poised to set a precedent for future funds. Investors are keenly observing how this partnership will navigate the competitive landscape and deliver returns, with implications for the broader investment community.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ ORRICK, HERRINGTON & SUTCLIFFE LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ ORRICK EXPANDS TECH COMPANIES PLATFORM WITH NEW EMPLOYMENT PARTNER

Orrick, Herrington & Sutcliffe has announced the addition of employment advisor Sarah Tauman as a partner in the firm’s California Employment team based in Sacramento. As of 12th May 2026, Tauman will focus on advising venture-backed technology companies on employment matters as they scale operations and expand their businesses.Tauman’s extensive experience spans various sectors, including health tech, fintech, and consumer tech companies. She also counsels clients on the employment-related aspects of mergers and acquisitions, as well as initial public offerings, which are critical services for companies navigating complex growth trajectories.Her arrival marks the third significant addition to Orrick’s Tech Companies platform in 2026, following the recent hires of venture capital advisor Agatha Kluk in New York and intellectual property licensing and transactions advisor Gina Marek in Silicon Valley. This strategic expansion underscores Orrick’s commitment to meeting the increasing client demand for employment counseling services among emerging growth and technology firms.The firm’s California Employment practice is recognized as Band 1 by Chambers, a testament to its comprehensive approach and industry expertise. Julie Totten, Leader of Global Employment Law and Litigation Practice at Orrick, emphasized the demand for employment counseling at all stages of business growth, expressing enthusiasm about adding another top talent to their roster.In her own words, Tauman stated, 'Orrick is known for leadership in Tech and Employment, and particularly for innovation. I’m excited to be joining such a highly regarded team, and I look forward to collaborating to serve our clients.' Her addition is expected to enhance Orrick's capabilities in providing tailored employment solutions to its technology sector clients.

+ ORRICK WELCOMES SENIOR IP ATTORNEY FROM USPTO

Orrick, Herrington & Sutcliffe has strengthened its intellectual property (IP) practice by hiring a senior attorney from the U.S. Patent and Trademark Office (USPTO). This strategic addition is expected to enhance Orrick's capabilities in navigating complex patent issues, particularly as the firm continues to expand its footprint in the technology and telecommunications sectors.The new hire will officially join the firm on the 28th of April, 2026, bringing with them a wealth of experience from their tenure at the USPTO. With an extensive background in patent examination and litigation, the attorney's expertise aligns well with Orrick's focus on providing comprehensive legal solutions to its clients in rapidly evolving industries.Orrick's managing partner commented on the significance of this hire, noting that the firm's commitment to offering top-tier IP services is underscored by the addition of professionals who possess a deep understanding of the intricacies of patent law. This move is seen as part of Orrick's broader strategy to leverage the changing landscape of intellectual property rights in the digital age.The firm's IP practice has been gaining momentum in recent years, particularly in light of increasing demand for legal counsel in technology-driven sectors. By enhancing its team with experienced attorneys from key regulatory bodies like the USPTO, Orrick is well-positioned to meet the needs of clients facing complex challenges in patent law.As Orrick continues to attract talent from leading institutions, the legal community is keenly observing how these strategic hires will influence the firm's growth trajectory and its ability to provide innovative legal solutions. Investors and clients alike are likely to be interested in the potential impact of this development on Orrick's competitive standing in the market.

+ ORRICK WELCOMES YOUTH ONLINE SAFETY EXPERT NICOLE LOPEZ

Orrick, Herrington & Sutcliffe has announced the addition of Nicole Lopez, a leading authority in youth online safety policy and litigation, to its team. This strategic move comes at a crucial time as the firm aims to enhance its capabilities in navigating the evolving landscape of online safety. On 28th April 2026, Lopez expressed her enthusiasm for the new role and the opportunity to make a broader impact in the field.Lopez, who previously held a significant position at Meta, emphasized the importance of collaboration in her new role at Orrick. "I am so immensely proud of the pioneering work we did at Meta as a team, where I worked with incredibly talented colleagues who cared so deeply about getting it right," she stated. Her experience and expertise will be invaluable as Orrick seeks to provide comprehensive solutions for its clients facing increasing scrutiny over online safety issues.The urgency of youth online safety has escalated, with 25 state legislatures actively considering various forms of legislation aimed at safeguarding minors in digital environments. Jeremy Kudon, who leads Orrick’s State Legislative practice, noted, "We are facing a watershed moment. Online safety is top of mind for innovators, government, teens and parents, and society at large." Lopez's leadership in this conversation positions Orrick to effectively guide clients through the legislative maze.Aravind Swaminathan, Orrick’s Tech Sector Co-Leader, highlighted Lopez's multifaceted expertise, stating, "Nicole is part policy expert, part litigation and regulatory strategist, and part business advisor – she will be an extraordinary resource to our clients." As artificial intelligence continues to reshape the digital landscape, the need for proactive measures in youth safety is more pressing than ever, and Orrick is poised to be at the forefront of these changes.Orrick's commitment to developing robust online safety solutions is further exemplified by its Online Safety Law Center, which aims to keep clients informed of rapid developments in this area. With Nicole Lopez joining the team, the firm is well-positioned to advocate for effective policies and practices that balance innovation and child protection, ensuring that both companies and young users can thrive in a safer online environment.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

value.Title: New ACORE Investor Survey Report Kicks Off 2026 Finance Forum

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK3-3693-S8WH-T450-00000-00New ACORE Investor Survey Report Kicks Off 2026 Finance Forum2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:02ZLexisNexisNew ACORE Investor Survey Report Kicks Off 2026 Finance Forum

Key Highlights:

* Clean energy investors plan to increase investments in 2026 despite policy and regulatory uncertainty.

* Utility-scale solar and energy storage are the most attractive clean energy technologies for investment.

* Policy and investment uncertainty clouds the trajectory post-2026 for clean energy infrastructure development.

Original Press Release:

New York City, May 13 -- American Council on Renewable Energy issued the following news release:

A new report from ACORE shows that clean energy investors and developers largely plan to increase their investments in 2026 but cite policy, regulatory, and interconnection uncertainty as the biggest risks to their investment strategy after this year.

In its Navigating Uncertainty: Clean Energy Investment Trends (2026-2029) report, ACORE shares market sentiment analysis gathered from surveys of 36 leaders at U.S. and multinational companies that invested billions in the U.S. clean energy market in 2025.

Topline takeaways from the report include:

* Respondents identified federal regulatory and policy risks and interconnection uncertainty and costs as the top risks facing clean energy investments.

* Capital providers continue to view utility-scale solar and energy storage as the two most attractive clean energy technologies for investment.

* Despite declining attractiveness of the U.S. as a venue for clean energy investment compared to previous years, respondents said they plan to develop and finance more American clean energy projects in 2026 than they did in 2025.

* Policy and investment uncertainty clouds the trajectory post-2026, with the potential for additional roadblocks to financing and developing clean energy infrastructure.

This report complements the Clean Energy Investment Trends report released last month that S&P Global prepared for ACORE.

"ACORE's recent reports highlight a common thread: the U.S. clean energy sector remains capitalized and ready to help deliver electricity reliability and affordability for American consumers," said ACORE President and CEO Ray Long. "Our sector is thriving and poised to meet this moment of significant electricity demand growth, but investors and developers need policy certainty to deliver on this critical infrastructure for American energy security."

ACORE released the report at its annual Finance Forum in New York City today and discussed the takeaways during the opening panel with ACORE Senior Vice President for Policy Lesley Hunter, Avangrid CEO Jose Antonio Miranda, and S&P Global CERA Consulting Director Christopher Wilfong.

Please email communications@acore.org if you'd like to view the recording of the first panel or set up an interview with ACORE about the report. Register here to tune in to the other panels.

ACORE will host a member-only webinar to discuss both reports on May 21, 2026. Learn more about becoming an ACORE member here.

About ACORE:

ACORE is a nonpartisan nonprofit organization that operates at the intersection of affordability, reliability, and clean energy deployment. Our work is focused on stabilizing energy prices, strengthening the electric grid, and driving investment in cost-effective technologies to ensure that clean energy delivers for people, businesses, and the U.S. economy.

ACORE's membership includes clean energy investors, developers, energy buyers, power generators, manufacturers, and energy providers. In 2024, nearly 80% of the booming utility-scale domestic clean energy growth was financed, developed, owned, equipped, or contracted by ACORE members.

[Category: Banking and Financial Services, Industrial Gases, Renewable Energy, Events and Conferences, Regulatory and Policy Developments, Sentiment]

Source: American Council on Renewable Energy

value.Title: Equinor ships 5,000th oil cargo from Gullfaks field in North Sea
value.Overview: Trend Oil & Gas - Azerbaijan, SHIPPING INDUSTRY NEWS, SHIPPING INDUSTRY NEWS & NORWAY NEWS, 372words

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJW-MHK3-RX79-R4C0-00000-00Equinor ships 5,000th oil cargo from Gullfaks field in North Sea2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:02ZLexisNexisSHIPPING INDUSTRY NEWS, SHIPPING INDUSTRY NEWS & NORWAY NEWSEquinor ships 5,000th oil cargo from Gullfaks field in North Sea

BAKU, Azerbaijan, May 13. Equinor has announced
that the 5,000th oil cargo has been exported from the Gullfaks
field in the Norwegian sector of the North Sea, marking another
milestone in the field’s long production history, Trend
reports.

When production began in 1986, Gullfaks was originally expected
to remain operational until 2007. However, more than two decades
later, the field continues to deliver weekly shipments to
international markets, with the majority of volumes supplied to
Europe.

Since start-up, Gullfaks has produced approximately 2.8 billion
barrels of oil equivalent, nearly double initial estimates. The
field also functions as an export hub for production from nearby
assets, including the Snorre and Visund fields, as well as multiple
subsea developments in the region.

Gullfaks crude is characterized by its light quality and low
sulphur content, making it suitable for refining into high-value
products such as gasoline, diesel, jet fuel, LPG and naphtha.
Deliveries are made to refineries across Sweden, the United Kingdom
and Poland, supporting fuel supply chains for transport, industry
and households.

The 5,000th cargo was delivered to the St1 refinery in
Gothenburg, where it is processed into a range of fuels distributed
through approximately 1,150 retail stations across Finland, Sweden
and Norway. Additional shipments are also sent to refineries in the
UK and Poland, as well as occasionally to Equinor’s Mongstad
refinery in Norway.

Oil from Gullfaks is transported using shuttle tankers with a
capacity of around 800,000 barrels, enabling flexible logistics
between the Norwegian continental shelf and European buyers.

Gullfaks production began with the Gullfaks A platform in 1986,
followed by Gullfaks B in 1988 and Gullfaks C in 1989. The field
ranks as Norway’s seventh-largest oil and gas field by original
reserves, following giants such as Troll, Statfjord, Ekofisk,
Oseberg, Åsgard and Johan Sverdrup.

Production peaked in 1994, while last year the field and its
associated subsea assets produced just over 17 million barrels of
oil. Part of the platform’s energy needs are now supplied by
electricity from the floating offshore wind farm Hywind Tampen,
reflecting growing integration of renewable power in offshore
operations.

value.Title: INVESTOR ALERT: Pomerantz Law Firm Investigates Claims On Behalf Of Investors Of Primoris Services Corporation - PRIM

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK8-3TC3-SBJX-V3X9-00000-00INVESTOR ALERT: Pomerantz Law Firm Investigates Claims On Behalf Of Investors Of Primoris Services Corporation - PRIM2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:02ZLexisNexisINVESTOR ALERT: Pomerantz Law Firm Investigates Claims On Behalf Of Investors Of Primoris Services Corporation - PRIM

NEW YORK, May 14, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- Pomerantz LLP is investigating claims on behalf of investors of Primoris Services Corporation ("Primoris" or the "Company") (NYSE: PRIM ). Such investors are advised to contact Danielle Peyton at [email protected] or 646-581-9980, ext. 7980.


The investigation concerns whether Primoris and certain of its officers and/or directors have engaged in securities fraud or other unlawful business practices.
[Click here for information about joining the class action]
On May 5, 2026, Primoris issued a press release reporting its financial results for the first quarter of 2026. Primoris reported results below analyst expectations and slashed full-year adjusted EBITDA guidance from $560-$580 million to $480-$500 million. Primoris attributed the reduction to lower renewable energy activity, delayed project starts, and increased costs on renewable energy projects.
On this news, Primoris's stock price fell $101.69 per share, or 50.11%, to close at $101.23 per share on May 6, 2026.
Pomerantz LLP, with offices in New York, Chicago, Los Angeles, London, Paris, and Tel Aviv, is acknowledged as one of the premier firms in the areas of corporate, securities, and antitrust class litigation. Founded by the late Abraham L. Pomerantz, known as the dean of the class action bar, Pomerantz pioneered the field of securities class actions. Today, more than 85 years later, Pomerantz continues in the tradition he established, fighting for the rights of the victims of securities fraud, breaches of fiduciary duty, and corporate misconduct. The Firm has recovered numerous multimillion-dollar damages awards on behalf of class members. See .
Attorney advertising. Prior results do not guarantee similar outcomes.
CONTACT:
Danielle Peyton
Pomerantz LLP
[email protected]
646-581-9980 ext. 7980
SOURCE Pomerantz LLP

value.Title: ACORE Investor Survey Report Kicks Off 2026 Finance Forum

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK3-7V63-RRK5-S3T3-00000-00ACORE Investor Survey Report Kicks Off 2026 Finance Forum2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:02ZLexisNexisACORE Investor Survey Report Kicks Off 2026 Finance ForumTargeted News ServiceWASHINGTON

(TNSrpt) -- The American Council on Renewable Energy issued the following news release on May 13, 2026:

* * *

New ACORE Investor Survey Report Kicks Off 2026 Finance Forum

New York City, NY - A new report from ACORE shows that clean energy investors and developers largely plan to increase their investments in 2026 but cite policy, regulatory, and interconnection uncertainty as the biggest risks to their investment strategy after this year.

In its Navigating Uncertainty: Clean Energy Investment Trends (2026-2029) report, ACORE shares market sentiment analysis gathered from surveys of 36 leaders at U.S. and multinational companies that invested billions in the U.S. clean energy market in 2025.

Topline takeaways from the report include:

* Respondents identified federal regulatory and policy risks and interconnection uncertainty and costs as the top risks facing clean energy investments.

* Capital providers continue to view utility-scale solar and energy storage as the two most attractive clean energy technologies for investment.

* Despite declining attractiveness of the U.S. as a venue for clean energy investment compared to previous years, respondents said they plan to develop and finance more American clean energy projects in 2026 than they did in 2025.

* Policy and investment uncertainty clouds the trajectory post-2026, with the potential for additional roadblocks to financing and developing clean energy infrastructure.

This report complements the Clean Energy Investment Trends report released last month that S&P Global prepared for ACORE.

"ACORE's recent reports highlight a common thread: the U.S. clean energy sector remains capitalized and ready to help deliver electricity reliability and affordability for American consumers," said ACORE President and CEO Ray Long. "Our sector is thriving and poised to meet this moment of significant electricity demand growth, but investors and developers need policy certainty to deliver on this critical infrastructure for American energy security."

ACORE released the report at its annual Finance Forum in New York City today and discussed the takeaways during the opening panel with ACORE Senior Vice President for Policy Lesley Hunter, Avangrid CEO Jose Antonio Miranda, and S&P Global CERA Consulting Director Christopher Wilfong.

Please email communications@acore.org if you'd like to view the recording of the first panel or set up an interview with ACORE about the report. Register here (https://acore.swoogo.com/financeforum/begin?code=Press &mc_cid=1da3252584&mc_eid=UNIQID) to tune in to the other panels.

ACORE will host a member-only webinar to discuss both reports on May 21, 2026. Learn

* * *

About ACORE:

ACORE is a nonpartisan nonprofit organization that operates at the intersection of affordability, reliability, and clean energy deployment. Our work is focused on stabilizing energy prices, strengthening the electric grid, and driving investment in cost-effective technologies to ensure that clean energy delivers for people, businesses, and the U.S. economy.

ACORE's membership includes clean energy investors, developers, energy buyers, power generators, manufacturers, and energy providers. In 2024, nearly 80% of the booming utility-scale domestic clean energy growth was financed, developed, owned, equipped, or contracted by ACORE members.

* * *

REPORT: https://acore.org/wp-content/uploads/2026/05/Report-Navigating-Uncertainty-ACORE-Investment-Survey.pdf

* * *

[Category: Energy]

value.Title: Equinor ships 5,000th oil cargo from Gullfaks field in North Sea
value.Overview: Trend Daily News, SHIPPING INDUSTRY NEWS, SHIPPING INDUSTRY NEWS & NORWAY NEWS, 372words

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJW-MHK3-RX79-R4F3-00000-00Equinor ships 5,000th oil cargo from Gullfaks field in North Sea2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:02ZLexisNexisSHIPPING INDUSTRY NEWS, SHIPPING INDUSTRY NEWS & NORWAY NEWSEquinor ships 5,000th oil cargo from Gullfaks field in North Sea

BAKU, Azerbaijan, May 13. Equinor has announced
that the 5,000th oil cargo has been exported from the Gullfaks
field in the Norwegian sector of the North Sea, marking another
milestone in the field’s long production history, Trend
reports.

When production began in 1986, Gullfaks was originally expected
to remain operational until 2007. However, more than two decades
later, the field continues to deliver weekly shipments to
international markets, with the majority of volumes supplied to
Europe.

Since start-up, Gullfaks has produced approximately 2.8 billion
barrels of oil equivalent, nearly double initial estimates. The
field also functions as an export hub for production from nearby
assets, including the Snorre and Visund fields, as well as multiple
subsea developments in the region.

Gullfaks crude is characterized by its light quality and low
sulphur content, making it suitable for refining into high-value
products such as gasoline, diesel, jet fuel, LPG and naphtha.
Deliveries are made to refineries across Sweden, the United Kingdom
and Poland, supporting fuel supply chains for transport, industry
and households.

The 5,000th cargo was delivered to the St1 refinery in
Gothenburg, where it is processed into a range of fuels distributed
through approximately 1,150 retail stations across Finland, Sweden
and Norway. Additional shipments are also sent to refineries in the
UK and Poland, as well as occasionally to Equinor’s Mongstad
refinery in Norway.

Oil from Gullfaks is transported using shuttle tankers with a
capacity of around 800,000 barrels, enabling flexible logistics
between the Norwegian continental shelf and European buyers.

Gullfaks production began with the Gullfaks A platform in 1986,
followed by Gullfaks B in 1988 and Gullfaks C in 1989. The field
ranks as Norway’s seventh-largest oil and gas field by original
reserves, following giants such as Troll, Statfjord, Ekofisk,
Oseberg, Åsgard and Johan Sverdrup.

Production peaked in 1994, while last year the field and its
associated subsea assets produced just over 17 million barrels of
oil. Part of the platform’s energy needs are now supplied by
electricity from the floating offshore wind farm Hywind Tampen,
reflecting growing integration of renewable power in offshore
operations.

value.Title: April 30, 2026: Luxcara Secures Financing for 83 MW Greek Solar Portfolio with WFW Counsel

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJX-DGD3-RSP4-R3YR-00000-00April 30, 2026: Luxcara Secures Financing for 83 MW Greek Solar Portfolio with WFW Counsel2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:02ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSApril 30, 2026: Luxcara Secures Financing for 83 MW Greek Solar Portfolio with WFW Counsel

Luxcara, the Hamburg-based asset manager focused on energy transition infrastructure, has secured financing for an 83 MW solar photovoltaic portfolio in Greece, with advisory support from the international law firm Watson Farley & Williams (WFW). The transaction took place on 8th May 2026 and underscores Luxcara's commitment to scaling renewables across Europe.

The plants will deploy about 130,000 PV modules mounted on single-axis trackers to optimize output, and are being developed by Ameresco Sunel Energy S.A. The project strengthens Greece’s renewable energy transition and signals ongoing investor confidence in high-quality energy infrastructure assets.

Valina Giouzelaki, Counsel at WFW in Athens, said, "We are delighted to have advised Lux North Greece on the financing of Luxcara’s first solar photovoltaic projects in Greece. This transaction highlights the continued maturation of the Greek renewables market and demonstrates how structured project finance can enable large-scale energy transition investments." Lorenz Hahn, Investment Manager at Luxcara, added, "WFW delivered strong execution and was a dependable partner throughout the process."

INDEX

SECTION 1 WATSON, FARLEY & WILLIAMS PROFILE

SECTION 1 WATSON, FARLEY & WILLIAMS PROFILE

1.1 ACTIVITIES

Founded in 1982, Watson Farley & Williams is a law firm headquartered in New York City, New York. The company''s practice areas include asset financing, banking, anti-trust, corporate finance and securities, employment and immigration, financial services, finance, and commercial litigation, intellectual property, mergers and acquisitions, real estate, and taxation. WFW primarily serves clients in the energy, real estate, transportation sectors, aviation, internet and e-commerce, natural resources, oil and gas, logistics, telecommunication, media, and technology industries.

1.2 SUMMARY

Website: https://www.wfw.com/

value.Title: May 04, 2026: Feifei Wu Named Senior Managing Director to Accelerate AI and Investment-Technology Initiatives at Ontario Teachers ’

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK1-KM23-RS4K-525F-00000-00May 04, 2026: Feifei Wu Named Senior Managing Director to Accelerate AI and Investment-Technology Initiatives at Ontario Teachers’2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:02ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSMay 04, 2026: Feifei Wu Named Senior Managing Director to Accelerate AI and Investment-Technology Initiatives at Ontario Teachers’

TORONTO, May 4th, 2026 – Ontario Teachers’ Pension Plan Board ("Ontario Teachers’") today announced the appointment of Feifei Wu as Senior Managing Director, Investment Technology & Applied Intelligence, effective immediately. In this newly created role, Ms. Wu will work closely with investment leaders to ensure technology drives key business outcomes and will lead Ontario Teachers’ AI strategy to strengthen governance, accelerate adoption, and unlock new opportunities.

Ms. Wu brings more than 25 years of global experience across investment and wealth management, with expertise in technology, artificial intelligence, machine learning, and cloud transformation. Chief Technology Officer Terry Hickey said, "I am pleased to welcome Feifei to Ontario Teachers’ in this important role at a pivotal time in our technology journey. She brings a strong combination of technical expertise and proven leadership across global financial institutions. Her experience building high-performing technology teams and her forward-looking approach to applied intelligence will help accelerate innovation, deepen investment insights, and deliver long-term value for our members."

Prior to joining Ontario Teachers’, Ms. Wu served as Managing Director, Global Head of Engineering of Macquarie Asset Management at Macquarie Group in New York. Her career also includes General Partner, Digital & Technology at Edward Jones, Global Chief Information Officer at Brown Brothers Harriman, and leadership posts at RBC Capital Markets and BNY.

Ms. Wu holds an MS and PhD in Computer Science from Rutgers University, focusing on artificial intelligence and machine learning, along with an ME in Computer Engineering from Zhejiang University and a BE in Computer Engineering from Northeastern University in China.

Ontario Teachers' Pension Plan Board is a global investor with net assets of $279.4 billion as of December 31, 2025, serving about 346,000 working members and pensioners. For more information, visit otpp.com and follow us on LinkedIn.

INDEX

SECTION 1 ONTARIO TEACHERS PENSION PLAN (OTPP) PROFILE

SECTION 2 ONTARIO TEACHERS PENSION PLAN (OTPP) TOP MANAGEMENT

SECTION 1 ONTARIO TEACHERS PENSION PLAN (OTPP) PROFILE

1.1 ACTIVITIES

Ontario Teachers Pension Plan (OTPP), founded in 1990, is a pension fund manager based in Toronto, Canada. With around 1,300 employees, it manages approximately $250 billion in assets. OTPP invests in infrastructure, real estate, and private equity globally. In 2024, it expanded AI and renewable energy portfolios. Competing with CPPIB, its mission is to deliver sustainable pension returns through diversified, innovative investments.

value.Title: Equinor ships 5,000th oil cargo from Gullfaks field in North Sea
value.Overview: Trend Business Review - Azerbaijan, SHIPPING INDUSTRY NEWS, SHIPPING INDUSTRY NEWS & NORWAY NEWS, 372words

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJW-MHK3-RX79-R4D7-00000-00Equinor ships 5,000th oil cargo from Gullfaks field in North Sea2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:02ZLexisNexisSHIPPING INDUSTRY NEWS, SHIPPING INDUSTRY NEWS & NORWAY NEWSEquinor ships 5,000th oil cargo from Gullfaks field in North Sea

BAKU, Azerbaijan, May 13. Equinor has announced
that the 5,000th oil cargo has been exported from the Gullfaks
field in the Norwegian sector of the North Sea, marking another
milestone in the field’s long production history, Trend
reports.

When production began in 1986, Gullfaks was originally expected
to remain operational until 2007. However, more than two decades
later, the field continues to deliver weekly shipments to
international markets, with the majority of volumes supplied to
Europe.

Since start-up, Gullfaks has produced approximately 2.8 billion
barrels of oil equivalent, nearly double initial estimates. The
field also functions as an export hub for production from nearby
assets, including the Snorre and Visund fields, as well as multiple
subsea developments in the region.

Gullfaks crude is characterized by its light quality and low
sulphur content, making it suitable for refining into high-value
products such as gasoline, diesel, jet fuel, LPG and naphtha.
Deliveries are made to refineries across Sweden, the United Kingdom
and Poland, supporting fuel supply chains for transport, industry
and households.

The 5,000th cargo was delivered to the St1 refinery in
Gothenburg, where it is processed into a range of fuels distributed
through approximately 1,150 retail stations across Finland, Sweden
and Norway. Additional shipments are also sent to refineries in the
UK and Poland, as well as occasionally to Equinor’s Mongstad
refinery in Norway.

Oil from Gullfaks is transported using shuttle tankers with a
capacity of around 800,000 barrels, enabling flexible logistics
between the Norwegian continental shelf and European buyers.

Gullfaks production began with the Gullfaks A platform in 1986,
followed by Gullfaks B in 1988 and Gullfaks C in 1989. The field
ranks as Norway’s seventh-largest oil and gas field by original
reserves, following giants such as Troll, Statfjord, Ekofisk,
Oseberg, Åsgard and Johan Sverdrup.

Production peaked in 1994, while last year the field and its
associated subsea assets produced just over 17 million barrels of
oil. Part of the platform’s energy needs are now supplied by
electricity from the floating offshore wind farm Hywind Tampen,
reflecting growing integration of renewable power in offshore
operations.

value.Title: May 05, 2026: Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman Expands to Boston with New Office Launch

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJW-DVG3-S2B7-82JJ-00000-00May 05, 2026: Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman Expands to Boston with New Office Launch2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:54:14ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSMay 05, 2026: Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman Expands to Boston with New Office Launch

Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman is set to open an office in Boston, expanding its footprint in one of the largest legal and business markets in the United States. On 12th May 2026, the firm announced that it will commence operations with a team of five senior lawyers, including a notable litigator hired from Latham & Watkins.

Robert Gilbert, who joined Pillsbury after a seven-year tenure at Latham, specializes in representing policyholders in complex insurance recovery matters. He has previously acted for prominent Boston-area clients such as Harvard University, UniFirst Corporation, and Northland Investment Corporation. Gilbert will be instrumental in launching the Boston office alongside key figures from Pillsbury's leadership, including Michael Zeliger, Andrew Troop, Don Lonczak, and David Simons.

David Dekker, chair and CEO of Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman, emphasized the strategic importance of the Boston office, citing the city's dynamic start-up ecosystem, world-class research institutions, and a concentration of financial services companies and private capital investors. He remarked, "Pillsbury has long represented clients headquartered in the Boston area and those connected to the Boston-Cambridge innovation ecosystem. Opening a Boston office is a natural next step given our capabilities and relationships in the tech, life sciences, healthcare, and financial industries in Boston and around the world."

This new office marks Pillsbury’s 17th location in the U.S. and its 25th globally. The firm joins a growing list of major law firms that have established a presence in Boston, aiming to leverage the city's thriving technology and life sciences sectors, as well as its expanding financial services market. Recent entrants include Reed Smith, which opened in March, along with a slew of other firms like Freshfields and Simpson Thacher & Bartlett.

With its Boston office, Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman is poised to deepen its engagement with local clients and industries, further solidifying its role in the evolving legal landscape of the region.

INDEX

SECTION 1 PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP PROFILE

SECTION 2 PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP TOP MANAGEMENT

SECTION 1 PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP PROFILE

1.1 ACTIVITIES

Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP, founded in 1868, is a global law firm headquartered in New York City. With over 700 lawyers across 20 offices, it serves clients in energy, technology, and finance, specializing in M&A, regulatory, and litigation. Generating over $700 million annually, Pillsbury advised on 2024 renewable energy transactions, recognized by Columbia Law School. Its culture emphasizes innovation, diversity, and pro bono work, with strong associate training programs. Competing with Baker McKenzie, Pillsbury stands out for its energy and tech regulatory expertise. In 2024, it expanded its AI and ESG practices, focusing on sustainable finance. Recent initiatives include cloud-based legal tech and partnerships with nonprofits like Lawyers Alliance for New York, reinforcing its community impact.

Mission, Vision, and Values

At Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP, our mission is clear: to deliver innovative, high-quality legal services while positively impacting the communities we serve. Since our founding in 1868, we've built a reputation as a leading global law firm, combining forward-thinking legal strategies with a commitment to excellence.

Vision

Our vision is to be recognized as a premier global law firm, distinguished by our exceptional legal representation and unwavering dedication to public service.

Core Values

Innovation

We continuously innovate and refine our legal services to stay at the forefront of the industry.

Client Focus

Our priority is always our clients-anticipating their needs and delivering unmatched service.

Diversity and Inclusion

We are deeply committed to fostering a culture of diversity and equity, empowering our people to grow and succeed.

Community Impact

Beyond legal counsel, we strive to make a lasting difference through pro bono work, philanthropy, and volunteerism.

Integrity

We uphold the highest standards of transparency and honesty in every aspect of our practice, building trusted partnerships with clients and the global community.

With a presence in major cities such as New York, San Francisco, London, and Shanghai, Pillsbury merges a profound legacy with cutting-edge innovation to empower clients and drive progress worldwide.

value.Title: May 05, 2026: Watson Farley & Williams Promotes Two Madrid Partners in Strategic Office Expansion

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJX-DGD3-RSP4-R3YS-00000-00May 05, 2026: Watson Farley & Williams Promotes Two Madrid Partners in Strategic Office Expansion2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:02ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSMay 05, 2026: Watson Farley & Williams Promotes Two Madrid Partners in Strategic Office Expansion

Watson Farley &Williams (WFW) has announced the promotion of Lidia Fernandez and Javier Ruffin to partner at its Madrid office. This move is part of a broader global initiative, with seven new partners appointed across various jurisdictions. As of 5th May 2026, the Madrid office now counts 15 partners, marking significant growth for the firm in Spain's competitive legal landscape.

Maria Pilar Garcia Guijarro, managing partner of the Madrid office, said, "I congratulate Lidia and Javier on their well-deserved promotions. I am enormously proud to have accompanied their development over these years, which have made them the outstanding lawyers they are." She noted that their advancement underscores WFW's commitment to nurturing talent internally and aligns with the firm's values of expertise and commercial focus.

Fernandez, promoted from the regulatory department where she specialized in energy and renewable energy projects, has dedicated eleven years to WFW. Ruffin, whose focus is on corporate and M&A with a concentration on energy and infrastructure, has advised funds and independent power producers on acquisitions and joint ventures, and was named Energy Lawyer of the Year under 40 by Iberian Lawyer in 2025.

INDEX

SECTION 1 WATSON, FARLEY & WILLIAMS PROFILE

SECTION 1 WATSON, FARLEY & WILLIAMS PROFILE

1.1 ACTIVITIES

Founded in 1982, Watson Farley & Williams is a law firm headquartered in New York City, New York. The company''s practice areas include asset financing, banking, anti-trust, corporate finance and securities, employment and immigration, financial services, finance, and commercial litigation, intellectual property, mergers and acquisitions, real estate, and taxation. WFW primarily serves clients in the energy, real estate, transportation sectors, aviation, internet and e-commerce, natural resources, oil and gas, logistics, telecommunication, media, and technology industries.

1.2 SUMMARY

Website: https://www.wfw.com/

Address:

"15 Appold Street London EC2A 2HB England, United Kingdom"

value.Title: NEW ACORE INVESTOR SURVEY REPORT KICKS OFF 2026 FINANCE FORUM

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK4-7GN3-RTV0-P159-00000-00NEW ACORE INVESTOR SURVEY REPORT KICKS OFF 2026 FINANCE FORUM2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:02ZLexisNexisNEW ACORE INVESTOR SURVEY REPORT KICKS OFF 2026 FINANCE FORUMStates News ServiceNEW YORK CITY, N.Y.

The following information was released by the American Council On Renewable Energy (ACORE):

A new report from ACORE shows that clean energy investors and developers largely plan to increase their investments in 2026 but cite policy, regulatory, and interconnection uncertainty as the biggest risks to their investment strategy after this year.

In its Navigating Uncertainty: Clean Energy Investment Trends (2026-2029) report, ACORE shares market sentiment analysis gathered from surveys of 36 leaders at U.S. and multinational companies that invested billions in the U.S. clean energy market in 2025.

Topline takeaways from the report include:

Respondents identified federal regulatory and policy risks and interconnection uncertainty and costs as the top risks facing clean energy investments.

Capital providers continue to view utility-scale solar and energy storage as the two most attractive clean energy technologies for investment.

Despite declining attractiveness of the U.S. as a venue for clean energy investment compared to previous years, respondents said they plan to develop and finance more American clean energy projects in 2026 than they did in 2025.

Policy and investment uncertainty clouds the trajectory post-2026, with the potential for additional roadblocks to financing and developing clean energy infrastructure.

This report complements the Clean Energy Investment Trends report released last month that SandP Global prepared for ACORE.

"ACORE's recent reports highlight a common thread: the U.S. clean energy sector remains capitalized and ready to help deliver electricity reliability and affordability for American consumers," said ACORE President and CEO Ray Long. "Our sector is thriving and poised to meet this moment of significant electricity demand growth, but investors and developers need policy certainty to deliver on this critical infrastructure for American energy security."

ACORE released the report at its annual Finance Forum in New York City today and discussed the takeaways during the opening panel with ACORE Senior Vice President for Policy Lesley Hunter, Avangrid CEO Jose Antonio Miranda, and SandP Global CERA Consulting Director Christopher Wilfong.

Please email communications@acore.org if you'd like to view the recording of the first panel or set up an interview with ACORE about the report. Register here to tune in to the other panels.

ACORE will host a member-only webinar to discuss both reports on May 21, 2026. Learn more about becoming an ACORE member here.

###

About ACORE:

ACORE is a nonpartisan nonprofit organization that operates at the intersection of affordability, reliability, and clean energy deployment. Our work is focused on stabilizing energy prices, strengthening the electric grid, and driving investment in cost-effective technologies to ensure that clean energy delivers for people, businesses, and the U.S. economy.

ACORE's membership includes clean energy investors, developers, energy buyers, power generators, manufacturers, and energy providers. In 2024, nearly 80% of the booming utility-scale domestic clean energy growth was financed, developed, owned, equipped, or contracted by ACORE members.

value.Title: April 30, 2026: Luxcara Reaches Financing Milestone for 83 MW Solar Project in Greece , WFW Advises

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK1-KM23-RS4K-52GK-00000-00April 30, 2026: Luxcara Reaches Financing Milestone for 83 MW Solar Project in Greece, WFW Advises2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:02ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSApril 30, 2026: Luxcara Reaches Financing Milestone for 83 MW Solar Project in Greece, WFW Advises

Luxcara, a Hamburg-based asset manager focused on energy transition infrastructure, has secured financing for an 83 MW solar PV portfolio in Greece, with advisory support from Watson Farley & Williams. The transaction took place on 8th May 2026.

The 83 MW project comprises roughly 130,000 PV modules on single-axis trackers and is being constructed by Ameresco Sunel Energy S.A. The project aligns with Greece’s renewable energy transition and signals ongoing investor confidence in high-quality energy infrastructure assets.

The energy team at WFW was led by Counsel Valina Giouzelaki from the Athens office, supported by Senior Associate Dimitrianna Kolonia and Associate Fotini Nassou. Athens Partner Marsila Karpida and Senior Associate Haris Kazantzis provided English-law advice, while London Associate Kristina Buckberry contributed expertise in hedging.

Valina Giouzelaki, Counsel at WFW in Athens, said, "We are delighted to have advised Lux North Greece on the financing of Luxcara’s first solar photovoltaic projects in Greece. This transaction highlights the continued maturation of the Greek renewables market and demonstrates how structured project finance can successfully enable large-scale energy transition investments." Lorenz Hahn, Investment Manager at Luxcara, added, "WFW delivered strong execution and was a dependable partner throughout the process."

INDEX

SECTION 1 WATSON, FARLEY & WILLIAMS PROFILE

SECTION 1 WATSON, FARLEY & WILLIAMS PROFILE

1.1 ACTIVITIES

Founded in 1982, Watson Farley & Williams is a law firm headquartered in New York City, New York. The company''s practice areas include asset financing, banking, anti-trust, corporate finance and securities, employment and immigration, financial services, finance, and commercial litigation, intellectual property, mergers and acquisitions, real estate, and taxation. WFW primarily serves clients in the energy, real estate, transportation sectors, aviation, internet and e-commerce, natural resources, oil and gas, logistics, telecommunication, media, and technology industries.

value.Title: Equinor ships 5,000th oil cargo from Gullfaks field in North Sea
value.Overview: Trend News Agency - Central Asia (English), SHIPPING INDUSTRY NEWS, SHIPPING INDUSTRY NEWS & NORWAY NEWS, 372words

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJW-MHK3-RX79-R4FR-00000-00Equinor ships 5,000th oil cargo from Gullfaks field in North Sea2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:02ZLexisNexisSHIPPING INDUSTRY NEWS, SHIPPING INDUSTRY NEWS & NORWAY NEWSEquinor ships 5,000th oil cargo from Gullfaks field in North Sea

BAKU, Azerbaijan, May 13. Equinor has announced
that the 5,000th oil cargo has been exported from the Gullfaks
field in the Norwegian sector of the North Sea, marking another
milestone in the field’s long production history, Trend
reports.

When production began in 1986, Gullfaks was originally expected
to remain operational until 2007. However, more than two decades
later, the field continues to deliver weekly shipments to
international markets, with the majority of volumes supplied to
Europe.

Since start-up, Gullfaks has produced approximately 2.8 billion
barrels of oil equivalent, nearly double initial estimates. The
field also functions as an export hub for production from nearby
assets, including the Snorre and Visund fields, as well as multiple
subsea developments in the region.

Gullfaks crude is characterized by its light quality and low
sulphur content, making it suitable for refining into high-value
products such as gasoline, diesel, jet fuel, LPG and naphtha.
Deliveries are made to refineries across Sweden, the United Kingdom
and Poland, supporting fuel supply chains for transport, industry
and households.

The 5,000th cargo was delivered to the St1 refinery in
Gothenburg, where it is processed into a range of fuels distributed
through approximately 1,150 retail stations across Finland, Sweden
and Norway. Additional shipments are also sent to refineries in the
UK and Poland, as well as occasionally to Equinor’s Mongstad
refinery in Norway.

Oil from Gullfaks is transported using shuttle tankers with a
capacity of around 800,000 barrels, enabling flexible logistics
between the Norwegian continental shelf and European buyers.

Gullfaks production began with the Gullfaks A platform in 1986,
followed by Gullfaks B in 1988 and Gullfaks C in 1989. The field
ranks as Norway’s seventh-largest oil and gas field by original
reserves, following giants such as Troll, Statfjord, Ekofisk,
Oseberg, Åsgard and Johan Sverdrup.

Production peaked in 1994, while last year the field and its
associated subsea assets produced just over 17 million barrels of
oil. Part of the platform’s energy needs are now supplied by
electricity from the floating offshore wind farm Hywind Tampen,
reflecting growing integration of renewable power in offshore
operations.

value.Title: INVESTOR ALERT: Pomerantz Law Firm Investigates Claims On Behalf Of Investors Of Primoris Services Corporation - PRIM

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJP-YP83-RVHY-P542-00000-00INVESTOR ALERT: Pomerantz Law Firm Investigates Claims On Behalf Of Investors Of Primoris Services Corporation - PRIM2026-05-12T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:02ZLexisNexisINVESTOR ALERT: Pomerantz Law Firm Investigates Claims On Behalf Of Investors Of Primoris Services Corporation - PRIM

NEW YORK, May 12, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Pomerantz LLP is investigating claims on behalf of investors of Primoris Services Corporation (“Primoris” or the“Company”) (NYSE: PRIM). Such investors are advised to contact Danielle Peyton at ... or 646-581-9980, ext. 7980. The investigation concerns whether Primoris and certain of its officers and/or directors have engaged in securities fraud or other unlawful business practices.


[Click here for information about joining the class action] On May 5, 2026, Primoris issued a press release reporting its financial results for the first quarter of 2026. Primoris reported results below analyst expectations and slashed full-year adjusted EBITDA guidance from $560-$580 million to $480-$500 million. Primoris attributed the reduction to lower renewable energy activity, delayed project starts, and increased costs on renewable energy projects.
On this news, Primoris's stock price fell $101.69 per share, or 50.11%, to close at $101.23 per share on May 6, 2026.
Pomerantz LLP, with offices in New York, Chicago, Los Angeles, London, Paris, and Tel Aviv, is acknowledged as one of the premier firms in the areas of corporate, securities, and antitrust class litigation. Founded by the late Abraham L. Pomerantz, known as the dean of the class action bar, Pomerantz pioneered the field of securities class actions. Today, more than 85 years later, Pomerantz continues in the tradition he established, fighting for the rights of the victims of securities fraud, breaches of fiduciary duty, and corporate misconduct. The Firm has recovered numerous multimillion-dollar damages awards on behalf of class members. See .
