value.Title: May 11, 2026: CONSIGLI CONSTRUCTION CO., INC. PEER LANDSCAPE: RECENT NEWS HIGHLIGHTS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJM-G8H3-S3TD-1210-00000-00May 11, 2026: CONSIGLI CONSTRUCTION CO., INC. PEER LANDSCAPE: RECENT NEWS HIGHLIGHTS2026-05-12T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:51:24ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSMay 11, 2026: CONSIGLI CONSTRUCTION CO., INC. PEER LANDSCAPE: RECENT NEWS HIGHLIGHTS

On February 25, 2026, Consigli Construction Co., Inc. commenced work on the Williams College Museum of Art, marking the first purpose-built facility for the museum on the campus. Abigail Berkebile, a prominent figure at the firm, noted the unique design challenges and the rewarding nature of witnessing staff enthusiasm as the building progresses. She also emphasized the diverse career opportunities available for women in the construction industry, noting that teams rely on expertise in accounting, IT, legal, and other fields that do not require a formal construction background. To maintain focus during complex projects, Berkebile highlighted her routine of organizing schedules and connecting with her team daily to foster collaboration.

In the broader competitive landscape, Suffolk Construction is advancing its regional infrastructure commitments. The firm managed the Southeastern Public Service Authority interchange project that led to the Virginia Department of Transportation reopening a long-term lane closure on U.S. Route 58 on May 5, 2026. The completion of this project facilitates a new ramp and flyover for eastbound traffic to access the regional landfill without crossing westbound lanes. Meanwhile, a solar farm in Suffolk currently under construction by Egg Power, set to commence generation in 2027, became the center of a 10-year Power Purchase Agreement announced on May 1, 2026. Virgin Media O2 will source approximately five percent of its total energy demands from this 70MW facility. Mark Hardman, Director of Finance Operations at VMO2, stated that the agreement represents the latest step in securing long-term renewable energy and achieving net-zero emissions by the end of 2040.

Expanding on significant industry developments, Turner Construction celebrated the topping out of the 72-megawatt Aligned IAD-06 Data Center in Frederick, Maryland, on April 28, 2026. The 450,000-square-foot facility, located on a 75-acre campus, logged over 80,000 craft hours with approximately 345 skilled workers. Nick Sevier, Operations Manager at Turner, remarked that the milestone reflects the strength of the partnership with Aligned and the dedication to delivering a complex data center safely. On that same day, Turner Construction commenced vertical construction on a $900 million expansion of the Geisinger Wyoming Valley Medical Center in Pennsylvania. The 11-story, 600,000-square-foot patient tower will increase the hospital's capacity to 500 beds and is slated to open in early 2028. David Kaminski, Vice President and Senior General Manager at Turner, stated that the project will enhance patient care and support caregivers while generating a significant local economic impact.

Shaping its financial strategies to remain competitive with peers, Gilbane Development appointed Andreas Vlahakis as head of capital markets on April 28, 2026. Operating from New York City and reporting to President and CEO James Patchett, Vlahakis oversees funding strategies, investment decisions, and equity partnerships for ground-up multifamily and student housing investments. In a related push for student housing development, the Western Kentucky University Student Government Association confirmed a public-private partnership with Gilbane following legislative approval on April 6, 2026, and initial authorization on December 11, 2025. The firm will design and develop new residence halls to replace outdated facilities on campus. SGA Senator Jakob Barker confirmed the authorization, noting that the university is now cleared to begin building the new accommodations.

value.Title: Studies from Delhi Technological University in the Area of Biofuel Described (Comprehensive Life Cycle Analysis of Bioethanol From Molasses: Addressing Energy Use and Uncertainty)
value.Overview: Climate Change Daily News, BIOTECHNOLOGY - BIOFUEL, 590words

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJG-P063-RVWV-04JH-00000-00Studies from Delhi Technological University in the Area of Biofuel Described (Comprehensive Life Cycle Analysis of Bioethanol From Molasses: Addressing Energy Use and Uncertainty)2026-05-11T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:02ZLexisNexisBIOTECHNOLOGY - BIOFUELStudies from Delhi Technological University in the Area of Biofuel Described (Comprehensive Life Cycle Analysis of Bioethanol From Molasses: Addressing Energy Use and Uncertainty)


2026 MAY 11 (NewsRx) -- By a News Reporter-Staff News Editor at Climate Change Daily News -- Researchers detail new data in Biotechnology - Biofuel. According to news reporting originating in Delhi, India, by NewsRx journalists, research stated, "The growing global demand for cleaner and renewable energy sources has intensified interest in bioethanol production from agro-industrial byproducts such as molasses. This study presents a comprehensive life cycle assessment of bioethanol production from sugarcane molasses to evaluate its environmental sustainability, energy efficiency, and associated uncertainties."


The news reporters obtained a quote from the research from Delhi Technological University, "The analysis employs the ReCiPe 2016 and cumulative energy demand methods to quantify midpoint and endpoint environmental impacts along with total energy use across the production chain. A gate-to-gate system boundary was adopted, encompassing molasses preparation, fermentation, distillation, and dehydration processes. Results indicate that electricity and steam generation are the dominant contributors to environmental burdens, particularly influencing global warming, fine particulate matter formation, and human toxicity categories. The total energy requirement was found to be 26.98 MJ per kg of ethanol produced, with fossil energy accounting for 93% of this demand. Despite this, the process demonstrated a slightly positive energy balance, reflected by a net energy ratio of 1.04, confirming marginally favorable energy performance. Uncertainty analysis using Monte Carlo simulation revealed stable results with low variability, indicating model robustness. Human non-carcinogenic toxicity and global warming potential were identified as the impact categories contributing most significantly to the overall uncertainty in the results. Overall, the findings suggest that molasses-based bioethanol is a viable renewable fuel option with potential to reduce fossil energy dependence, provided that efficiency improvements in electricity and steam usage are implemented."
According to the news reporters, the research concluded: "The study underscores the importance of integrating energy and uncertainty analyses for more reliable environmental sustainability assessments of biofuel systems."
This research has been peer-reviewed.
For more information on this research see: Comprehensive Life Cycle Analysis of Bioethanol From Molasses: Addressing Energy Use and Uncertainty. BioEnergy Research, 2026;19(1). BioEnergy Research can be contacted at: Springer, One New York Plaza, Suite 4600, New York, Ny, United States. (Springer - www.springer.com; BioEnergy Research - www.springerlink.com/content/1939-1234/)
Our news correspondents report that additional information may be obtained by contacting Kulvendra Patel, Delhi Technological University, Dept. of Environmental Engineering, Delhi 110042, India.
The direct object identifier (DOI) for that additional information is: https://doi.org/10.1007/s12155-026-11003-5. This DOI is a link to an online electronic document that is either free or for purchase, and can be your direct source for a journal article and its citation.
Keywords for this news article include: Delhi, India, Asia, Bio Ethanol, Bioengineering, Biofuel, Biotechnology, Climate Change, Energy, Global Warming, Oil and Gas Research, Renewable Energy, Delhi Technological University.

value.Title: April 29, 2026: Fortress Investment Group Extends Forward-Flow Commitment with Upstart at $1.25 Billion

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHS-DX73-RTB8-N0BR-00000-00April 29, 2026: Fortress Investment Group Extends Forward-Flow Commitment with Upstart at $1.25 Billion2026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:02ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSApril 29, 2026: Fortress Investment Group Extends Forward-Flow Commitment with Upstart at $1.25 Billion

Upstart Holdings said Fortress Investment Group will purchase up to $1.25 billion of consumer loans originated through its platform over 15 months, a forward-flow commitment disclosed on 5th May 2026.

The deal expands a partnership that began in 2025 and comes as Upstart continues to broaden its network of capital partners, now counting more than 100 banks and credit unions using its AI-driven models and cloud applications. The Upstart platform offers personal loans, auto financing, home equity lines of credit, and a Cash Line revolving credit product.

Executives framed the arrangement as a scalable opportunity for both firms: a stronger funding base for Upstart and access to differentiated consumer credit opportunities for Fortress, with Upstart’s data-driven platform enabling efficient, scalable origination and the potential for durable returns across market conditions. The collaboration signals a wider trend of technology-enhanced consumer credit solutions.

INDEX

SECTION 1 FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC PROFILE

SECTION 2 FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC TOP MANAGEMENT

SECTION 1 FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC PROFILE

1.1 ACTIVITIES

Fortress Investment Group LLC, founded in 1998, is a global investment firm headquartered in New York City. Employing over 900 people, it manages $48 billion in assets across private equity, credit, and real estate. Acquired by SoftBank in 2017, Fortress operates independently, focusing on distressed assets and infrastructure. In 2024, it invested $2 billion in renewable energy projects. Its culture emphasizes risk management, innovation, and agility, with training in asset valuation and fintech. Competing with Apollo Global, Fortress stands out for its credit expertise and opportunistic investments. Recent initiatives include blockchain-based real estate funds and ESG-focused portfolios, aligning with investment trends. Fortress diversified strategy ensures resilience in volatile markets.

1.2 SUMMARY

urn:contentItem:6JHV-CXJ3-RTBH-G0MV-00000-00April 29, 2026: Upstart Secures $1.25 Billion Forward-Flow Pact with Fortress Investment Group2026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:02ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSApril 29, 2026: Upstart Secures $1.25 Billion Forward-Flow Pact with Fortress Investment Group

Upstart Holdings, an AI lending marketplace, announced a forward-flow commitment from funds managed by affiliates of Fortress Investment Group. Fortress will purchase up to $1.25 billion of consumer loans originated through the Upstart platform over 15 months. The revelation came on 5th May 2026.

The arrangement deepens a partnership that began in 2025. It underscores Upstart’s push to diversify its capital base, now drawing on more than 100 banks and credit unions that use its AI-driven models and cloud applications. Upstart’s platform supports a range of products, including personal loans, automotive loans, home equity lines of credit, and a Cash Line revolving credit option.

INDEX

SECTION 1 FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC PROFILE

SECTION 2 FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC TOP MANAGEMENT

SECTION 1 FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC PROFILE

1.1 ACTIVITIES

Fortress Investment Group LLC, founded in 1998, is a global investment firm headquartered in New York City. Employing over 900 people, it manages $48 billion in assets across private equity, credit, and real estate. Acquired by SoftBank in 2017, Fortress operates independently, focusing on distressed assets and infrastructure. In 2024, it invested $2 billion in renewable energy projects. Its culture emphasizes risk management, innovation, and agility, with training in asset valuation and fintech. Competing with Apollo Global, Fortress stands out for its credit expertise and opportunistic investments. Recent initiatives include blockchain-based real estate funds and ESG-focused portfolios, aligning with investment trends. Fortress diversified strategy ensures resilience in volatile markets.

Mon Apr 27, 2026: AES Corporation Secures Siting Permits for 343 MW Renewable Project in New York

The AES Corporation has successfully obtained siting permits for a significant renewable energy project in New York, encompassing 343 megawatts (MW) of wind and solar capacity. In a move that reinforces its commitment to sustainable energy solutions, the company has positioned itself to play a pivotal role in New York's clean energy landscape. The announcement comes on the heels of the permits being granted on the 27th of April, 2026.

The approved project aligns with New York's ambitious renewable energy goals, which aim to achieve 70% of the state's electricity from renewable sources by 2030. By integrating both wind and solar energy into its portfolio, the AES Corporation not only enhances its operational capabilities but also contributes to the state's effort to reduce greenhouse gas emissions and combat climate change.

The 343 MW project will involve a combination of onshore wind turbines and solar photovoltaic installations. AES Corporation's strategy reflects a broader trend in the energy sector, where companies are increasingly diversifying their renewable energy sources to meet rising demand. This dual approach is expected to create more resilient energy systems that can adapt to varying environmental conditions and energy needs.

In addition to contributing to New York's energy mix, the project is anticipated to create numerous jobs during the construction phase, further supporting local economies. AES Corporation has been vocal about its commitment to community engagement and the economic benefits that renewable energy projects can generate, particularly in areas where these developments are located.

The siting permits mark a significant milestone for AES Corporation as it continues to expand its footprint in the renewable energy market. Investors and stakeholders will be closely watching the project's progression, as it could serve as a model for future initiatives in other regions. The company's proactive approach in securing these permits underscores its ambition to lead in the transition towards cleaner energy solutions.

Recent News 2 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 369 words

Thu Apr 23, 2026: AESIndiana and Google Forge $770 Million Savings Pact for Central Indiana

AESIndiana, a subsidiary of The AES Corporation (NYSE: AES), has announced a significant partnership with Google that aims to deliver over $770 million in customer savings while powering growth in Central Indiana. The agreement, filed under HEA 1007, was officially disclosed on 24th April 2026. This initiative is poised to ensure that Google will pay for 100 percent of its energy consumption and the infrastructure required to support its proposed data center in Monrovia, thereby safeguarding residential and small business customers from any associated costs.

During a recent public meeting with the Indiana Utility Regulatory Commission (IURC), AES Indiana emphasized the importance of responsible growth that prioritizes customer protection. Brandi Davis-Handy, President of AES Indiana, stressed that affordability is one of Indiana's core energy pillars, guiding the company's planning and operational strategies. The framework established by the state legislature allows AES Indiana to address rising energy demands while maintaining a commitment to keeping energy costs low for existing customers.

Will Conkling, Region Head of Google Data Center Energy, highlighted the collaborative nature of this partnership, stating that it exemplifies how data centers and utilities can work together to foster economic development without burdening ratepayers. The agreement is expected to create new jobs and bolster infrastructure in Indiana, with Google covering all costs related to the energy demands of its data center, thus ensuring that families and small businesses in the area do not face additional financial strain.

The proposed investment will require approximately $1.3 billion in new energy infrastructure, which will support long-term construction jobs and enhance the local tax base. The HEA 1007 filing mandates that utilities demonstrate that large-load customers fully fund the infrastructure necessary to serve them. AES Indiana's proposal not only offers robust cost protections but also facilitates proactive infrastructure planning to enhance system reliability as demand grows.

All proposed investments will undergo review and approval by the IURC, with a decision anticipated in September. This partnership aligns with the White House Ratepayer Protection Pledge, which aims to safeguard ratepayers while promoting economic growth and maintaining a reliable energy grid as businesses expand. For more details about AES Indiana's initiatives to support responsible growth in Central Indiana, interested parties can visit the company's website.

Recent News 3 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 549 words

Fri Apr 17, 2026: AES Corporation Acquisition Highlights $33.4 Billion Infrastructure Megadeal

A landmark transaction is reshaping the global infrastructure landscape. A consortium led by Global Infrastructure Partners (GIP) and EQT has agreed to acquire AES Corporation in a deal valued at approximately $33.4 billion, marking one of the largest infrastructure buyouts of 2026 and signaling a new phase in the competition for strategic energy assets. The date of this significant announcement was the 17th of April 2026, underscoring a powerful convergence of themes currently driving alternative investments: the global transition toward renewable energy, the insatiable demand for power driven by artificial intelligence infrastructure, and the increasing role of private capital in funding critical energy systems. For institutional investors, the AES deal is more than just another megabuyout—it is a window into how capital is being deployed at scale in one of the most consequential sectors of the modern economy.

The acquisition of AES represents a defining moment for infrastructure investing in 2026. At $33.4 billion enterprise value, the deal ranks among the largest sponsor-led transactions globally this year, rivaling major buyouts across private equity and energy sectors. Global Infrastructure Partners, one of the most prominent infrastructure investors globally, brings deep experience in energy, transport, and digital infrastructure. Its partnership with EQT—another global powerhouse with a rapidly expanding infrastructure platform—signals a coordinated effort to consolidate and scale assets that are central to the future of energy generation and distribution. AES, historically known for its diversified power generation portfolio, has spent the past decade repositioning itself toward renewables, battery storage, and grid modernization, making it an attractive platform for long-term capital deployment.

The AES deal reflects broader structural shifts in infrastructure investing. Over the past decade, infrastructure has evolved from a niche allocation to a core component of institutional portfolios. Pension funds, sovereign wealth funds, and insurance companies have significantly increased their exposure to the asset class, attracted by its combination of yield, inflation protection, and long-duration cash flows. In 2026, this trend has accelerated. Several key drivers are fueling demand: the global push toward decarbonization necessitating massive capital expenditures in renewable energy; the rise of artificial intelligence reshaping energy markets, particularly in data centers; and the privatization of public assets due to fiscal pressures on governments, creating opportunities for private capital to step in and fund infrastructure development.

The involvement of GIP and EQT highlights the growing dominance of mega-funds in infrastructure investing. These firms have raised massive pools of capital, often exceeding tens of billions of dollars, enabling them to pursue transactions of unprecedented scale. This consolidation has implications, including pricing power that allows larger funds to outbid smaller competitors, operational expertise that drives efficiency, and global reach that enables capital deployment across regions. However, this concentration of capital raises questions about competition and potential overvaluation for high-quality assets, which could compress future returns.

Despite its strategic appeal, the AES acquisition is not without risks. Regulatory challenges may arise due to changing policies and environmental regulations across jurisdictions, and execution risks could emerge from integrating diverse energy assets. Market dynamics also pose threats, as shifts in energy prices and demand patterns could affect valuations. Lastly, increased competition for infrastructure assets may lead to higher acquisition prices. For institutional investors, the AES deal serves as both an opportunity and a challenge, emphasizing the importance of navigating the complexities of infrastructure investment.

SECTION 6 CORPORATE PROFILE

6.1 Activities

The AES Corporation is a leading global energy company listed on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE), specializing in the generation, distribution, and sale of electricity across more than 15 countries. Its core business activities encompass a diversified portfolio that includes thermal power plants, renewable energy sources, and energy storage solutions, serving both regulated and competitive markets. AES focuses on sustainable energy practices, with its fastest-growing segments being renewables such as solar and wind projects, driven by increasing demand for clean energy and supportive government policies. For instance, the company has expanded its renewable capacity significantly in recent years, including large-scale solar farms in the U.S. and wind developments in Latin America, positioning it for continued growth in the transition to a low-carbon economy. Through strategic investments and operational efficiency, AES maintains a strong commitment to innovation and environmental stewardship, enhancing its competitive edge in the global energy sector. It is the NYSE's 15th largest Conventional electricity company by market capitalisation.

urn:contentItem:6JHY-DKN3-RTX9-G0N6-00000-00April 29, 2026: Upstart Expands Fortress Partnership with $1.25 Billion Forward-Flow Agreement2026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:02ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSApril 29, 2026: Upstart Expands Fortress Partnership with $1.25 Billion Forward-Flow Agreement

Upstart Holdings announced a forward-flow commitment from Fortress affiliates to buy up to $1.25 billion of consumer loans originated on its platform over 15 months, with the disclosure on 5th May 2026.

The deal expands a partnership that began in 2025 and reflects Upstart’s effort to broaden its network of capital partners. Fortress and its affiliates join more than 100 banks and credit unions using Upstart’s AI-driven models and cloud applications to originate loans.

Upstart’s platform covers a broad suite of products, including personal loans, automotive finance and refinance loans, home equity lines of credit, and a Cash Line revolving credit product.

Executives described the arrangement as a win for both sides, noting that a larger base of capital partners helps reduce borrowing costs and complexity for borrowers, while Fortress seeks differentiated, high-quality consumer credit opportunities for its investors. The collaboration signals a broader industry shift toward technology-driven credit solutions and positions both companies to pursue opportunities in the evolving lending landscape.

SECTION 1 FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC PROFILE

1.1 ACTIVITIES

Fortress Investment Group LLC, founded in 1998, is a global investment firm headquartered in New York City. Employing over 900 people, it manages $48 billion in assets across private equity, credit, and real estate. Acquired by SoftBank in 2017, Fortress operates independently, focusing on distressed assets and infrastructure. In 2024, it invested $2 billion in renewable energy projects. Its culture emphasizes risk management, innovation, and agility, with training in asset valuation and fintech. Competing with Apollo Global, Fortress stands out for its credit expertise and opportunistic investments. Recent initiatives include blockchain-based real estate funds and ESG-focused portfolios, aligning with investment trends. Fortress diversified strategy ensures resilience in volatile markets.

urn:contentItem:6JHV-R103-SD9Y-N2DD-00000-00PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP - RECENT INDUSTRY NEWS2026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:02ZLexisNexisSTOCKPILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP - RECENT INDUSTRY NEWS

DASHBOARD: PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP

PermID4296383614Websitewww.pillsburylaw.com/en/IndustryFull-Service BigLawAddress31 West 52nd Street, New York, NY 10019 USA

ACTIVITIES:

Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP, founded in 1868, is a global law firm headquartered in New York City. With over 700 lawyers across 20 offices, it serves clients in energy, technology, and finance, specializing in M&A, regulatory, and litigation. Generating over $700 million annually, Pillsbury advised on 2024 renewable energy transactions, recognized by Columbia Law School. Its culture emphasizes innovation, diversity, and pro bono work, with strong associate training programs. Competing with Baker McKenzie, Pillsbury stands out for its energy and tech regulatory expertise. In 2024, it expanded its AI and ESG practices, focusing on sustainable finance. Recent initiatives include cloud-based legal tech and partnerships with nonprofits like Lawyers Alliance for New York, reinforcing its community impact.

+ PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP [3 Press Releases]

+ PILLSBURY OPENS IN BOSTON WITH A FOCUS ON TECHNOLOGY, LIFE SCIENCES AND FINANCIAL INDUSTRIES

Pillsbury is opening a new office in Boston, marking an important expansion into one of the nation's leading growth and innovation centers. The office will launch with a team of five senior attorneys and additional associates, who bring diverse transactional and litigation backgrounds.

Source: Company Website

+ PILLSBURYS MARQUEE INSURANCE RECOVERY & ADVISORY PRACTICE CONTINUES EXPANSION, WELCOMING RENOWNED POLICYHOLDER LITIGATOR BOB GILBERT

Robert J. Gilbert, a nationally recognized litigator who represents policyholders in complex insurance recovery matters, has joined Pillsbury from Latham & Watkins. He comes to the firm as one of the founding partners of Pillsbury's newly established Boston office.

Source: Company Website

+ AS NOVEL DIGITAL RIGHTS ISSUES COME TO THE FORE, PILLSBURY ADDS IP LITIGATOR ROBERT L. URIARTE IN LOS ANGELES

Pillsbury has further enhanced its expansive tech-focused capabilities with the addition of Intellectual Property litigator and trial lawyer Robert L. Uriarte as a partner in its Los Angeles office.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ PERKINS COIE [3 Press Releases]

+ PERKINS COIE REPRESENTS SEDRON IN INVESTMENT FROM ARA PARTNERS OF UP TO $500 MILLION

PORTLAND, OR (April 15, 2026)-Perkins Coie announced today that it represented Sedron Technologies, a leader in advanced upcycling systems that transform waste streams into reusable water and fertilizer products, in an investment of up to $500 million from Ara Partners, a global private equity and infrastructure firm focused on industrial decarbonization.

Source: Company Website

+ PERKINS COIE STRENGTHENS PACIFIC NORTHWEST TRIAL BENCH WITH COMMERCIAL LITIGATOR CHAD COLTON

PORTLAND, OR and SEATTLE, WA (April 6, 2026)-Perkins Coie is pleased to announce that Chad Colton has joined its Commercial Litigation practice as a partner in Portland and Seattle, further strengthening the firm's trial capabilities in the Pacific Northwest.

Bringing two decades of experience, Chad represents clients in high-stakes commercial matters such as partnership, shareholder, breach of contract, IP, and licensing disputes. He also frequently serves as lead trial counsel for bet-the-company cases, often joining to advise and advocate for clients in the middle of litigation or immediately before trial.

Source: Company Website

+ PERKINS COIE RANKS 44TH ON FORTUNE'S "100 BEST COMPANIES TO WORK FOR®" LIST FOR 2026

SEATTLE (April 1, 2026)-Perkins Coie is proud to announce that it has again been named to Fortune's "100 Best Companies to Work For®" list for 2026, marking the 24th consecutive year that the firm has been featured on Fortune's prestigious list.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ COOLEY LLP [4 Press Releases]

+ COOLEY LLP VETHOS ANNOUNCES $200 MILLION IPO

Cooley advised Ethos Technologies, a top life insurance technology company on a mission to democratize access to life insurance, on its $200 million initial public offering (IPO). Ethos offered 5,127,696 shares of Class A common stock, and selling stockholders offered an additional 5,398,619 shares of Class A common stock, at a price of $19 per share. Ethos began trading on the Nasdaq Global Select Market on January 29, 2026, under the ticker symbol LIFE.

Partner Matthew Bartus led the Cooley team advising Ethos since its formation in 2016, including on its $100 million Series D-1 in July 2021 and $200 million Series D in May 2021.

Lawyers Jon Avina, Jean Park, Milson Yu, Samara Zaifman, Katie Lapidus and Charlotte Yin led the Cooley team advising Ethos on its IPO.

Source: Company Website

+ COOLEY LLP INFERACT ANNOUNCES $150 MILLION SEED FINANCING

Cooley advised Inferact, a startup founded by creators and core maintainers of the open-source project vLLM, on its $150 million seed financing at an $800 million valuation. The financing was co-led by Andreessen Horowitz and Lightspeed Venture Partners, with investments from Sequoia Capital, Altimeter Capital, Redpoint Ventures, ZhenFund, The House Fund, Striker Venture Partners, Laude Ventures, Databricks Ventures and the UC Berkeley Chancellor's Fund.

Lawyers Peter Werner, Tara Capsuto, Won Lee, Carl Min and Isabella Ysebrands led the Cooley team advising Inferact.

Source: Company Website

+ COOLEY LLP OPENEVIDENCE RAISES $250 MILLION SERIES D

Cooley advised OpenEvidence, the most widely used artificial intelligence (AI) platform by doctors in America, on its $250 million Series D financing, valuing the company at $12 billion and making it the most valuable healthcare AI company in the world. The new funding will be used to invest heavily in the research and development and compute costs associated with the multi-AI agentic architecture of OpenEvidence, which provides the highest quality and most accurate medical answers of any system in the world.

The round was co-led by Thrive Capital and DST Global, and brings the total funding raised by OpenEvidence to nearly $700 million over the past year.

Lawyers Derek Colla, David Safren and Sela Dougherty led the Cooley team advising OpenEvidence.

Source: Company Website

+ COOLEY LLP NEUROPHOS SECURES $110 MILLION SERIES A

Cooley advised Neurophos, a leader in photonic artificial intelligence chip technology, on its oversubscribed $110 million Series A round, bringing total funding to $118 million. The new funding will accelerate the delivery of Neurophos' first integrated photonic compute system, including datacenter-ready optical processing unit modules, a full software stack and early access developer hardware.

The round was led by Gates Frontier, with participation from M12 (Microsoft's venture fund), Carbon Direct Capital, Aramco Ventures, Bosch Ventures, Tectonic Ventures, Space Capital and others.

Lawyers James Allen, Nick Hobson, James Reizman and Nathan Alamillo led the Cooley team advising Neurophos.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ K&L GATES [3 Press Releases]

+ TWO K&L GATES PARTNERS AGAIN NAMED TO INDIA BUSINESS LAW JOURNALS INTERNATIONAL A-LIST 2026

K&L Gates is pleased to announce that Singapore Office Managing Partner Raja Bose and Singapore Corporate partner Meraj Noor have once again been named to India Business Law Journal's (IBLJ) International A-List. This marks the eighth consecutive year both partners have received this recognition.

IBLJ's International A-List identifies the top lawyers based outside of India who are recognized for their work on India-related matters. The A-List was compiled by IBLJ's seasoned editorial team following extensive market research, drawing on feedback from hundreds of lawyers at Indian law firms and thousands of India-focused in-house counsel within India and abroad.

The full International A-List 2026 is featured on IBLJ's website.

**All Singapore lawyers are members of K&L Gates Straits Law LLC, the Singapore office of global law firm K&L Gates. Source: Company Website

+ MORE THAN 80 K&L GATES LAWYERS ACROSS ASIA-PACIFIC RECOGNISED IN 2027 BEST LAWYERS, ONES TO WATCH EDITIONS

Best Lawyers®, a peer-review publication in the legal profession, has named more than 80 K&L Gates lawyers across the Asia-Pacific region in the 2027 editions of its guides, covering Australia, Japan, and Singapore. In addition, nearly 20 of the firm's lawyers were named as Best Lawyers: Ones to Watch® in Australia and Japan, lists that recognise lawyers early in their careers.

Alongside these recognitions, four Australian partners were recognised with "Lawyer of the Year" awards, an accolade that is particularly noteworthy, as it is only bestowed on one standout lawyer for each practice area in each jurisdiction.

The Best Lawyers® guides highlight top legal talent, selected by peers within the same geographical region and legal practice area, through millions of votes each year. Congratulations to the Lawyer of the Year award recipients below, as well as all lawyers recognised in the 2027 editions - resulting in nearly 180 total recognitions for the firm. Source: Company Website

+ K&L GATES ADVISES DOLPHIN ENTERTAINMENT, INC. ON US$15 MILLION PIPE INVESTMENT

Global law firm K&L Gates LLP advised Dolphin Entertainment, Inc., an entertainment marketing and premium content production company, in connection with a US$15 million private investment in public equity (PIPE) financing with Lincoln Park Capital, an investment group and asset management firm.

The Corporate team advising on the transaction was led by Miami partner Clayton Parker with support from Miami partner Erin Fogarty.

"This transaction underscores Dolphin Entertainment's commitment to accelerating strategic growth across its marketing and content-driven businesses. We were pleased to support them in securing investment from a partner aligned with its long-term vision," said Parker.

K&L Gates' Corporate practice is one of the most substantial in the legal industry, with hundreds of lawyers in offices across the globe providing clients with practical legal solutions in the structuring, financing, and completion of domestic, international, and cross-border transactions. Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ REED SMITH LLP [4 Press Releases]

+ REED SMITH ADVISES NATURPAK ON SALE BY TRIVEST TO PPC

CHICAGO - Global law firm Reed Smith represented Trivest Partners and IPMF, LLC, doing business as NaturPak, a manufacturer of bone broths, soups, sauces, wet pet food, and food ingredients for national retailers and brands, on its sale by Trivest Growth Investment Fund II to Pritzker Private Capital ("PPC"). Trivest Growth Investment Fund II, managed by Trivest Partners LP, a private equity firm focused exclusively on investments in founder- and family owned businesses, partnered with NaturPak through a minority investment in 2020. During Trivest's partnership, NaturPak transformed from a small specialty co-manufacturer into a scaled, multi-segment platform, increasing its R&D and manufacturing capabilities and production capacity.

The cross-office Reed Smith deal team included attorneys in Chicago, New York, Denver, Philadelphia, Dallas, Orange County (California), Pittsburgh, Washington, Houston, Tysons (Virginia), and Miami. The team was led by Nick Gibson, Kristen Capriotti, Crockett Stevenson, and Sudeep Kalkunte, with support from Constantine Karides, Brad Schmarak, David Hayes, Simon Kliegman, Ian Sherwin, Paulo McKeeby, Daniel Hagedorn, Michelle Mantine, Cori Goldberg, A.J. Wissinger, Jillian Burstein, Tyler Thompson, Mike Summers, Michael Jacobs, Brendan Leanos, Benjamin Brimeyer, Michael Galibois, Michael Lowell, Joseph Mammone Jr., Michelle DiPuma, Batool Banker, Leah Hungerman, Taber Rueter, Clirae Bourke, Michaela Mapes, Peter Loy, Talia Boiangin Behar, Elliott Race, George Avdellas, Katherine Litaker, Taylor Kollmansberger, and Nikita Bazaj.

Source: Company Website

+ REED SMITH PARTNERS JOSH LOVE, ERIC MARDER, STEPHEN SESSA, AND EDWARD SHAPIRO NAMED TO VARIETY'S "DEALMAKERS 2025" REPORT

LOS ANGELES - Global law firm Reed Smith announced today that Josh Love, Eric Marder, Stephen Sessa, and Edward Shapiro, partners in the firm's global Entertainment & Media Industry Group, have been named to Variety's "Dealmakers 2025" report. Love, Marder, Sessa, and Shapiro are among an elite group of entertainment attorneys who have been chosen based on their more than $3 billion in music transactions in 2025, high‑profile client roster, and leadership across the music industry. The publication highlights their work on "sports and music transactions, start-ups and brands," "recording, music publishing, and live performance agreements (including Bad Bunny's upcoming Super Bowl halftime show)," and "catalog acquisitions."

This recognition reflects Reed Smith's continued leadership in high-stakes entertainment and music transactions, talent representation, and emerging legal issues at the intersection of entertainment, music, technology, and finance. The four partners were selected for their high-profile talent negotiations and representation business.

Source: Company Website

+ REED SMITH ADVISES LEON CAPITAL ON STRATEGIC INVESTMENT IN AVANTIS GROUP

LONDON - Global law firm Reed Smith has advised funds advised by Leon Capital on the strategic investment in Avantis Group, a UK-based provider of specialist engineering services to the maritime and energy industries. The transaction, which represents the latest new investment by Leon Capital, completed on 23 December 2025. The terms of the investment are confidential.

The transaction involved the Development Bank of Wales and Avantis Group's founders, Chris David and Tom David. Avantis Group's existing management team will retain operational control of the business. Blake Morgan acted as legal counsel to the Development Bank of Wales, while Acuity Law advised the founders. AMA Capital Partners acted as corporate finance adviser to Avantis Group.

Reed Smith advised Leon Capital on all aspects of the transaction, providing comprehensive M&A support in connection with the strategic investment. The matter involved a multi-disciplinary team spanning M&A, tax, and regulatory considerations.

The investment supports Avantis Group's long-term growth strategy across maritime and energy markets, including expansion into digital infrastructure and defence, and reflects Leon Capital's confidence in the company's differentiated engineering platform and experienced management team.

For Reed Smith, the transaction represents a further example of the firm's work advising private capital sponsors on complex M&A transactions and continues to strengthen its relationship with Leon Capital as it expands its platform.

The Reed Smith team advising on the matter was led by Andrew Houghton, with support from Ciaran Sweeney, Georgia Moorhouse and Selina Cook on M&A matters; Caspar Fox and Tom Baxter on tax; and Emma Weeden on FDI and antitrust.

Source: Company Website

+ REED SMITH STRENGTHENS ASIA-PACIFIC FINANCE AND TRANSPORTATION CAPABILITY WITH HIRE IN SINGAPORE

SINGAPORE - Global law firm Reed Smith announced the appointment of banking and finance partner Dr. Gregory Xu to its Singapore office. Xu, who joins from Stephenson Harwood, where he led the general banking and project finance offering in Southeast Asia and served on the firm's Supervisory Council, its executive committee, will join Reed Smith's Transportation Industry Group.

With over 15 years of experience, Xu advises on high-value, cross-border transactions across a wide range of sectors, including transportation - shipping, aviation, and logistics - trade, life sciences, energy, and technology. He regularly acts for financial institutions, funds, export credit agencies, leasing houses, asset owners, and Fortune 500 companies on complex financing, restructuring, and enforcement matters.

Xu has a strong track record in green and sustainability-linked lending and advises on multi-jurisdictional insolvency-related enforcement and restructuring matters. He also brings extensive experience in general corporate and commercial matters.

Richard Hakes, chair of Reed Smith's Transportation Industry Group, said: "Gregory is an outstanding addition to our team, bringing deep knowledge of ship finance, aviation finance, and banking throughout Southeast Asia. His arrival further strengthens our capabilities in Singapore and reinforces our commitment to the maritime, aviation, and broader transportation sectors across the Asia-Pacific region and beyond. Working closely with our shipping teams in Hong Kong and Shanghai and our aviation team in Singapore as part of our global Transportation Industry Group, Gregory will enhance our ability to provide seamless, cross-border support to clients operating in one of the world's most dynamic industries."

Xu commented: "Reed Smith's global platform, sector focus, and collaborative culture are hugely impressive. The firm's market-leading finance practice and its long-standing reputation in general banking, asset finance, and project finance were major draws for me. I'm delighted to be joining such a highly collegiate team with a clear strategy for growth in the region and look forward to contributing to the continued expansion of the firm's finance offering across Asia-Pacific."

Praj Samant, Reed Smith's Asia-Pacific managing partner, said: "We are delighted to welcome Gregory to the Singapore team. His arrival further strengthens our offering in one of the region's most strategically important sectors. We look forward to the valuable contributions he will bring to the firm locally and globally."

Xu is recognized as a leading practitioner by multiple legal directories. Chambers Asia-Pacific ranks him as a "Leading Individual" for Banking & Finance (India), while The Legal 500 identifies him as a "Leading Partner," "Next Generation Partner," and "Recommended" lawyer across Banking & Finance, Asset Finance, Projects, and Shipping in Singapore and in the foreign juris

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ SIDLEY AUSTIN LLP [3 Press Releases]

+ SIDLEY ADVISES ON BROOKFIELD INDIA REIT AND POONAWALLA FINCORP QUALIFIED INSTITUTIONS PLACEMENTS

Sidley has advised on two significant qualified institutions placements (QIPs) in India, for Brookfield India Real Estate Trust (Brookfield India REIT) and Poonawalla Fincorp, together raising over ₹5,100 crore (US$550 million).

Source: Company Website

+ SIDLEY REPRESENTS APOLLO ON ITS STRATEGIC INVESTMENT IN VVARDIS

Sidley is representing funds managed by Apollo ("Apollo Funds") on their strategic minority investment in vVARDIS Holding AG ("vVARDIS"), a high-growth Swiss dental medtech company focused on non-invasive treatment solutions.

Source: Company Website

+ TEN SIDLEY LAWYERS NAMED AMONG D MAGAZINE'S 2026 "BEST LAWYERS IN DALLAS"

Share D Magazine has named 10 Sidley lawyers to its annual "Best Lawyers in Dallas" list. Lawyers are nominated by their peers, then reviewed by an anonymous panel of lawyers and D Magazine's editorial staff. Recognized Sidley lawyers are:

Sara Garcia Duran Kelly M. Dybala Yolanda C. Garcia Lauren G. Grau William D. Howell Tara M. Lancaster S. Scott Parel Rakhee V. Patel Eric M. Winwood Angela C. Zambrano

+ MORRISON & FOERSTER LLP CHRISTINE WONG NAMED TO SAN FRANCISCO BUSINESS TIMES 2026 MOST INFLUENTIAL WOMEN IN BAY AREA BUSINESS

The San Francisco Business Times has named Morrison Foerster partner Christine Wong to its 2026 list of the Most Influential Women in Bay Area Business. This recognition honors trailblazing leaders who are shaping industries, championing mentorship and community impact, and redefining what leadership looks like across the region.

Source: Company Website

+ MORRISON FOERSTER BOLSTERS MARKET-LEADING TECHNOLOGY AND TRANSACTIONS CREDENTIALS WITH TRIPLE PARTNER ADDITION INCLUDING MIKE PIERIDES, WILL HOLDER, AND OLIVER BELL

Morrison Foerster, a leading global law firm, is pleased to announce the addition of three partners, Mike Pierides, Will Holder, and Oliver Bell, adding significant strength and depth to the firm's global, cross‑border technology transactions credentials. Collectively, they bring over 55 years of experience bolstering the firm's capabilities in tech-focused M&A, complex technology transactions, outsourcing, and digital transformation.

Morrison Foerster's State and Local Government Task Force is pleased to provide our quarterly newsletter summarizing noteworthy developments from state attorneys general ("State AGs") across the country and local government agencies and legislative bodies, with links to primary sources. + GREENBERG TRAURIG, LLP [4 Press Releases]

+ GREENBERG TRAURIG ATTORNEYS RECOGNIZED ON 2026 VIRGINIA SUPER LAWYERS 'RISING STARS LIST

McLEAN, Va. - April 28, 2026 - Three attorneys from global law firm Greenberg Traurig, LLP were selected for inclusion on the 2026 Virginia Super Lawyers "Rising Stars" list, recognizing outstanding young lawyers across a range of practice areas.

Super Lawyers evaluates attorneys statewide across more than 70 practice areas. The "Rising Stars" designation is awarded to no more than 2.5% of lawyers practicing in each state who demonstrate outstanding professional achievement and peer recognition early in their careers. Candidates are identified through a rigorous selection process that combines peer nominations with independent research and evaluation.

Source: Company Website

+ GREENBERG TRAURIG, LLP WHICH LAW FIRMS ARE LEADING IN THE FINANCIAL SECTOR?

Greenberg Traurig is featured in a Latin Lawyer's LACCA article titled "Which Law Firms are Leading in the Financial Sector?"

Source: Company Website

+ GREENBERG TRAURIG TOKYO HOSTS ANREV BRIEFING ON REAL ASSETS, DATA CENTRES AND AI

TOKYO - April 30, 2026 - Greenberg Traurig, LLP's Tokyo office recently hosted and sponsored a briefing for the Asian Association for Investors in Non‑Listed Real Estate Vehicles (ANREV), bringing together institutional investors and industry professionals to discuss emerging trends in real assets, data centres, and artificial intelligence.

The program featured a panel discussion titled "Real Assets & New Technologies: Data Centres & AI Opportunities," with Greenberg Traurig Shareholder Joel H. Rothstein, chair of the firm's Asia Real Estate Practice, among the speakers. Panelists discussed how advances in AI are reshaping capital allocation, infrastructure planning, and investment strategies across global real asset markets, with a particular focus on the growing demand for data centre assets throughout the Asia‑Pacific region.

Source: Company Website

+ GREENBERG TRAURIG, LLP WHO REPRESENTS LATIN AMERICA'S BIGGEST BANKS 2025: THE FULL LIST

Greenberg Traurig is featured in a Latin Lawyer's LACCA article titled "Who Represents Latin America's Biggest Banks 2025: The Full List."

+ GOODWIN PLC [4 Press Releases]

+ GOODWIN GUIDES PHARMAKON ADVISORS ON $250 MILLION DEBT FACILITY WITH ZENAS BIOPHARMA

The Intellectual Property team advised Pharmakon Advisors on entering into a five-year, up to $250 million senior secured debt facility with Zenas BioPharma, who are developing a bifunctional monoclonal antibody drug product for the treatment of IgG4-related disease (IgG4-RD).

Source: Company Website

+ GOODWIN GUIDES HUMANS& IN RAISING OVER $500 MILLION AT $4.5 BILLION VALUATION IN SEED ROUND

The Technology team guided humans& in raising over $500 million in a seed financing round, which values the company at approximately $4.5 billion. The round was led by SV Angel and co-founder Georges Harik, while Nvidia, Jeff Bezos, and Alphabet's VC arm, GV, among other venture capital investors, also participated.

Source : Company Website

+ GOODWIN GUIDES EVANTIC CAPITAL IN PARTICIPATION IN SYNTHESIAS $200 MILLION SERIES E

The Technology team advised Evantic Capital in its participation in the $200 million Series E funding round for Synthesia, the AI video platform transforming how organizations create and share knowledge.

Source: Company Website

+ GOODWIN PROCTER LLP FLOW NEUROSCIENCE ANNOUNCES FDA APPROVAL OF WORLDS FIRST AT-HOME BRAIN STIMULATION TREATMENT FOR DEPRESSION

Goodwin congratulates Flow Neuroscience on receiving FDA approval of their breakthrough at-home brain-stimulation device for the treatment of major depressive disorder (MDD). FDA approval marks the first time clinicians in the US are able to prescribe an at-home non-drug therapy as both a stand-alone and an adjunctive treatment for adults with moderate to severe depression. Goodwin's Technology and Life Sciences teams were thrilled to assist Flow Neuroscience in bringing this breakthrough device to patients in the US

Source: Company Website

+ ORRICK, HERRINGTON & SUTCLIFFE LLP AXELERA AI SECURES OVER $250M IN RECORD EU AI SEMICONDUCTOR INVESTMENT

Axelera AI, an Edge AI hardware startup, has raised more than $250 million in its most recent funding round.

Orrick advised Axelera AI on the transaction.

THE COMPANY Axelera AI is a European semiconductor leader developing advanced AI acceleration hardware for edge computing and data centre applications, driving the next generation of artificial intelligence across Europe.

Source : Company Website

+ AGATHA KLUK JOINS ORRICKS NEW YORK TECH COMPANIES GROUP

Leading tech companies advisor Agatha Kluk joins Orrick as a partner in New York. Agatha is a sought-after counselor to founders, operators, board members and venture investors in the NY tech ecosystem. She advises clients throughout the lifecycle - from formation to venture financing to exit - in the consumer tech, AI, health and wellness, fintech and cleantech sectors. Agatha joins from Perkins Coie, which in 2022 combined with Kluk Farber Law, the 12-lawyer venture law boutique she founded. Agatha began her legal career at Debevoise & Plimpton.

Source : Company Website

+ ORRICK, HERRINGTON & SUTCLIFFE LLPREALTEK SECURES KEY ANTITRUST WIN IN SMART TV CHIP DISPUTE

Semiconductor company Realtek Semiconductor Corp. secured a key win in its dispute over a "bounty" patent licensing agreement by competitors that included a $1 million incentive to sue Realtek with the District Court of the Northern District of California denying the defendants' early motion for summary judgment. urn:contentItem:6JHY-RN03-RXPP-F169-00000-00PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP - INDUSTRY NEWS RECAP2026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:02ZLexisNexisSTOCKPILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP - INDUSTRY NEWS RECAP

DASHBOARD: PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP

PermID4296383614Websitewww.pillsburylaw.com/en/IndustryFull-Service BigLawAddress31 West 52nd Street, New York, NY 10019 USA

ACTIVITIES:

Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP, founded in 1868, is a global law firm headquartered in New York City. With over 700 lawyers across 20 offices, it serves clients in energy, technology, and finance, specializing in M&A, regulatory, and litigation. Generating over $700 million annually, Pillsbury advised on 2024 renewable energy transactions, recognized by Columbia Law School. Its culture emphasizes innovation, diversity, and pro bono work, with strong associate training programs. Competing with Baker McKenzie, Pillsbury stands out for its energy and tech regulatory expertise. In 2024, it expanded its AI and ESG practices, focusing on sustainable finance. Recent initiatives include cloud-based legal tech and partnerships with nonprofits like Lawyers Alliance for New York, reinforcing its community impact.

+ PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP [3 Press Releases]

+ PILLSBURY OPENS IN BOSTON WITH A FOCUS ON TECHNOLOGY, LIFE SCIENCES AND FINANCIAL INDUSTRIES

Pillsbury is opening a new office in Boston, marking an important expansion into one of the nation's leading growth and innovation centers. The office will launch with a team of five senior attorneys and additional associates, who bring diverse transactional and litigation backgrounds.

Source: Company Website

+ PILLSBURYS MARQUEE INSURANCE RECOVERY & ADVISORY PRACTICE CONTINUES EXPANSION, WELCOMING RENOWNED POLICYHOLDER LITIGATOR BOB GILBERT

Robert J. Gilbert, a nationally recognized litigator who represents policyholders in complex insurance recovery matters, has joined Pillsbury from Latham & Watkins. He comes to the firm as one of the founding partners of Pillsbury's newly established Boston office.

Source: Company Website

+ AS NOVEL DIGITAL RIGHTS ISSUES COME TO THE FORE, PILLSBURY ADDS IP LITIGATOR ROBERT L. URIARTE IN LOS ANGELES

Pillsbury has further enhanced its expansive tech-focused capabilities with the addition of Intellectual Property litigator and trial lawyer Robert L. Uriarte as a partner in its Los Angeles office.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ PERKINS COIE [3 Press Releases]

+ PERKINS COIE REPRESENTS SEDRON IN INVESTMENT FROM ARA PARTNERS OF UP TO $500 MILLION

PORTLAND, OR (April 15, 2026)-Perkins Coie announced today that it represented Sedron Technologies, a leader in advanced upcycling systems that transform waste streams into reusable water and fertilizer products, in an investment of up to $500 million from Ara Partners, a global private equity and infrastructure firm focused on industrial decarbonization.

Source: Company Website

+ PERKINS COIE STRENGTHENS PACIFIC NORTHWEST TRIAL BENCH WITH COMMERCIAL LITIGATOR CHAD COLTON

PORTLAND, OR and SEATTLE, WA (April 6, 2026)-Perkins Coie is pleased to announce that Chad Colton has joined its Commercial Litigation practice as a partner in Portland and Seattle, further strengthening the firm's trial capabilities in the Pacific Northwest.

Bringing two decades of experience, Chad represents clients in high-stakes commercial matters such as partnership, shareholder, breach of contract, IP, and licensing disputes. He also frequently serves as lead trial counsel for bet-the-company cases, often joining to advise and advocate for clients in the middle of litigation or immediately before trial.

Source: Company Website

+ PERKINS COIE RANKS 44TH ON FORTUNE'S "100 BEST COMPANIES TO WORK FOR®" LIST FOR 2026

SEATTLE (April 1, 2026)-Perkins Coie is proud to announce that it has again been named to Fortune's "100 Best Companies to Work For®" list for 2026, marking the 24th consecutive year that the firm has been featured on Fortune's prestigious list.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ COOLEY LLP [4 Press Releases]

+ COOLEY LLP VETHOS ANNOUNCES $200 MILLION IPO

Cooley advised Ethos Technologies, a top life insurance technology company on a mission to democratize access to life insurance, on its $200 million initial public offering (IPO). Ethos offered 5,127,696 shares of Class A common stock, and selling stockholders offered an additional 5,398,619 shares of Class A common stock, at a price of $19 per share. Ethos began trading on the Nasdaq Global Select Market on January 29, 2026, under the ticker symbol LIFE.

Partner Matthew Bartus led the Cooley team advising Ethos since its formation in 2016, including on its $100 million Series D-1 in July 2021 and $200 million Series D in May 2021.

Lawyers Jon Avina, Jean Park, Milson Yu, Samara Zaifman, Katie Lapidus and Charlotte Yin led the Cooley team advising Ethos on its IPO.

Source: Company Website

+ COOLEY LLP INFERACT ANNOUNCES $150 MILLION SEED FINANCING

Cooley advised Inferact, a startup founded by creators and core maintainers of the open-source project vLLM, on its $150 million seed financing at an $800 million valuation. The financing was co-led by Andreessen Horowitz and Lightspeed Venture Partners, with investments from Sequoia Capital, Altimeter Capital, Redpoint Ventures, ZhenFund, The House Fund, Striker Venture Partners, Laude Ventures, Databricks Ventures and the UC Berkeley Chancellor's Fund.

Lawyers Peter Werner, Tara Capsuto, Won Lee, Carl Min and Isabella Ysebrands led the Cooley team advising Inferact.

Source: Company Website

+ COOLEY LLP OPENEVIDENCE RAISES $250 MILLION SERIES D

Cooley advised OpenEvidence, the most widely used artificial intelligence (AI) platform by doctors in America, on its $250 million Series D financing, valuing the company at $12 billion and making it the most valuable healthcare AI company in the world. The new funding will be used to invest heavily in the research and development and compute costs associated with the multi-AI agentic architecture of OpenEvidence, which provides the highest quality and most accurate medical answers of any system in the world.

The round was co-led by Thrive Capital and DST Global, and brings the total funding raised by OpenEvidence to nearly $700 million over the past year.

Lawyers Derek Colla, David Safren and Sela Dougherty led the Cooley team advising OpenEvidence.

Source: Company Website

+ COOLEY LLP NEUROPHOS SECURES $110 MILLION SERIES A

Cooley advised Neurophos, a leader in photonic artificial intelligence chip technology, on its oversubscribed $110 million Series A round, bringing total funding to $118 million. The new funding will accelerate the delivery of Neurophos' first integrated photonic compute system, including datacenter-ready optical processing unit modules, a full software stack and early access developer hardware.

The round was led by Gates Frontier, with participation from M12 (Microsoft's venture fund), Carbon Direct Capital, Aramco Ventures, Bosch Ventures, Tectonic Ventures, Space Capital and others.

Lawyers James Allen, Nick Hobson, James Reizman and Nathan Alamillo led the Cooley team advising Neurophos.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ K&L GATES [3 Press Releases]

+ TWO K&L GATES PARTNERS AGAIN NAMED TO INDIA BUSINESS LAW JOURNALS INTERNATIONAL A-LIST 2026

K&L Gates is pleased to announce that Singapore Office Managing Partner Raja Bose and Singapore Corporate partner Meraj Noor have once again been named to India Business Law Journal's (IBLJ) International A-List. This marks the eighth consecutive year both partners have received this recognition.

IBLJ's International A-List identifies the top lawyers based outside of India who are recognized for their work on India-related matters. The A-List was compiled by IBLJ's seasoned editorial team following extensive market research, drawing on feedback from hundreds of lawyers at Indian law firms and thousands of India-focused in-house counsel within India and abroad.

The full International A-List 2026 is featured on IBLJ's website.

**All Singapore lawyers are members of K&L Gates Straits Law LLC, the Singapore office of global law firm K&L Gates. Source: Company Website

+ MORE THAN 80 K&L GATES LAWYERS ACROSS ASIA-PACIFIC RECOGNISED IN 2027 BEST LAWYERS, ONES TO WATCH EDITIONS

Best Lawyers®, a peer-review publication in the legal profession, has named more than 80 K&L Gates lawyers across the Asia-Pacific region in the 2027 editions of its guides, covering Australia, Japan, and Singapore. In addition, nearly 20 of the firm's lawyers were named as Best Lawyers: Ones to Watch® in Australia and Japan, lists that recognise lawyers early in their careers.

Alongside these recognitions, four Australian partners were recognised with "Lawyer of the Year" awards, an accolade that is particularly noteworthy, as it is only bestowed on one standout lawyer for each practice area in each jurisdiction.

The Best Lawyers® guides highlight top legal talent, selected by peers within the same geographical region and legal practice area, through millions of votes each year. Congratulations to the Lawyer of the Year award recipients below, as well as all lawyers recognised in the 2027 editions - resulting in nearly 180 total recognitions for the firm. Source: Company Website

+ K&L GATES ADVISES DOLPHIN ENTERTAINMENT, INC. ON US$15 MILLION PIPE INVESTMENT

Global law firm K&L Gates LLP advised Dolphin Entertainment, Inc., an entertainment marketing and premium content production company, in connection with a US$15 million private investment in public equity (PIPE) financing with Lincoln Park Capital, an investment group and asset management firm.

The Corporate team advising on the transaction was led by Miami partner Clayton Parker with support from Miami partner Erin Fogarty.

"This transaction underscores Dolphin Entertainment's commitment to accelerating strategic growth across its marketing and content-driven businesses. We were pleased to support them in securing investment from a partner aligned with its long-term vision," said Parker.

K&L Gates' Corporate practice is one of the most substantial in the legal industry, with hundreds of lawyers in offices across the globe providing clients with practical legal solutions in the structuring, financing, and completion of domestic, international, and cross-border transactions. Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ REED SMITH LLP [4 Press Releases]

+ REED SMITH ADVISES NATURPAK ON SALE BY TRIVEST TO PPC

CHICAGO - Global law firm Reed Smith represented Trivest Partners and IPMF, LLC, doing business as NaturPak, a manufacturer of bone broths, soups, sauces, wet pet food, and food ingredients for national retailers and brands, on its sale by Trivest Growth Investment Fund II to Pritzker Private Capital ("PPC"). Trivest Growth Investment Fund II, managed by Trivest Partners LP, a private equity firm focused exclusively on investments in founder- and family owned businesses, partnered with NaturPak through a minority investment in 2020. During Trivest's partnership, NaturPak transformed from a small specialty co-manufacturer into a scaled, multi-segment platform, increasing its R&D and manufacturing capabilities and production capacity.

The cross-office Reed Smith deal team included attorneys in Chicago, New York, Denver, Philadelphia, Dallas, Orange County (California), Pittsburgh, Washington, Houston, Tysons (Virginia), and Miami. The team was led by Nick Gibson, Kristen Capriotti, Crockett Stevenson, and Sudeep Kalkunte, with support from Constantine Karides, Brad Schmarak, David Hayes, Simon Kliegman, Ian Sherwin, Paulo McKeeby, Daniel Hagedorn, Michelle Mantine, Cori Goldberg, A.J. Wissinger, Jillian Burstein, Tyler Thompson, Mike Summers, Michael Jacobs, Brendan Leanos, Benjamin Brimeyer, Michael Galibois, Michael Lowell, Joseph Mammone Jr., Michelle DiPuma, Batool Banker, Leah Hungerman, Taber Rueter, Clirae Bourke, Michaela Mapes, Peter Loy, Talia Boiangin Behar, Elliott Race, George Avdellas, Katherine Litaker, Taylor Kollmansberger, and Nikita Bazaj.

Source: Company Website

+ REED SMITH PARTNERS JOSH LOVE, ERIC MARDER, STEPHEN SESSA, AND EDWARD SHAPIRO NAMED TO VARIETY'S "DEALMAKERS 2025" REPORT

LOS ANGELES - Global law firm Reed Smith announced today that Josh Love, Eric Marder, Stephen Sessa, and Edward Shapiro, partners in the firm's global Entertainment & Media Industry Group, have been named to Variety's "Dealmakers 2025" report. Love, Marder, Sessa, and Shapiro are among an elite group of entertainment attorneys who have been chosen based on their more than $3 billion in music transactions in 2025, high‑profile client roster, and leadership across the music industry. The publication highlights their work on "sports and music transactions, start-ups and brands," "recording, music publishing, and live performance agreements (including Bad Bunny's upcoming Super Bowl halftime show)," and "catalog acquisitions."

This recognition reflects Reed Smith's continued leadership in high-stakes entertainment and music transactions, talent representation, and emerging legal issues at the intersection of entertainment, music, technology, and finance. The four partners were selected for their high-profile talent negotiations and representation business.

Source: Company Website

+ REED SMITH ADVISES LEON CAPITAL ON STRATEGIC INVESTMENT IN AVANTIS GROUP

LONDON - Global law firm Reed Smith has advised funds advised by Leon Capital on the strategic investment in Avantis Group, a UK-based provider of specialist engineering services to the maritime and energy industries. The transaction, which represents the latest new investment by Leon Capital, completed on 23 December 2025. The terms of the investment are confidential.

The transaction involved the Development Bank of Wales and Avantis Group's founders, Chris David and Tom David. Avantis Group's existing management team will retain operational control of the business. Blake Morgan acted as legal counsel to the Development Bank of Wales, while Acuity Law advised the founders. AMA Capital Partners acted as corporate finance adviser to Avantis Group.

Reed Smith advised Leon Capital on all aspects of the transaction, providing comprehensive M&A support in connection with the strategic investment. The matter involved a multi-disciplinary team spanning M&A, tax, and regulatory considerations.

The investment supports Avantis Group's long-term growth strategy across maritime and energy markets, including expansion into digital infrastructure and defence, and reflects Leon Capital's confidence in the company's differentiated engineering platform and experienced management team.

For Reed Smith, the transaction represents a further example of the firm's work advising private capital sponsors on complex M&A transactions and continues to strengthen its relationship with Leon Capital as it expands its platform.

The Reed Smith team advising on the matter was led by Andrew Houghton, with support from Ciaran Sweeney, Georgia Moorhouse and Selina Cook on M&A matters; Caspar Fox and Tom Baxter on tax; and Emma Weeden on FDI and antitrust.

Source: Company Website

+ REED SMITH STRENGTHENS ASIA-PACIFIC FINANCE AND TRANSPORTATION CAPABILITY WITH HIRE IN SINGAPORE

SINGAPORE - Global law firm Reed Smith announced the appointment of banking and finance partner Dr. Gregory Xu to its Singapore office. Xu, who joins from Stephenson Harwood, where he led the general banking and project finance offering in Southeast Asia and served on the firm's Supervisory Council, its executive committee, will join Reed Smith's Transportation Industry Group.

With over 15 years of experience, Xu advises on high-value, cross-border transactions across a wide range of sectors, including transportation - shipping, aviation, and logistics - trade, life sciences, energy, and technology. He regularly acts for financial institutions, funds, export credit agencies, leasing houses, asset owners, and Fortune 500 companies on complex financing, restructuring, and enforcement matters.

Xu has a strong track record in green and sustainability-linked lending and advises on multi-jurisdictional insolvency-related enforcement and restructuring matters. He also brings extensive experience in general corporate and commercial matters.

Richard Hakes, chair of Reed Smith's Transportation Industry Group, said: "Gregory is an outstanding addition to our team, bringing deep knowledge of ship finance, aviation finance, and banking throughout Southeast Asia. His arrival further strengthens our capabilities in Singapore and reinforces our commitment to the maritime, aviation, and broader transportation sectors across the Asia-Pacific region and beyond. Working closely with our shipping teams in Hong Kong and Shanghai and our aviation team in Singapore as part of our global Transportation Industry Group, Gregory will enhance our ability to provide seamless, cross-border support to clients operating in one of the world's most dynamic industries."

Xu commented: "Reed Smith's global platform, sector focus, and collaborative culture are hugely impressive. The firm's market-leading finance practice and its long-standing reputation in general banking, asset finance, and project finance were major draws for me. I'm delighted to be joining such a highly collegiate team with a clear strategy for growth in the region and look forward to contributing to the continued expansion of the firm's finance offering across Asia-Pacific."

Praj Samant, Reed Smith's Asia-Pacific managing partner, said: "We are delighted to welcome Gregory to the Singapore team. His arrival further strengthens our offering in one of the region's most strategically important sectors. We look forward to the valuable contributions he will bring to the firm locally and globally."

Xu is recognized as a leading practitioner by multiple legal directories. Chambers Asia-Pacific ranks him as a "Leading Individual" for Banking & Finance (India), while The Legal 500 identifies him as a "Leading Partner," "Next Generation Partner," and "Recommended" lawyer across Banking & Finance, Asset Finance, Projects, and Shipping in Singapore and in the foreign juris

McLEAN, Va. - April 28, 2026 - Three attorneys from global law firm Greenberg Traurig, LLP were selected for inclusion on the 2026 Virginia Super Lawyers "Rising Stars" list, recognizing outstanding young lawyers across a range of practice areas.

Super Lawyers evaluates attorneys statewide across more than 70 practice areas. The "Rising Stars" designation is awarded to no more than 2.5% of lawyers practicing in each state who demonstrate outstanding professional achievement and peer recognition early in their careers. + GREENBERG TRAURIG TOKYO HOSTS ANREV BRIEFING ON REAL ASSETS, DATA CENTRES AND AI

TOKYO - April 30, 2026 - Greenberg Traurig, LLP's Tokyo office recently hosted and sponsored a briefing for the Asian Association for Investors in Non‑Listed Real Estate Vehicles (ANREV), bringing together institutional investors and industry professionals to discuss emerging trends in real assets, data centres, and artificial intelligence.

The program featured a panel discussion titled "Real Assets & New Technologies: Data Centres & AI Opportunities," with Greenberg Traurig Shareholder Joel H. Rothstein, chair of the firm's Asia Real Estate Practice, among the speakers. Panelists discussed how advances in AI are reshaping capital allocation, infrastructure planning, and investment strategies across global real asset markets, with a particular focus on the growing demand for data centre assets throughout the Asia‑Pacific region.

+ GOODWIN PROCTER LLP FLOW NEUROSCIENCE ANNOUNCES FDA APPROVAL OF WORLDS FIRST AT-HOME BRAIN STIMULATION TREATMENT FOR DEPRESSION
