Ares Management (NYSE:ARES) has announced an agreement to acquire Whitestone REIT in a transaction valued at approximately US$1.7 billion, effectively taking the retail-focused real estate investment trust private. This strategic move is aimed at expanding Ares' portfolio in necessity-based retail centers located in rapidly growing metropolitan areas across the United States.The announcement, made on 14th April 2026, also included significant leadership changes within Ares Management's Asia operations. E.G. Morse has been appointed as Partner and Head of Asia Credit, while Dinesh Goel and Gabriel Fong will lead the Asia Special Situations team. These appointments reflect Ares' commitment to enhancing its credit and special situations strategies in the region, aligning with the company's broader growth objectives.The acquisition of Whitestone REIT is particularly noteworthy as it allows Ares Management to bolster its position in the retail real estate sector, catering to essential consumer needs. Investors monitoring NYSE:ARES should consider how this acquisition fits into Ares' overall strategy, which increasingly emphasizes private credit, real assets, and special situations. The move underscores Ares' intent to diversify its capital allocation across various geographies and asset types.Key considerations for investors include the integration of Whitestone into Ares’ existing operational framework, as well as the execution of fundraising initiatives in Asia. The new leadership team is expected to play a critical role in determining how capital is deployed and managed across regions. Nonetheless, investors should be mindful of potential risks, including profit margin compression, high levels of debt, and the current dividend yield of 5.38%, which is not fully supported by earnings.As Ares Management embarks on this significant acquisition and leadership restructuring, the implications for its future growth trajectory will be closely watched. The market's response to these developments could provide insights into how effectively Ares can leverage its new assets and leadership to enhance shareholder value over time.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ QATAR AIRWAYS [3 News That Matters]

+ QATAR AIRWAYS RESUMES CARGO SERVICES TO IRAQ WITH ENHANCED CAPACITY

Qatar Airways Cargo has resumed freighter and belly-hold operations to Iraq, according to its latest published schedule, marking the return of cargo connectivity between Doha and Baghdad. This development follows the airline's phased restoration of services, with cargo sales now open for shipments on the Doha to Baghdad route.Effective 7th May 2026, the carrier will introduce a weekly Boeing 777 freighter service to Baghdad. This will be complemented by the launch of twice-weekly passenger flights on the same route starting from 10th May, thereby adding belly-hold cargo capacity alongside dedicated freighter operations. With both freighter and passenger services in place, Qatar Airways will offer a combined cargo capacity of over 115 tonnes from Doha to Baghdad beginning 10th May.The new addition is expected to significantly enhance cargo flows between the two markets, leveraging a mix of scheduled freighter uplift and passenger aircraft capacity. Qatar Airways Cargo reported that its freighter network has expanded to more than 60 destinations as of 1st May. The passenger network is also set to grow further, reaching over 150 destinations from 16th June as the airline continues to scale up operations.All cargo products are now available for booking through standard sales channels, including the Digital Lounge and local Qatar Airways Cargo sales teams. The airline confirmed that capacity is open for customer bookings, and those with shipment-specific inquiries are encouraged to reach out to cargohelp@qatarairways.com.qa or their local sales representatives.The resumption of services to Baghdad forms part of Qatar Airways Cargo ’s broader network strategy, which combines freighter operations with passenger aircraft capacity to support global cargo demand. The airline operates a fleet of 30 Boeing 777 freighters and more than 230 passenger aircraft, supported by a road feeder network, while continuing to invest in digitalization, products, and infrastructure as part of its operational strategy.

+ QATAR AIRWAYS MARKS RETURN TO ERBIL WITH RESUMPTION OF FLIGHTS

Qatar Airways will resume passenger flights to Erbil (EBL) on 10th May 2026, marking the return of direct air connectivity between the Kurdistan Region and Doha as part of a wider reinstatement of its Iraq operations, the airline announced Saturday.The Doha-based carrier stated that it will restart services to three Iraqi cities — Erbil, Baghdad (BGW), and Basra (BSR) — in a phased return aimed at rebuilding its regional network and restoring connectivity across key Middle Eastern destinations.In parallel with passenger services, Qatar Airways will also resume freighter operations to Baghdad starting 7th May, reinforcing cargo and trade links with Iraq. The airline noted that these resumptions follow recent reinstatements of flights to several regional destinations, including Dubai, Sharjah, Bahrain, Damascus, and Kozhikode, as part of a wider strategy to restore its global network.By mid-June 2026, Qatar Airways expects to serve more than 150 destinations worldwide. Passengers traveling through Erbil International Airport (EBL) will gain renewed access to Qatar Airways’ global network via its Doha hub, Hamad International Airport, which the airline describes as a key connector linking six continents.The carrier, which has been repeatedly recognized as the 'World’s Best Airline' by Skytrax, emphasized that its return to Iraq reflects ongoing efforts to restore stability to its route network while maintaining flexibility in response to regional conditions. The resumption of passenger flights to Erbil follows the suspension of services in late February 2026 due to the outbreak of the US–Israel–Iran war, which triggered widespread airspace closures across Iraq and the wider Middle East.

+ QATAR AIRWAYS EXPANDS FLIGHT NETWORK, BOOSTING AUSTRALIA'S TOURISM POTENTIAL

Australia’s tourism industry is set to receive a major boost as Qatar Airways announces significant increases in its flights between Doha and key Australian cities. As of 5th May 2026, the expanded services come at a crucial time for the Australian travel market, helping to reconnect Australia with major international tourism sources in Europe, Asia, and the Middle East. With the airline resuming daily flights and adding more options for travelers, this move is expected to foster a strong recovery for Australia’s tourism sector after a period of disruption.Beginning in May 2026, Qatar Airways will reinstate daily flights from Doha to Sydney, Melbourne, and Perth. These routes, which were previously limited to around five flights a week, will now operate daily, ensuring better accessibility for international travelers looking to explore Australia’s top destinations. Additionally, the airline will launch four weekly flights to Brisbane starting 16th May, which will increase to daily flights by 16th June 2026, giving more tourists the opportunity to visit Queensland’s attractions.This flight expansion reflects Qatar Airways’ ongoing commitment to strengthening global connectivity, and the airline is capitalizing on its strategic hub in Doha. By offering these additional flights, Qatar Airways makes it easier for travelers from Europe, the Middle East, and Asia to plan direct routes to Australia, supporting the country’s tourism recovery post-pandemic. The increased connectivity will not only enhance tourism but also improve trade and business opportunities between the two nations.Australia’s travel industry stands to benefit significantly from this expansion. The extra flights are expected to provide an influx of international visitors, boosting demand for accommodations, tours, and local attractions. Major cities like Sydney, Melbourne, and Perth, which already attract millions of tourists annually, are likely to see a further rise in visitors, especially as the summer months approach. For Australia’s tourism bodies, the expanded flights mean more opportunities to market the country as an accessible, diverse, and attractive destination.However, it’s essential to acknowledge that travel advisories continue to affect certain routes. According to Australia’s Smart Traveller service, there remains an active “Do Not Travel” advisory for Qatar due to ongoing regional security concerns. This caution applies to all travelers, even those transiting through Doha’s Hamad International Airport. Despite these advisories, the increased flight frequencies demonstrate Qatar Airways’ confidence in the safety and operational efficiency of its services. Looking ahead, the partnership between Qatar Airways and Australia is expected to flourish, positioning the tourism sector for long-term growth.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

value.Title: Soloviev Group and Bank of America Announce $1.8 Billion Refinance of 9 West 57th Street, The World's Premier Office Towers

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHM-XSP3-RSD0-G3PC-00000-00Soloviev Group and Bank of America Announce $1.8 Billion Refinance of 9 West 57th Street, The World's Premier Office Towers2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:02ZLexisNexisSoloviev Group and Bank of America Announce $1.8 Billion Refinance of 9 West 57th Street, The World's Premier Office TowersNEW YORK, May 7, 2026

PR NewswireSoloviev's strategic financing execution secured an interest rate of 4.97%, further enhancing the Class A property's long-term performanceNEW YORK, May 7, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- Soloviev Group, a leader and pioneer in commercial and residential development for more than five decades, together with lead lender Bank of America announce the successful $1.8 billion refinancing of its flagship tower, 9 West 57th Street, according to Chairman Stefan Soloviev.The transaction reflects the Class A building's exceptional quality, strong tenant demand, and continued outperformance at the top of the market. As part of the execution, Soloviev Group hedged the index, significantly reducing the effective interest rate and further enhancing 9 West 57th Street's long-term performance. Upon stabilization, the 50-story, 1.7 million square foot tower is expected to achieve a market valuation of $3.9 billion.Stefan Soloviev, Chairman of Soloviev Group said: "This refinancing is a clear validation of the strength of 9 West 57th Street and the collaborative success of lead lender Bank of America, and secondary leads Wells Fargo and Citibank."9 West 57th Street continues to set the standard for trophy assets, combining iconic architecture, sweeping Central Park views, and an unparalleled tenant experience.

Stefan Soloviev recently announced a 10-year lease averaging $340.00 per rentable square foot, the highest rate ever achieved for the renowned building and what we believe to be the highest rent ever recorded in the New York City commercial marketplace.Over the last several years, 9 West 57th Street has attracted the strongest roster of commercial tenants in New York City, most recently welcoming HBeyond, The U.S. Soccer Federation, Hess Group, Beaconlight Capital, and Platinum Equity. The property's distinguished list of tenants also includes long-term large footprint occupiers Apollo Global Management, CHANEL, Loews Corporation, Qatar Investment Authority, Coatue, Veritas and Standard Industries. Additionally, recent additions of Mousse Partners, Davidson Kempner, Tikehau Capital, Jain Capital, PointState Capital, and Panco Operations. The soaring skyscraper recently completed major upgrades to the lobby, elevator modernization, and building system technology. Additionally, as part of its renewed commitment to bolstering the tenant experience, 9 West 57th Street introduced new amenities including a 20,000 square foot amenity floor, offering Central Park views, modern conferencing, multi-functional meeting space, a grab and go coffee bar, executive dining, and a hospitality area.ABOUT SOLOVIEV GROUP
Soloviev Group brings successful development across community-focused verticals such as logistics, railroads, agriculture, grain storage, prime beef production, renewable energy, commercial and residential development, hospitality, and philanthropy. For more information, visitsolovievgroup.com. urn:contentItem:6JHM-XJ53-RSYN-92RP-00000-00PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP - RECENT INSIGHTS: SECTOR TRENDS & NEWS DEVELOPMENTS2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:02ZLexisNexisSTOCKPILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP - RECENT INSIGHTS: SECTOR TRENDS & NEWS DEVELOPMENTS

DASHBOARD: PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP

PermID4296383614Websitewww.pillsburylaw.com/en/IndustryFull-Service BigLawAddress31 West 52nd Street, New York, NY 10019 USA

ACTIVITIES:

Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP, founded in 1868, is a global law firm headquartered in New York City. With over 700 lawyers across 20 offices, it serves clients in energy, technology, and finance, specializing in M&A, regulatory, and litigation. Generating over $700 million annually, Pillsbury advised on 2024 renewable energy transactions, recognized by Columbia Law School. Its culture emphasizes innovation, diversity, and pro bono work, with strong associate training programs. Competing with Baker McKenzie, Pillsbury stands out for its energy and tech regulatory expertise. In 2024, it expanded its AI and ESG practices, focusing on sustainable finance. Recent initiatives include cloud-based legal tech and partnerships with nonprofits like Lawyers Alliance for New York, reinforcing its community impact.

+ PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP [3 Press Releases]

+ PILLSBURY OPENS IN BOSTON WITH A FOCUS ON TECHNOLOGY, LIFE SCIENCES AND FINANCIAL INDUSTRIES

Pillsbury is opening a new office in Boston, marking an important expansion into one of the nation's leading growth and innovation centers. The office will launch with a team of five senior attorneys and additional associates, who bring diverse transactional and litigation backgrounds.

Source: Company Website

+ PILLSBURYS MARQUEE INSURANCE RECOVERY & ADVISORY PRACTICE CONTINUES EXPANSION, WELCOMING RENOWNED POLICYHOLDER LITIGATOR BOB GILBERT

Robert J. Gilbert, a nationally recognized litigator who represents policyholders in complex insurance recovery matters, has joined Pillsbury from Latham & Watkins. He comes to the firm as one of the founding partners of Pillsbury's newly established Boston office.

Source: Company Website

+ AS NOVEL DIGITAL RIGHTS ISSUES COME TO THE FORE, PILLSBURY ADDS IP LITIGATOR ROBERT L. URIARTE IN LOS ANGELES

Pillsbury has further enhanced its expansive tech-focused capabilities with the addition of Intellectual Property litigator and trial lawyer Robert L. Uriarte as a partner in its Los Angeles office.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ PERKINS COIE [3 Press Releases]

+ PERKINS COIE REPRESENTS SEDRON IN INVESTMENT FROM ARA PARTNERS OF UP TO $500 MILLION

PORTLAND, OR (April 15, 2026)-Perkins Coie announced today that it represented Sedron Technologies, a leader in advanced upcycling systems that transform waste streams into reusable water and fertilizer products, in an investment of up to $500 million from Ara Partners, a global private equity and infrastructure firm focused on industrial decarbonization.

Source: Company Website

+ PERKINS COIE STRENGTHENS PACIFIC NORTHWEST TRIAL BENCH WITH COMMERCIAL LITIGATOR CHAD COLTON

PORTLAND, OR and SEATTLE, WA (April 6, 2026)-Perkins Coie is pleased to announce that Chad Colton has joined its Commercial Litigation practice as a partner in Portland and Seattle, further strengthening the firm's trial capabilities in the Pacific Northwest.

Bringing two decades of experience, Chad represents clients in high-stakes commercial matters such as partnership, shareholder, breach of contract, IP, and licensing disputes. He also frequently serves as lead trial counsel for bet-the-company cases, often joining to advise and advocate for clients in the middle of litigation or immediately before trial.

Source: Company Website

+ PERKINS COIE RANKS 44TH ON FORTUNE'S "100 BEST COMPANIES TO WORK FOR®" LIST FOR 2026

SEATTLE (April 1, 2026)-Perkins Coie is proud to announce that it has again been named to Fortune's "100 Best Companies to Work For®" list for 2026, marking the 24th consecutive year that the firm has been featured on Fortune's prestigious list.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ COOLEY LLP [4 Press Releases]

+ COOLEY LLP VETHOS ANNOUNCES $200 MILLION IPO

Cooley advised Ethos Technologies, a top life insurance technology company on a mission to democratize access to life insurance, on its $200 million initial public offering (IPO). Ethos offered 5,127,696 shares of Class A common stock, and selling stockholders offered an additional 5,398,619 shares of Class A common stock, at a price of $19 per share. Ethos began trading on the Nasdaq Global Select Market on January 29, 2026, under the ticker symbol LIFE.

Partner Matthew Bartus led the Cooley team advising Ethos since its formation in 2016, including on its $100 million Series D-1 in July 2021 and $200 million Series D in May 2021.

Lawyers Jon Avina, Jean Park, Milson Yu, Samara Zaifman, Katie Lapidus and Charlotte Yin led the Cooley team advising Ethos on its IPO.

Source: Company Website

+ COOLEY LLP INFERACT ANNOUNCES $150 MILLION SEED FINANCING

Cooley advised Inferact, a startup founded by creators and core maintainers of the open-source project vLLM, on its $150 million seed financing at an $800 million valuation. The financing was co-led by Andreessen Horowitz and Lightspeed Venture Partners, with investments from Sequoia Capital, Altimeter Capital, Redpoint Ventures, ZhenFund, The House Fund, Striker Venture Partners, Laude Ventures, Databricks Ventures and the UC Berkeley Chancellor's Fund.

Lawyers Peter Werner, Tara Capsuto, Won Lee, Carl Min and Isabella Ysebrands led the Cooley team advising Inferact.

Source: Company Website

+ COOLEY LLP OPENEVIDENCE RAISES $250 MILLION SERIES D

Cooley advised OpenEvidence, the most widely used artificial intelligence (AI) platform by doctors in America, on its $250 million Series D financing, valuing the company at $12 billion and making it the most valuable healthcare AI company in the world. The new funding will be used to invest heavily in the research and development and compute costs associated with the multi-AI agentic architecture of OpenEvidence, which provides the highest quality and most accurate medical answers of any system in the world.

The round was co-led by Thrive Capital and DST Global, and brings the total funding raised by OpenEvidence to nearly $700 million over the past year.

Lawyers Derek Colla, David Safren and Sela Dougherty led the Cooley team advising OpenEvidence.

Source: Company Website

+ COOLEY LLP NEUROPHOS SECURES $110 MILLION SERIES A

Cooley advised Neurophos, a leader in photonic artificial intelligence chip technology, on its oversubscribed $110 million Series A round, bringing total funding to $118 million. The new funding will accelerate the delivery of Neurophos' first integrated photonic compute system, including datacenter-ready optical processing unit modules, a full software stack and early access developer hardware.

The round was led by Gates Frontier, with participation from M12 (Microsoft's venture fund), Carbon Direct Capital, Aramco Ventures, Bosch Ventures, Tectonic Ventures, Space Capital and others.

Lawyers James Allen, Nick Hobson, James Reizman and Nathan Alamillo led the Cooley team advising Neurophos.

Source: Company Website

+ K&L GATES [3 Press Releases]

+ TWO K&L GATES PARTNERS AGAIN NAMED TO INDIA BUSINESS LAW JOURNALS INTERNATIONAL A-LIST 2026

K&L Gates is pleased to announce that Singapore Office Managing Partner Raja Bose and Singapore Corporate partner Meraj Noor have once again been named to India Business Law Journal's (IBLJ) International A-List. This marks the eighth consecutive year both partners have received this recognition.

IBLJ's International A-List identifies the top lawyers based outside of India who are recognized for their work on India-related matters. The A-List was compiled by IBLJ's seasoned editorial team following extensive market research, drawing on feedback from hundreds of lawyers at Indian law firms and thousands of India-focused in-house counsel within India and abroad.

The full International A-List 2026 is featured on IBLJ's website.

**All Singapore lawyers are members of K&L Gates Straits Law LLC, the Singapore office of global law firm K&L Gates. Source: Company Website

+ MORE THAN 80 K&L GATES LAWYERS ACROSS ASIA-PACIFIC RECOGNISED IN 2027 BEST LAWYERS, ONES TO WATCH EDITIONS

Best Lawyers®, a peer-review publication in the legal profession, has named more than 80 K&L Gates lawyers across the Asia-Pacific region in the 2027 editions of its guides, covering Australia, Japan, and Singapore. In addition, nearly 20 of the firm's lawyers were named as Best Lawyers: Ones to Watch® in Australia and Japan, lists that recognise lawyers early in their careers.

Alongside these recognitions, four Australian partners were recognised with "Lawyer of the Year" awards, an accolade that is particularly noteworthy, as it is only bestowed on one standout lawyer for each practice area in each jurisdiction.

The Best Lawyers® guides highlight top legal talent, selected by peers within the same geographical region and legal practice area, through millions of votes each year. Congratulations to the Lawyer of the Year award recipients below, as well as all lawyers recognised in the 2027 editions - resulting in nearly 180 total recognitions for the firm. Source: Company Website

+ K&L GATES ADVISES DOLPHIN ENTERTAINMENT, INC. ON US$15 MILLION PIPE INVESTMENT

Global law firm K&L Gates LLP advised Dolphin Entertainment, Inc., an entertainment marketing and premium content production company, in connection with a US$15 million private investment in public equity (PIPE) financing with Lincoln Park Capital, an investment group and asset management firm.

The Corporate team advising on the transaction was led by Miami partner Clayton Parker with support from Miami partner Erin Fogarty.

"This transaction underscores Dolphin Entertainment's commitment to accelerating strategic growth across its marketing and content-driven businesses. We were pleased to support them in securing investment from a partner aligned with its long-term vision," said Parker.

K&L Gates' Corporate practice is one of the most substantial in the legal industry, with hundreds of lawyers in offices across the globe providing clients with practical legal solutions in the structuring, financing, and completion of domestic, international, and cross-border transactions. Source: Company Website

+ REED SMITH LLP [4 Press Releases]

+ REED SMITH ADVISES NATURPAK ON SALE BY TRIVEST TO PPC

CHICAGO - Global law firm Reed Smith represented Trivest Partners and IPMF, LLC, doing business as NaturPak, a manufacturer of bone broths, soups, sauces, wet pet food, and food ingredients for national retailers and brands, on its sale by Trivest Growth Investment Fund II to Pritzker Private Capital ("PPC"). Trivest Growth Investment Fund II, managed by Trivest Partners LP, a private equity firm focused exclusively on investments in founder- and family owned businesses, partnered with NaturPak through a minority investment in 2020. During Trivest's partnership, NaturPak transformed from a small specialty co-manufacturer into a scaled, multi-segment platform, increasing its R&D and manufacturing capabilities and production capacity.

The cross-office Reed Smith deal team included attorneys in Chicago, New York, Denver, Philadelphia, Dallas, Orange County (California), Pittsburgh, Washington, Houston, Tysons (Virginia), and Miami. The team was led by Nick Gibson, Kristen Capriotti, Crockett Stevenson, and Sudeep Kalkunte, with support from Constantine Karides, Brad Schmarak, David Hayes, Simon Kliegman, Ian Sherwin, Paulo McKeeby, Daniel Hagedorn, Michelle Mantine, Cori Goldberg, A.J. Wissinger, Jillian Burstein, Tyler Thompson, Mike Summers, Michael Jacobs, Brendan Leanos, Benjamin Brimeyer, Michael Galibois, Michael Lowell, Joseph Mammone Jr., Michelle DiPuma, Batool Banker, Leah Hungerman, Taber Rueter, Clirae Bourke, Michaela Mapes, Peter Loy, Talia Boiangin Behar, Elliott Race, George Avdellas, Katherine Litaker, Taylor Kollmansberger, and Nikita Bazaj.

Source: Company Website

+ REED SMITH PARTNERS JOSH LOVE, ERIC MARDER, STEPHEN SESSA, AND EDWARD SHAPIRO NAMED TO VARIETY'S "DEALMAKERS 2025" REPORT

LOS ANGELES - Global law firm Reed Smith announced today that Josh Love, Eric Marder, Stephen Sessa, and Edward Shapiro, partners in the firm's global Entertainment & Media Industry Group, have been named to Variety's "Dealmakers 2025" report. Love, Marder, Sessa, and Shapiro are among an elite group of entertainment attorneys who have been chosen based on their more than $3 billion in music transactions in 2025, high‑profile client roster, and leadership across the music industry. The publication highlights their work on "sports and music transactions, start-ups and brands," "recording, music publishing, and live performance agreements (including Bad Bunny's upcoming Super Bowl halftime show)," and "catalog acquisitions."

This recognition reflects Reed Smith's continued leadership in high-stakes entertainment and music transactions, talent representation, and emerging legal issues at the intersection of entertainment, music, technology, and finance. The four partners were selected for their high-profile talent negotiations and representation business.

Source: Company Website

+ REED SMITH ADVISES LEON CAPITAL ON STRATEGIC INVESTMENT IN AVANTIS GROUP

LONDON - Global law firm Reed Smith has advised funds advised by Leon Capital on the strategic investment in Avantis Group, a UK-based provider of specialist engineering services to the maritime and energy industries. The transaction, which represents the latest new investment by Leon Capital, completed on 23 December 2025. The terms of the investment are confidential.

The transaction involved the Development Bank of Wales and Avantis Group's founders, Chris David and Tom David. Avantis Group's existing management team will retain operational control of the business. Blake Morgan acted as legal counsel to the Development Bank of Wales, while Acuity Law advised the founders. AMA Capital Partners acted as corporate finance adviser to Avantis Group.

Reed Smith advised Leon Capital on all aspects of the transaction, providing comprehensive M&A support in connection with the strategic investment. The matter involved a multi-disciplinary team spanning M&A, tax, and regulatory considerations.

The investment supports Avantis Group's long-term growth strategy across maritime and energy markets, including expansion into digital infrastructure and defence, and reflects Leon Capital's confidence in the company's differentiated engineering platform and experienced management team.

For Reed Smith, the transaction represents a further example of the firm's work advising private capital sponsors on complex M&A transactions and continues to strengthen its relationship with Leon Capital as it expands its platform.

The Reed Smith team advising on the matter was led by Andrew Houghton, with support from Ciaran Sweeney, Georgia Moorhouse and Selina Cook on M&A matters; Caspar Fox and Tom Baxter on tax; and Emma Weeden on FDI and antitrust.

Source: Company Website

+ REED SMITH STRENGTHENS ASIA-PACIFIC FINANCE AND TRANSPORTATION CAPABILITY WITH HIRE IN SINGAPORE

SINGAPORE - Global law firm Reed Smith announced the appointment of banking and finance partner Dr. Gregory Xu to its Singapore office. Xu, who joins from Stephenson Harwood, where he led the general banking and project finance offering in Southeast Asia and served on the firm's Supervisory Council, its executive committee, will join Reed Smith's Transportation Industry Group.

With over 15 years of experience, Xu advises on high-value, cross-border transactions across a wide range of sectors, including transportation - shipping, aviation, and logistics - trade, life sciences, energy, and technology. He regularly acts for financial institutions, funds, export credit agencies, leasing houses, asset owners, and Fortune 500 companies on complex financing, restructuring, and enforcement matters.

Xu has a strong track record in green and sustainability-linked lending and advises on multi-jurisdictional insolvency-related enforcement and restructuring matters. He also brings extensive experience in general corporate and commercial matters.

Richard Hakes, chair of Reed Smith's Transportation Industry Group, said: "Gregory is an outstanding addition to our team, bringing deep knowledge of ship finance, aviation finance, and banking throughout Southeast Asia. His arrival further strengthens our capabilities in Singapore and reinforces our commitment to the maritime, aviation, and broader transportation sectors across the Asia-Pacific region and beyond. Working closely with our shipping teams in Hong Kong and Shanghai and our aviation team in Singapore as part of our global Transportation Industry Group, Gregory will enhance our ability to provide seamless, cross-border support to clients operating in one of the world's most dynamic industries."

Xu commented: "Reed Smith's global platform, sector focus, and collaborative culture are hugely impressive. The firm's market-leading finance practice and its long-standing reputation in general banking, asset finance, and project finance were major draws for me. I'm delighted to be joining such a highly collegiate team with a clear strategy for growth in the region and look forward to contributing to the continued expansion of the firm's finance offering across Asia-Pacific."

Praj Samant, Reed Smith's Asia-Pacific managing partner, said: "We are delighted to welcome Gregory to the Singapore team. His arrival further strengthens our offering in one of the region's most strategically important sectors. We look forward to the valuable contributions he will bring to the firm locally and globally."

Xu is recognized as a leading practitioner by multiple legal directories. Chambers Asia-Pacific ranks him as a "Leading Individual" for Banking & Finance (India), while The Legal 500 identifies him as a "Leading Partner," "Next Generation Partner," and "Recommended" lawyer across Banking & Finance, Asset Finance, Projects, and Shipping in Singapore and in the foreign juris

Super Lawyers evaluates attorneys statewide across more than 70 practice areas. + GREENBERG TRAURIG TOKYO HOSTS ANREV BRIEFING ON REAL ASSETS, DATA CENTRES AND AI

TOKYO - April 30, 2026 - Greenberg Traurig, LLP's Tokyo office recently hosted and sponsored a briefing for the Asian Association for Investors in Non‑Listed Real Estate Vehicles (ANREV), bringing together institutional investors and industry professionals to discuss emerging trends in real assets, data centres, and artificial intelligence.

The program featured a panel discussion titled "Real Assets & New Technologies: Data Centres & AI Opportunities," with Greenberg Traurig Shareholder Joel H. Rothstein, chair of the firm's Asia Real Estate Practice, among the speakers. Panelists discussed how advances in AI are reshaping capital allocation, infrastructure planning, and investment strategies across global real asset markets, with a particular focus on the growing demand for data centre assets throughout the Asia‑Pacific region.

+ GOODWIN PROCTER LLP FLOW NEUROSCIENCE ANNOUNCES FDA APPROVAL OF WORLDS FIRST AT-HOME BRAIN STIMULATION TREATMENT FOR DEPRESSION

+ ORRICK, HERRINGTON & SUTCLIFFE LLP [3 Press Releases]

+ ORRICK, HERRINGTON & SUTCLIFFE LLP AXELERA AI SECURES OVER $250M IN RECORD EU AI SEMICONDUCTOR INVESTMENT

Axelera AI, an Edge AI hardware startup, has raised more than $250 million in its most recent funding round.

Orrick advised Axelera AI on the transaction.

THE COMPANY Axelera AI is a European semiconductor leader developing advanced AI acceleration hardware for edge computing and data centre applications, driving the next generation of artificial intelligence across Europe.

Source : Company Website

+ AGATHA KLUK JOINS ORRICKS NEW YORK TECH COMPANIES GROUP

Leading tech companies advisor Agatha Kluk joins Orrick as a partner in New York. Agatha is a sought-after counselor to founders, operators, board members and venture investors in the NY tech ecosystem. She advises clients throughout the lifecycle - from formation to venture financing to exit - in the consumer tech, AI, health and wellness, fintech and cleantech sectors. Agatha joins from Perkins Coie, which in 2022 combined with Kluk Farber Law, the 12-lawyer venture law boutique she founded. Agatha began her legal career at Debevoise & Plimpton.

Source : Company Website

+ ORRICK, HERRINGTON & SUTCLIFFE LLPREALTEK SECURES KEY ANTITRUST WIN IN SMART TV CHIP DISPUTE

Semiconductor company Realtek Semiconductor Corp. secured a key win in its dispute over a "bounty" patent licensing agreement by competitors that included a $1 million incentive to sue Realtek with the District Court of the Northern District of California denying the defendants' early motion for summary judgment. American Lawyer recognized the win in its Litigators of the Week column.

Source : Company Website

urn:contentItem:6JHR-Y0K3-RSK9-H45Y-00000-00PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP - RECENT INDUSTRY AND PEER NEWS ROUNDUP2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:02ZLexisNexisSTOCKPILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP - RECENT INDUSTRY AND PEER NEWS ROUNDUP

DASHBOARD: PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP

PermID4296383614Websitewww.pillsburylaw.com/en/IndustryFull-Service BigLawAddress31 West 52nd Street, New York, NY 10019 USA

ACTIVITIES:

Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP, founded in 1868, is a global law firm headquartered in New York City. With over 700 lawyers across 20 offices, it serves clients in energy, technology, and finance, specializing in M&A, regulatory, and litigation. Generating over $700 million annually, Pillsbury advised on 2024 renewable energy transactions, recognized by Columbia Law School. Its culture emphasizes innovation, diversity, and pro bono work, with strong associate training programs. Competing with Baker McKenzie, Pillsbury stands out for its energy and tech regulatory expertise. In 2024, it expanded its AI and ESG practices, focusing on sustainable finance. Recent initiatives include cloud-based legal tech and partnerships with nonprofits like Lawyers Alliance for New York, reinforcing its community impact.

+ PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP [1 News That Matters]

+ MORGAN LEWIS TAPS PILLSBURY TALENT TO DEEPEN BENEFITS PRACTICE

Morgan Lewis & Bockius LLP has added a prominent Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP attorney to its Employee Benefits team, reflecting a strategic response to rising complexity in benefits law.The attorney's background encompasses compliance and regulatory issues across employee benefits, with expectations to strengthen the firm's ability to provide comprehensive legal services.Arriving on 08th April 2026, the hire supports Morgan Lewis' ongoing plan to expand its benefits practice in a changing regulatory environment, while Pillsbury has seen departures to other firms.The move underscores the competitive talent market in corporate law and signals Morgan Lewis' commitment to maintaining state-of-the-art capabilities for its diverse client base.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ PERKINS COIE [3 News That Matters]

+ ASHURST AND PERKINS COIE LAUNCH GLOBAL FIRM ASHURST PERKINS COIE TO EXPAND CROSS-BORDER OFFERINGS

On 13th April 2026 the partnership vote to merge Ashurst and Perkins Coie into Ashurst Perkins Coie was approved, with a closing target in the third quarter subject to customary conditions.The leadership structure will feature Bill Malley and Paul Jenkins as global co-CEOs, while Karen Davies of Ashurst and Brian Eiting of Perkins Coie will serve as co-chairs.The merged group will comprise about 3,000 lawyers in 52 offices across 32 countries, with core hubs in Seattle, London, Sydney and New York and a focus on technology, energy, infrastructure and financial services.The combined revenues are around USD 2.8 billion, reflecting extensive cross-border experience across cities such as Brussels, Dubai, Frankfurt, Hong Kong, Paris, Seoul, Shenzhen, Singapore and Tokyo. The aim is to sharpen competitive edge and position the firm for ongoing growth in the global market.

+ GLOBAL LAW FIRMS PERKINS COIE AND ASHURST UNITE UNDER ASHURST PERKINS COIE

NEW YORK — Perkins Coie and Ashurst announced today that the partnerships of both firms have voted overwhelmingly to approve their proposed combination, reflecting strong support for a union that combines geographic reach with sector depth and practice strength. The date of this announcement is recorded as 2026-04-14 16:18:02.950901.Upon closing, Ashurst Perkins Coie will become a globally integrated firm with combined revenues of approximately US$2.8 billion and about 3,000 lawyers across more than 50 offices, including flagship hubs in Seattle, London, Sydney, and New York. The combined firm will be noted for its technology, energy and infrastructure, and financial services capabilities, aligned with the ongoing global economic transformation.Bill Malley, the Firmwide Managing Partner at Perkins Coie, remarked that the partnership's approval signals a belief that the merger will deliver a differentiated global platform with scale and leadership in technology to meet cross-border client needs. 'Our partnership's approval reflects a belief that combining to form Ashurst Perkins Coie will create a truly differentiated global legal platform.'Paul Jenkins, Global CEO of Ashurst, echoed the sentiment, emphasizing alignment between the firms and their joint future ambitions. 'This vote confirms our strong alignment and the compelling platform it creates for clients and talent alike,' he said. The parties noted that the merger will enhance cross-border service capability across key sectors.The combination remains subject to customary closing conditions and is expected to finalize in the third quarter of 2026.

+ PERKINS COIE REENTERS CHINA MARKET THROUGH SHENZHEN OFFICE

Perkins Coie has opened a Shenzhen representative office, signaling a renewed foothold in China. The office was inaugurated on Apr. 14, 2026. The move follows the 2024 closure of the firm's Beijing and Shanghai offices and reflects a strategic pivot toward Shenzhen to leverage the city's growing tech and manufacturing base in the Greater Bay Area. Bing Ai and Wei Yuan will lead the office as chief representative and managing partner and as representative and litigation partner, respectively; both are US-trained and bring deep experience advising technology clients. Ai focuses on patent procurement and portfolio management for semiconductors and medical devices, while Yuan handles patent infringement disputes. Bill Malley, Perkins Coie's managing partner, says the Shenzhen launch reinforces the firm's cross-border US legal capabilities and its aim to help clients navigate global markets.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ COOLEY LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ CROSBY GRABS $60 MILLION SERIES B BACKING

Crosby has raised $60 million in a Series B, a mark of its rapid fundraising that was announced on April 3, 2026.The round was led by Lux Capital and Index Ventures, with participation from Sequoia, 01 Advisors, Bain Capital Ventures and angel investor Elad Gil, with Cooley LLP listed among the backers.Funds will be used to expand Crosby’s technology and support a growing client base that includes Clay, Rogo and Ramp.The company blends artificial intelligence with legal know-how to speed contract reviews and says the total value of its negotiated contracts now exceeds $1 billion, up from about $30 million when it emerged from stealth last year. In a blog post, Crosby wrote, "As we’ve scaled, we’ve also started experimenting with entirely new technology experiences." The firm has introduced simulations of counterparty responses to redlines, voice agents that negotiate for clients, and a dashboard for clients to oversee Crosby’s work. Crosby was founded in 2024 by Ryan Daniels and John Sarihan, with Daniels a former Cooley associate and Sarihan a former Google software engineer and Ramp tech lead.

+ COOLEY WELCOMES FRESHFIELDS VETERANS TO BOLSTER TECHNOLOGY GROUP

Cooley LLP announced a substantial extension of its technology practice through the addition of several attorneys formerly with Freshfields Bruckhaus Deringer. The move is designed to enhance Cooley's tech capabilities and underscores the firm's aim to remain competitive amid a dynamic legal environment.As of 23rd November 2025, the recruitment drive underscores the firm's continued push to expand technology offerings in a rapidly evolving sector. The infusion of Freshfields Bruckhaus Deringer alumni is expected to strengthen the existing practice and draw new clients needing technology-focused legal counsel.The incoming team members come with extensive experience advising leading clients on IP, data protection, and corporate tech transactions. Their expertise is expected to augment Cooley's well-established venture capital and startup advisory services, further cementing the firm’s leadership in technology law.Cooley's decision to recruit from Freshfields reflects a broader market trend in the legal industry: firms actively pursue top talent to meet demands of a fast-moving, tech-centric market. As tech-related legal challenges grow more complex, the need for specialized knowledge becomes critical, making these hires timely.With this growth, Cooley aims to solidify its status as a preferred destination for technology clients, reinforcing its capabilities in an area essential for innovation and growth. As integration proceeds, observers will monitor the impact on Cooley's market position and client services in the coming months.

+ COOLEY LLP FACES A LEADERSHIP CHALLENGE AS LITIGATION PRACTICE EXPANDS

Cooley LLP's litigation practice is advancing into the spotlight as a finalist for The American Lawyer's General Litigation Department of the Year. The firm is reputed for excellence and is now negotiating what its partners describe as a 'test of leadership.' Led by Ian Shapiro, the team is addressing pivotal challenges in a rapidly evolving legal environment.A recent interview with Shapiro, joined by Michael Attanasio and Kathleen Hartnett, shed light on the firm’s strategic decision-making. They highlighted Cooley’s work representing Jenner & Block in its high-profile bid against the administration, underscoring the commitment to meaningful advocacy. The interview was published roughly 10 hours ago, offering a timely snapshot of the firm’s operations.In parallel with Jenner & Block matters, Cooley has taken on a range of pro bono matters that emphasize public service and social justice. This dedication has fueled growth for the firm over the last year. As the litigation group expands, Cooley intends to deploy its expertise to address complex legal challenges while upholding its core values.The expansion of Cooley’s litigation practice reflects a larger industry shift toward specialized services. This strategic move enhances Cooley's competitive standing and positions it for future opportunities. With the fiscal year ending soon, observers will monitor Cooley's next moves.During this unfolding 'test of leadership,' the legal community and investors will look to see how Cooley navigates these challenges. The firm’s proactive approach and commitment to excellence suggest it could emerge stronger in a crowded market.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ REED SMITH LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ REED SMITH BOLSTERS PRIVATE EQUITY PRACTICE IN LOS ANGELES WITH NEW PARTNER

Reed Smith has announced the addition of a private equity partner in its Los Angeles office, strengthening its capability in a market that remains pivotal for private capital flows.The new partner joins on 4th May 2026 and brings deep experience in private equity transactions and related advisory services, aligning with the firm's plan to broaden its private equity practice and serve investment firms and corporate clients involved in M&A.The appointment underscores Reed Smith's aim to provide strategic insights and tailored solutions as the private equity landscape evolves, supporting sophisticated investment structures and regulatory considerations.This strategic hire positions Reed Smith to pursue growth opportunities in private equity and continue delivering high-quality service and durable client relationships.

+ REED SMITH ANNOUNCES PHILADELPHIA OFFICE LEADER FROM WITHIN

ReedSmith LLP has tapped an internal attorney to lead its Philadelphia office, signaling a strategic shift in a key market. The new leader has more than a decade with the firm and has contributed to notable cases and regional growth, with focus on client relationships and innovative solutions. Effective from 27th April 2026, the appointment reinforces Reed Smith's plan to bolster its local presence and maintain continuity across leadership, while expanding capabilities in the Philadelphia market.

+ REED SMITH ESTABLISHES FIRST BOSTON MANAGING PARTNER POSITION TO DEEPEN MARKET PRESENCE

ReedSmith LLP announced the appointment of its first Managing Partner in Boston, a move designed to intensify the firm's regional focus. The appointment is effective from 23rd April 2026. The Boston leadership will oversee local operations, with a mandate to deepen client relationships and drive growth across sectors in the Northeast. The firm said the decision reflects the region's rising demand for specialized legal services and its readiness to reinforce its presence with a dedicated leadership role. With extensive experience and a broad network, the new Managing Partner is expected to accelerate operational efficiency and strengthen Reed Smith's reputation in Boston, positioning the office as a key driver of the firm's long-term strategy.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ WILSON SONSINI GOODRICH & ROSATI P.C. [3 News That Matters]

+ WILSON SONSINI NAVIGATES ARCELLX-GILEAD MERGER, HIGHLIGHTING BIOTECH CONSOLIDATION TREND

Wilson Sonsini Goodrich & Rosati P.C. advised Arcellx in its acquisition by Gilead Sciences, a transaction completed on 29th April 2026, reinforcing the firm’s position as a premier advisor in life sciences M&A. The firm served as lead legal adviser, drawing on extensive public-company M&A experience and a long-standing relationship with Arcellx that began before its IPO and included strategic collaborations such as Kite Pharma. With Gilead’s resources backing Arcellx, the merger is expected to accelerate development of its therapies. The Wilson Sonsini team—led by Robert T. Ishii, Ross J. Tanaka and Dan R. Koeppen—oversaw governance, regulatory and capital markets aspects to ensure a smooth close, reflecting ongoing industry consolidation.

+ WILSON SONSINI GUIDES AVOCA THROUGH SEED TO SERIES B FINANCING, VALUATION REACHES $1B

Wilson Sonsini Goodrich & Rosati P.C. advised Avoca as it raised over $125 million across Seed, Series A and Series B rounds, with the news announced on 27th April 2026 and valuing the company at about $1 billion. The Series B was led by Meritech and General Catalyst, while Kleiner Perkins participated in the Series A. Funds will support enhancements to Avoca's service automation tools, including 24/7 call handling, scheduling and targeted marketing campaigns, plus customer follow-ups. The advisors on the matter included Rob Broderick, Sam Obenhaus, Estefano Martinez and Ayush Sahni.

+ WILSON SONSINI WELCOMES NEW COO FROM GLOBAL FIRM TO DRIVE EFFICIENCY

Wilson Sonsini Goodrich & Rosati P.C. has appointed a Chief Operating Officer from Baker McKenzie to bolster operations and support growth. The new leader's name has not been disclosed.The firm announced the move on 22-04-2026, signaling a strategic shift to streamline processes and advance technology integration and client relationship management.Wilson Sonsini is known for its focus on technology and innovation, serving high growth companies and venture capital clients. The incoming COO's experience at Baker McKenzie in running operational initiatives is expected to align with the firm's objectives and support expansion into biotechnology, cybersecurity, and intellectual property sectors.Industry analysts view the change positively, suggesting it could strengthen strategic direction and execution. Investors will likely monitor the impact on the firm's competitive positioning and performance in the coming years.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ SIDLEY AUSTIN LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ SIDLEY AUSTIN GUIDES TELIX IN 50:50 CO-DEVELOPMENT AND CO-COMMERCIALISATION DEAL WITH REGENERON WORTH USD 2.1 BILLION

Telix Pharmaceuticals has enlisted Sidley Austin to guide its collaboration with Regeneron, a deal valued at up to USD 2.1 billion that covers the co-development and co-commercialisation of next-generation radiopharmaceutical therapies. The arrangement provides for a 50:50 split of costs and profits and includes an upfront USD 40 million payment for four initial programmes. Announced on 21st April 2026, the agreement is built to pool the companies' strengths in oncology and radiopharmaceuticals, aiming to move candidates through development more efficiently while sharing regulatory and governance responsibilities. Sidley partner Joshua Hofheimer noted that governance, regulatory matters, and intellectual property are central to these collaborations, stating that "These considerations affect governance, regulatory matters, and intellectual property, in particular." The alliance reflects a broader push in the biopharma space toward strategic collaborations designed to accelerate innovation in cancer care, a trend that law firms like Sidley are well-placed to support given the increasing complexity of cross-border deals.

+ RECRUITMENT PUSH DRIVES 33% GROWTH IN SIDLEY AUSTIN'S CITY OPERATIONS

Sidley Austin reported a 33% rise in City revenue, attributed to its hiring strategy as of 14th April 2026. Management credits a focused approach to identifying and integrating skilled professionals that expanded the City teams and diversified practice areas. The improvements in service have helped lift billable hours and client retention, reinforcing confidence in the hiring model. Analysts say the move demonstrates a willingness to invest in human capital to keep pace with a shifting legal market.

+ REGULATOR ALIGNS WITH TOP FIRMS AS CFTC CREATES INNOVATION TASK FORCE

The CFTC has formed an Innovation Task Force with Sidley Austin and Latham & Watkins to strengthen the regulatory framework for derivatives markets as technology reshapes the sector.The 11th April 2026 announcement notes the collaboration aims to incorporate insights from both firms to reflect evolving market realities, including digital assets and new trading technologies.Sidley Austin will provide regulatory guidance while Latham & Watkins contributes practical policy experience, helping the commission evaluate how tools like blockchain and AI intersect with oversight objectives.The task force is part of a wider effort to balance prudent supervision with fostering innovation, as the U.S. seeks to maintain leadership in global financial markets.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ GREENBERG TRAURIG, LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ GOLDEN ENTERTAINMENT BECOMES PRIVATE, FAMILY-OWNED IN TRANSACTION WITH GREENBERG TRAURIG

Greenberg Traurig advised Blake L. Sartini in moving Golden Entertainment to private family ownership, with the closing on 30th April 2026.Under the terms, Golden Entertainment shareholders received a cash dividend of $2.75 and 0.902 shares of VICI Properties Inc. for each share of Golden stock, providing liquidity while keeping an equity stake in the gaming and hospitality company.VICI Properties bought the real estate assets of seven Golden casino resorts, while a triple-net master lease was arranged with Sartini-controlled entities, enabling Golden to continue operating its full portfolio without disruption.Michael J. Bonner, Co-Managing Shareholder of Greenberg Traurig's Las Vegas office, remarked, "We were honored to represent Blake L. Sartini in returning Golden Entertainment to private family ownership. We congratulate Blake and the entire Golden Entertainment team!" The firm also highlighted its expertise across corporate transactions and real estate law, with a team including Dmitriy A. Tartakovskiy, Jim Mace, and James Doyle.

+ GREENBERG TRAURIG IN TOKYO CHARTS AI-DRIVEN SHIFTS IN GLOBAL REAL ASSET MARKETS

Greenberg Traurig, LLP's Tokyo office hosted a briefing for ANREV on Real Assets & New Technologies: Data Centres & AI Opportunities on the 30th of April, 2026. Panelist Joel H. Rothstein, who chairs the Asia Real Estate Practice, joined colleagues to discuss AI's effects on capital allocation, infrastructure planning, and investment strategies, with emphasis on growing data centre activity in the Asia-Pacific amid digitalization. Rothstein stressed the firm's commitment to industry dialogue and noted that the conversations reflect the convergence of digital infrastructure, real estate, and emerging technologies. The team draws on Greenberg Traurig's global platform to support cross-border investments in real assets. Greenberg Traurig operates about 3,100 lawyers across 51 locations and has been recognized for its real estate and infrastructure work, including a BTI Best of the Best honor in 2025 and listings on Am Law Global 100 and Law360 400.

+ LAW ROCKS LA: GREENBERG TRAURIG BAND THE MELTONES CLINCH TOP HONOR

On 29th April 2026, The Meltones, Greenberg Traurig, LLP’s house band, took top honors at Law Rocks Los Angeles, held at Whisky A Go Go in West Hollywood. The event raised more than $65,000 in donations and sponsorships, including $41,000 for The Headstrong Project.Over three nights, Law Rocks Los Angeles set a new fundraising benchmark with a total that surpassed $569,000, the highest in 15 years for the city. The Meltones opened with a Green Day cover, and victory was determined by audience support, ticket sales, and judges’ scores. GT was a Hall of Fame sponsor.The Meltones feature a cross-section of GT talent, with notable members such as Stephen L. Rabinowitz and Burt Bruton on guitar and Christopher A. Cernik on keyboard; vocalists include Michael R. Hogue, Ashley A. LeBlanc, and Tracy Newirth, with Tom Helf on drums and Daniel Blumberg on bass joining as guests. The band carries the legacy of co-founder Mel Greenberg and remains a core part of GT’s philanthropic ethos, which also includes a recent Law Rocks NYC win raising over $100,000 for The Door; the firm now employs about 3,100 lawyers across 51 locations.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ GOODWIN PROCTER LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ FBT GIBBONS TAPS GOODWIN PROCTER VETERAN TO STRENGTHEN VC CAPABILITIES

FBT Gibbons is expanding its capabilities in venture capital by hiring from Goodwin Procter LLP for its New Jersey office. On 21st April 2026, the firm confirmed the recruitment of a venture capital attorney. The attorney comes with extensive venture capital experience from Goodwin Procter LLP, a leader in the field, and the firm plans to leverage that expertise for clients seeking guidance on capital raises. Industry observers note the hire reflects a broader trend of law firms sharpening niche practices to compete in a growing market.

+ GOODWIN PROCTER ENABLES CLOSURE OF $328 MILLION CONSUMER-FOCUSED FUND FOR TOPSPIN

Goodwin Procter LLP helped close Topspin Consumer Partners' latest fundraising, securing a $328 million commitment for a consumer-focused fund. The deal underscores a growing trend in private equity toward consumer-oriented strategies, with Goodwin Procter providing crucial legal support throughout the process.On April 15, 2026, Topspin announced the fund’s closure, designed to pursue opportunities in the consumer sector. The firm’s counsel was instrumental in navigating fundraising requirements, maintaining regulatory compliance, and simplifying investor workflows.The fund intends to target a broad spectrum of opportunities across consumer industries, emphasizing segments with strong growth trajectories. With Goodwin Procter’s guidance, Topspin Consumer Partners aims to back innovative ventures well positioned to compete in a dynamic market.This partnership reinforces Goodwin Procter’s leadership in private equity work and its dedication to client success, with potential implications for investor confidence as the fund progresses.

+ GOODWIN PROCTER MOVES TOWARD AI-DRIVEN TRANSFORMATION ACROSS ITS LEGAL SERVICES

Goodwin Procter LLP is pursuing an AI-native strategy, expanding its technology footprint to boost efficiency in its private equity and technology practices. The move aligns with industry momentum toward AI-driven legal services.On 10th April 2026, the firm announced plans to integrate AI into its practice, aligning with a wider industry trend of AI adoption across BigLaw.AI tools are expected to enhance research, document review and analytics in case management, helping attorneys focus on higher level work.The move underscores the legal sector's shift toward innovation as clients demand faster and more capable service.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ ORRICK, HERRINGTON & SUTCLIFFE LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ ORRICK WELCOMES SENIOR USPTO LAWYER AS IP PRACTICE GROWS

Orrick has expanded its IP practice by hiring a senior attorney from the USPTO, aiming to enhance capabilities in patent matters as the firm grows in tech and telecom.The new attorney will join on the 28th of April, 2026, and brings extensive patent examination and litigation experience.Orrick's leadership described the hire as evidence of the firm's commitment to top-tier IP services and to navigating the evolving IP rights landscape in the digital age.With demand for technology-focused counsel rising, the firm expects this addition to support clients facing complex patent challenges and strengthen its competitive standing.

+ ORRICK EXPANDS ONLINE SAFETY CAPABILITIES WITH NICOLE LOPEZ

Orrick has added Nicole Lopez, a leading authority in youth online safety policy and litigation, to its team. Lopez previously held a significant position at Meta, and on Apr. 28, 2026, she expressed enthusiasm for the new role.Her hiring comes as 25 state legislatures weigh measures to safeguard minors online, a shift Kudon calls a watershed moment. "We are facing a watershed moment. Online safety is top of mind for innovators, government, teens and parents, and society at large," Kudon said.Aravind Swaminathan praised Lopez as a multifaceted asset—"part policy expert, part litigation and regulatory strategist, and part business advisor"—and highlighted the Online Safety Law Center as a hub for guiding clients through rapid changes in the digital space.

+ FORMER META YOUTH SAFETY POLICY LEAD JOINS ORRICK

On Apr. 23, 2026, Orrick announced the addition of the former Meta youth safety policy head to its team, a move designed to expand its digital safety and compliance capabilities.The new hire comes with a strong track record in policy design and implementation, particularly around privacy and the protection of young users online.Orrick frames the appointment as part of its proactive response to evolving regulatory requirements governing youth online safety.The firm expects the hire to strengthen its advisory services as tech companies face heightened scrutiny and regulatory complexity.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

Foley & Lardner LLP R. Lynn Parins

May 7th, 2026 ( JD Supra - Delivered by Newstex )

After nearly two decades of flat electricity demand, the U.S. power market has entered a new era of rising prices, growing volatility, and structural change. Wholesale electricity prices surged across the country in 2025, with year-on-year increases of 62% in New York, 60% in New England, and 45% in PJM, driven by higher natural gas prices, tightening capacity markets, and deepening grid constraints. [1] Retail prices rose more modestly-about 2.3% nationally-but the widening gap between wholesale volatility and retail pass-through is creating significant financial exposure for market participants on both sides of power purchase agreements.

These price movements are not cyclical blips. They reflect a fundamental shift in the supply-demand balance. U.S. electricity demand is rising at the fastest pace in a generation, fueled by data center buildouts, manufacturing reshoring, and electrification of transportation and buildings. [2] The country commissioned 54 GW of new utility-scale generation and storage in 2025-the most in over two decades-and invested a record $115 billion in grid expansion and reinforcement. [3] [4] Yet even that record-setting buildout has not been enough to ease pricing pressure in congested regions, particularly in the Northeast and Mid-Atlantic, where natural gas dependence amplifies weather-driven volatility.

The January 2026 winter storm underscored this vulnerability. Frigid temperatures and restricted natural gas supplies triggered widespread power plant outages across the Eastern U.S., sending prices spiking and raising fresh concerns about resource adequacy during peak demand periods. [5] These events are not hypothetical risks-they are the operating environment in which PPAs, capacity market positions, and project finance assumptions must perform.

The PPA Market Is Repricing Risk

For renewable energy developers and corporate offtakers, the most consequential trend is the repricing of risk in the PPA market. S&P Global reports that spreads between buyer and seller price expectations have widened significantly in key markets, as both sides reassess the economics of long-term contracting in a higher-price, higher-volatility environment. [6] Solar capture rates-the ratio of a project's average realized revenue to the market clearing price-are deteriorating as more solar capacity compresses midday prices, exposing projects to growing periods of zero or negative pricing.

This dynamic is driving several important structural shifts in PPA terms. Contract durations are trending shorter, reflecting buyer reluctance to lock in long-term price commitments amid uncertainty about future wholesale price trajectories. Downside protections, including floor prices and shape guarantees, are becoming more common as developers seek to de-risk revenue streams. [7] And battery energy storage is increasingly being integrated into PPA structures-both co-located and standalone-as a mechanism to shift generation into higher-value hours and mitigate capture rate risk. Standalone and co-located BESS deals are rising rapidly, with particularly strong growth in Texas, California, and the PJM footprint. [8]

Capacity Markets Under Pressure

The capacity market overlay adds another layer of complexity. PJM's capacity market-the largest in the country-has been a flashpoint for litigation and regulatory action, as stakeholders contest whether current market designs adequately compensate the resources needed to ensure reliability in a rapidly changing resource mix. The D.C. Circuit recently found that FERC erred in declining to consider Section 206 relief in the 2024/25 PJM capacity auction re-run case, a decision that could have significant implications for future auction design and pricing. [9]

At the state level, regulators are grappling with the affordability implications of wholesale price increases. The combined share of electricity and natural gas costs as a percentage of total household expenditure rose to 1.62% in 2025, a departure from the recent trend of declining energy cost burdens. [10] Several state commissions are placing greater emphasis on how utility programs align with affordability goals and grid reliability, and are scrutinizing whether rate designs adequately protect consumers from wholesale price pass-through. [11] Massachusetts provides a compelling case study: Governor Healey's March 2026 executive order establishing the '10X10X10 Plan'-targeting 10 GW of new energy resources over 10 years with $10 billion in projected customer savings-was driven explicitly by ISO New England's projection that electricity consumption could rise nearly 15% by 2035. [12]

What This Means for Market Participants

For corporate energy buyers, the current environment demands a more sophisticated approach to energy procurement. Companies that have relied on standard virtual PPAs may find that basis risk-the divergence between the contract settlement point and the buyer's actual load zone-has become a material financial exposure. Physical PPAs and behind-the-meter generation options warrant fresh evaluation, particularly for companies with concentrated load in high-price regions. The strategic case for energy risk management has never been stronger-energy should appear alongside cybersecurity, supply chain, and regulatory risk in enterprise risk management frameworks. [13]

For renewable developers, the key challenge is structuring projects and offtake agreements that can attract financing in a market where merchant tail risk is increasing and capture rates are compressing. Lenders and tax equity investors are asking harder questions about revenue assumptions and hedge coverage, and project finance underwriting is tightening in response to wholesale price volatility. [14] Developers who can pair generation with storage, offer shaped products, and demonstrate grid-positive attributes will be best positioned to compete for offtake and financing.

The throughline connecting these developments is straightforward: the repricing of wholesale power is simultaneously reshaping PPA economics, pressuring capacity market design, and tightening project finance underwriting-and these forces are reinforcing one another. [14] [15] Higher wholesale prices widen the gap between contracted and market revenues, which intensifies capture rate risk in PPAs, which in turn makes lenders demand more conservative debt sizing and hedge coverage, which raises the cost of capital for new projects at precisely the moment when record new capacity is needed to meet surging demand. [14] [7] [3] The market participants who navigate this cycle successfully will be those who treat PPA structuring, capacity market positioning, and financing strategy not as separate workstreams but as an integrated discipline-and who act on that recognition now rather than after the next price spike forces their hand.

urn:contentItem:6JHM-RY53-RVFD-P0TW-00000-00ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - KEY PEER AND SECTOR DEVELOPMENTS: RECENT UPDATES2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:53:19ZLexisNexisSTOCKROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - KEY PEER AND SECTOR DEVELOPMENTS: RECENT UPDATES

DASHBOARD: ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION

PermID4296528943Websitehttps://www.rockefellerfoundation.org/IndustryPhilanthropy / Global DevelopmentAddress420 Fifth Avenue, New York City, New York, U.S.

ACTIVITIES:

The Rockefeller Foundation, founded in 1913 by John D. Rockefeller, is a global philanthropy headquartered in New York City. With around 200 staff, it manages a $6 billion endowment, granting $300 million annually to advance health, equity, and sustainability. In 2024, it committed $100 million to climate resilience in African cities. Its culture emphasizes innovation, collaboration, and data-driven impact, with training in global policy and grantmaking. Competing with Ford Foundation, Rockefeller stands out for its pioneering role in public health and green energy, including founding the Global Energy Alliance for People and Planet. Recent initiatives include AI for social good and support for the National Museum of African American History, aligning with social justice trends. The foundations century-long legacy includes eradicating hookworm and funding the Green Revolution, shaping modern philanthropy.

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION [3 News That Matters]

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION COMMITS TO WORKFORCE RESILIENCE AMID AI DISRUPTION

On 28th April 2026, the Rockefeller Foundation announced a grant-based national initiative to address disruptions from automation, aiming to prevent job displacement and help workers transition into AI-complementary roles. The program prioritizes workforce interventions over product development, stressing program design, metrics and evaluation. It will measure displacement risk, deployment outcomes and labor-market transitions to understand how automation affects employment. Funding will come through grants, programmatic pilots and partnerships with employers, training providers and local governments. The choices made during implementation will be critical in determining whether the foundation's funding yields scalable reskilling pipelines or merely funds isolated projects. This initiative is a strategic philanthropic signal rather than a regulatory or corporate move, intended to catalyze innovative retraining models and public-private collaboration, complementing ongoing discussions about workforce policies such as sectoral training and apprenticeships. It is also expected to boost demand for measurable, data-driven evaluations within the skills and labor analytics ecosystem.

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION LAUNCHES $100 MILLION JOB INITIATIVE IN BALTIMORE AT BIG BETS FOR AMERICA

The Rockefeller Foundation hosted the 'Big Bets for America: Baltimore' event on 21st April 2026, bringing together more than 250 leaders from policy, philanthropy and business in Baltimore to discuss strategies for revitalizing the economy and expanding opportunity across communities.Key announcements included a three-year, $100 million initiative to connect workers with good jobs in 250 of America's most distressed communities, aimed at creating about 1.6 million additional jobs across sectors with strong growth potential, including healthcare, energy transition, food systems and artificial intelligence. Dr. Rajiv J. Shah, the Foundation's President, stressed restoring the promise of a stable life through hard work and resilience for American workers.The Foundation also unveiled a $12 million boost for Invest in Our Future to mobilize clean energy opportunities and spur growth in underserved communities. Maryland Governor Wes Moore participated and announced $1.5 million in philanthropic awards to improve education and childcare in areas with high childhood poverty. The event also introduced the second class of U.S. Big Bets Fellows, ten innovators expanding workforce pathways and unlocking capital in underserved areas, and highlighted cross-sector partnerships with organizations such as the Engine and the Los Angeles Cleantech Incubator. The next Big Bets for America gathering is set for Cleveland on 9th June 2026.

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION CALLS FOR UNIFIED DATA PLATFORM AT ONE HEALTH SUMMIT

On April 21st, 2026, Simon Winter, Vice President of the Rockefeller Foundation for Reimagining Humanitarian Nutrition Security, called for unified data at the One Health Summit. He warned that data remains siloed across humanitarian, nutritional and climate sectors, hindering timely decisions and resource allocation. He said, "We have more data than we’ve ever had in history and it’s increasingly becoming more affordable in its access, and yet it’s stuck in silos." To illustrate potential benefits, Winter highlighted the drought crisis in Somalia, where the foundation is funding a local tech incubator to create a food and nutrition systems dashboard that integrates multiple datasets. The tool is designed to provide timely information to local communities and international funders, including the Somali diaspora, which contributes about $2 billion annually to support farmers and pastoralists. He concluded by stressing that empowering local stakeholders with access to global data products, combined with local information, could lead to more effective and cost-efficient humanitarian responses.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ FORD FOUNDATION [3 News That Matters]

+ NHRC RECOGNIZES FORD FOUNDATION FOR SUSTAINED HUMAN RIGHTS SUPPORT IN NIGERIA

On Apr. 28, 2026, the National Human Rights Commission in Abuja awarded the Ford Foundation an Award of Appreciation for its sustained contributions to human rights in Nigeria. The honor was collected by ChiChi Aniagolu-Okoye, the Foundation's Regional Director for West Africa, during a gathering with NHRC senior staff and officials.NHRC Executive Secretary Dr. Tony Ojukwu said the Foundation has consistently supported efforts to widen access to justice and to boost the Commission's capacity. He noted that Nigeria faces complex rights challenges and that ongoing collaboration with development partners is essential to turning policy into practical improvements for citizens. The reception underscored the importance of continuing joint work, with gratitude also extended by Hilary Ogbonna, the Commission's Senior Human Rights Adviser, who highlighted milestones such as the MOVE project and the dashboard system that benefited from the Ford Foundation's early backing.

+ FORD FOUNDATION URGES ECOWAS FIRST LADIES TO TARGET GBV THROUGH SOFT POWER

The Ford Foundation has urged ECOWAS First Ladies to deploy their influence more strategically in tackling gender-based violence, describing the First Ladies’ soft power as a critical driver of social change and institutional accountability. The appeal was made by ChiChi Aniagolu, West Africa Regional Director of the Ford Foundation, during a goodwill message at the ECOWAS First Ladies Forum on Zero Tolerance for GBV, held in Banjul under the SWEDD+ platform in partnership with the World Bank and the United Nations Population Fund on 24th April 2026.Aniagolu warned that despite existing frameworks and commitments, gender-based violence remains widespread, with one in three women globally affected. In West and Central Africa, harmful practices such as child marriage persist, with over 30 percent of girls in some countries married before the age of 18. She also highlighted the economic burden of GBV, estimating losses of up to 3.7 percent of GDP in affected countries — figures that exceed investments in key sectors such as education.While the Ford Foundation acknowledged that First Ladies are not formal policymakers, it stressed their significant role in shaping public discourse and influencing decision-making across the continent. Aniagolu cited examples of impactful First Ladies, including Maryam Babangida, whose Better Life for Rural Women Programme elevated rural women's issues into national discussions, and Fatima Bio, whose Hands Off Our Girls campaign contributed to a national emergency declaration on rape in 2019. Such instances illustrate how soft power, when utilized effectively, can instigate systemic change.

+ FORD FOUNDATION LAUNCHES $60 MILLION DRIVE TO BOLSTER DEMOCRATIC VALUES

The Ford Foundation unveiled a $60 million initiative to strengthen democratic processes across the United States. The announcement, dated April 14, 2026, commits grants to nonpartisan, nonprofit organizations dedicated to protecting democracy and the rule of law. The funding will support a diverse array of groups across the ideological spectrum, working to boost civic participation and safeguard voting rights. Recipients include Pillars of the Community, Veterans for All Voters, All Voting is Local, Campaign Legal Center, and We the Veterans Military Foundation. The foundation notes that this investment builds on a decade-long Civic Engagement and Government program, which has provided more than $1 billion in grants to nonpartisan initiatives in the United States. President Heather Gerken framed the grants as strengthening democratic institutions, adding, "As we look ahead, it is an honor to deepen this commitment and support the remarkable work taking place around the United States to strengthen democracy and the rule of law at this urgent moment."

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
